name: Student Life
description:
Student Life is dedicated to the education and development of UCR's diverse student body and to creating a meaningful, learning-centered campus life for students at the University. Student Life works in active partnership with students, faculty, and staff colleagues to foster and support student opportunities for learning, involvement, leadership, community building, and creative expression that complement classroom experiences. Student Life further supports student organizations and their leaders, and they in turn provide programs and resources to the student body and the wider University community.

In the office of Student Life, staff work to support student organizations, Highlander Orientation, commuter programs, leadership development programs, veterans services, and first-year programs.

For student organizations, each "cluster" of organizations has a designated Student Life Advisor. This person provides resources and information to support student organization functions such as event planning, leadership development, and officer transition for student organization leaders. The Student Life Advisors also facilitate workshops, and assist students in navigating the UCR campus.

Here are the various clusters for student organizations!

  • Academic/Professional
  • Action & Awareness
  • Arts and Expression 
  • Cultural
  • Fraternities and Sororities
  • Graduate Student Associations
  • Honor Societies
  • Recreational
  • Service
  • Spiritual/Religious/Atheist

Other program areas in Student Life office include First Year Programs, Highlander Orientation, Commuter Programs, and Campus Activities. You can refer questions about these programs to ucrlife@ucr.edu.
summary: Student Life oversees all registered student organizations (including fraternities/sororities, honor societies, and Graduate Student Associations). Student Life also coordinates many other aspects of the student experience.

name: Highlander Gloves
description:
Newspaper Feature

Welcome to the official boxing club at UCR. We introduce our fellow UCR students the fundamentals of the sport of boxing in order to provide not only a great workout, but also an environment that will both cultivate and enhance positive qualities within each individual.

To learn more about us don't hesitate to message us or head over to our social media accounts @highlandergloves or join our club discord 

Facebook Page

Instagram Page

Club Discord
summary: We introduce the training, discipline, and fundamental techniques of boxing to our fellow students at UCR. We also compete at the national level at USIBA National Tournament annually.

name: acts2fellowship
description:
What is Acts2Fellowship?
acts2fellowship (a2f) is a Christian organization on campus. We meet every week to study the Bible, get equipped for the Great Commission, and build lasting friendships. At a2f, you will find a safe space to ask questions about life and seek answers with other students. Throughout the week, we hang out, study together, go on fun and memorable trips, and just do life together! So whether you are a Christian who wants to grow in your faith, or someone simply curious about what Christianity is all about, we welcome you to join us!

If you would like to get in contact with us, feel free to message us and/or fill out this form.
For more up-to-date information on upcoming events, check out the events page on our website.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=35nRGhicT4E
summary: Welcome to acts2fellowship,  where we explore meaningful questions about life. Whether you've been going to church your whole life or just curious to see what Christianity is all about, we invite you to come and experience our community!

name: Environmental Sciences mini-Graduate Student Association
description: We organize and plan social events, professional development, and networking/career building opportunities for the Environmental Sciences graduate students, including joint ventures with Environmental Engineering and Environmental Toxicology. We provide a unified voice of ES graduate students within the Graduate Student Association by enhancing interaction, inclusivity, equity, and communication among the interdiciplinary student body.
summary: To provide a unified voice of ES graduate students within the Graduate Student Association by enhancing interaction and communication amoung the interdiciplinary student body that makes up the ES department.

name: Future Business Leaders of America at UCR
description:
Meetings every Tuesday 6:30 - 7:30 PM

What makes us different? We challenge the standard in all areas: 

As the largest business organization in the nation, FBLA’s mission is to develop business leaders, however, within our chapter at UC Riverside, we strive to surpass that goal. We aspire to nurture leadership within all our members, empowering them to discover their true selves, value, potential, and confidence; irrespective of their major or intended career path. We believe that preparing for a successful career transcends hard skills and professional experience; it involves being attuned with oneself and understanding their desired personal and professional growth. Our approach involves fostering a positive and welcoming environment for creativity, hands-on experience, personal development, and professional opportunities. We stand out by embracing both modern and traditional business practices to develop leaders to exceed their full potential.

 

No matter what age you are, your major, or interests you hold, when it comes to reaching your dreams and aspirations, every person needs the skills our organization seeks to develop and fine-tune. For this reason, we open our doors to all majors, all class levels, and all outside the community who are passionate about growth. Join us for an exciting journey of growth and achievement!

 

Why PBL-FBLA? 

This organization takes a different approach to developing leaders. Leadership starts with understanding your purpose, strengthens with confidence, and actualizes with tangible accomplishments. As an organization, we realize that true leadership requires more than just professional skills and development. A key factor that many may overlook is the importance and value of personal development, which requires complete self awareness, elevating self confidence, overcoming self doubt, and believing in your potential. In this organization, we target growth in these two factors so that it can fuel ambition and create a hunger for taking all opportunities available to us.

How We Do It: 

Unique to us is our flat organizational structure, that allows every person to voice their ideas and be heard. Additionally, UCR PBL-FBLA is an environment that is safe space for creativity with like minded individuals who help push each other towards success. This structure and environment are crucial to our organization’s culture. Collectively as fellow students, we understand how draining and demoralizing the pursuit of our ambitions may be - that rejection, fear, and struggle may derail us from our big picture goals. As difficult as the journey may be, our organization offers the care and support that of a family, but also challenges each member to to ask themselves if their actions and goals align with their idea of success - whether that means living happily, supporting a family, getting a desired job title, or working for a dream company. 

What We Do: 

With everything that we do at UCR PBL-FBLA, our number one goal is to help our members grow, to help you be the best version of yourself, to find and refine the leader that is already within you, and to foster your dreams into real actions. We use our “why” to design projects that are customized to benefit you, and we do so by analyzing what each member needs to be a step closer to self actualization. Here are some of the many things we do:

  • Committees: Programming, Outreach, and Marketing 
    • An opportunity for our members to gain hands-on experience, working on projects and gaining hard skills they can utilize within their future careers.
  • Weekly General Meetings
    • Interactive workshops touching a variety of personal/professional topics, oriented to advance your growth and leadership.
  • State and National Conferences 
    • Compete in events against other chapters across the state including UCSD, UC Berkeley, and more.
  • Mentor Mentee Program
      • Join our family and pair up with an officer/returning member for valuable insight and mentorship.
  • Member Development Program
    • A way for busier members to still gain a personalized experience by developing their skills and finding their passions.
  • Workshops
    • Not your average workshops for Networking, Resume Critiques, Elevator Pitches, LinkedIn, and more!
  • Professional Networking Opportunities 
    • Quarterly Talk Shows with professionals from firms such as Goldman Sachs to share industry knowledge.
    • We partner with the School of Business, Career Center, and ASPB to create events like our Networking Dinner.

Values

  • Stride Towards Self-Actualization
      • To be self-actualized is to realize one’s true self: from their goals and desires to their traits and qualities. By doing so, one can gain their own sense of self and purpose. Here at PBL-FBLA, we gladly push each individual towards their own self-actualization, asking the hard, thought-provoking questions, reflecting on the difficult challenges, and allowing members to think critically about their mindsets and personal identities. 
  • Grow Together
      • When one hears the word “leader”, they tend to think of one individual. However, here at PBL-FBLA, every individual is a leader in their own right, pursuing their own dreams while helping and inspiring those around them. Regardless of experience level or major, PBL-FBLA is a place where everyone can grow and push their limits to become better versions of themselves. 
  • Produce Quality
      • PBL-FBLA and the Officer Team promises to ensure everything we do is up to quality standards in order to create a complete and enjoyable experience for our members. Furthermore, we promise to push our members to achieve quality within their own works and careers, providing motivation, asking the right questions, and providing them with any possible resources we can. 
  • Always Celebrate 
    • Whether it’s achieving goals or overcoming struggles, leaders acknowledge success and know when to celebrate them. This is why PBL-FBLA strives to not only teach professional skills, but emphasize the importance of genuine connection and fun amongst its members. From socials to retreats, there is always something fun planned to meet new people, strengthen bonds, and create an enjoyable college experience! 

Are you a Vendor looking to be sponsored on campus? Email us!
summary: Future Business Leaders of America at UCR - provides opportunities for students in all majors to develop skills needed for their future careers: communication, interpersonal, leadership, networking, public speaking, and resume building.

name: Bioengineering Mini-GSA
description: The BIG-GSA shall serve, represent and promote the interests and welfare of the Bioengineering graduate student community at UCR. It shall encourage community representation and involvement in relevant university decision-making and shall strive to enhance the general quality of life and education for all members. It shall foster healthy communication between the student body, faculty, administration, alumni, and the local community and beyond. The BIG-GSA shall also sponsor, support and be responsible for events open to the community.
summary: Bioengineering Mini-Graduate Student Association

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers at UCR (ASME)
description: ASME seeks to develop well-rounded engineers at UC Riverside that are experienced and ready for the real world. We are an organization that robots (among other things) out of a love for engineering and because we understand the need for competent engineers worldwide. Through the various projects we are involved in, we can gain invaluable engineering experience before moving into the workforce. They say, "It's not about what you know, but who you know." We have a growing network of talented students/engineers. Through our professional development, outreach, volunteering events, and workshops, this is your chance to meet experienced engineers, and other students, and to learn marketable skills.
summary: To help engineering students develop professionally, socially and academically through: participating yearly in Regional ASME competitions, coordinating events with other ASME student chapters, and sponsoring industry-recruiting events.

name: Engineers Without Borders at UCR
description: Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life. This partnership involves the implementation of sustainable engineering projects, while involving and training internationally responsible engineers and engineering students.
summary: Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life. This partnership involves the implementation of sustainable engineering projects.

name: American Association for Aerosol Research Student Chapter at UCR
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide opportunity for students and professionals in or out of UCR to exchange scientific ideas about aerosol and atmosphere research.
summary: AAAR Student Chapter fosters a community for which students can exchange scientific ideas about aerosol research.

name: Latinic Societas Unitas (LSU)
description:
Latinic Societas Unitas is a multicultural community service-based sorority at UCR. We are a small but fun-loving organization always willing to support and uplift its women! 

 

We carry encourage and stress a unity through sisterhood. We emphasize the importance of education and acknowledge its distinct necessity. We stress and practice the need for diversity. We provide moral and academic support. We engage in our community through service. We are proud to be LSU!
summary: We carry, encourage and stress a unity through sisterhood. We emphasize the importance of education and acknowledge its distinct necessity. We stress and practice the need for diversity. We provide moral and academic support.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science
description:
1. To further the work of, to improve the effectiveness of, and to enhance the public understanding of and appreciation for Chicanx, Latino, Native Americans, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians and other underrepresented minorities in science.

2. To cooperate with other individuals, organizations, clubs, and other groups whose purposes include the achievement of public understanding for Chicanx, Latino, Native Americans, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians and other underrepresented minorities in the sciences.

3. To promote student recruitment and retention of Chicanx, Latinx, Native Americans, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians and other underrepresented minorities in science at UC Riverside.

4. To provide a forum for students from different science majors and science related majors to come together for academic, community service and social activities at UC Riverside.
summary: We, the members of SACNAS at UCR, established to foster the success of Hispanic/Chicanx/Latinx and Native American scientists to attain advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership.

name: Nikkei Student Union
description:
Nikkei Student Union (NSU) is an socio-cultural organization devoted to promoting, preserving, and educating the surrounding community and college-aged youth about the history of Japanese and Japanese Americans through cultural events and social activities.

We host bi-weekly General Meetings where our NSU community gather to learn more about Japanese American Culture, participate in engaging games and icebreaker activities, as well as hear more about upcoming events open to General Members.

 

If you'd like to learn more about the club or keep up to date with our events, follow us @ucrnsu on Instagram.
Check our linktree for additional resources if you don't have/use Instagram
summary: Japanese-American Social and Cultural Club

name: Riverside Free Clinic at UCR
description:
At RFC, we strive to provide free interdisciplinary health and wellness care to the underserved population of the Inland Empire while also providing an effective training environment for future healthcare professionals and leaders dedicated to serving the Inland Empire. Our undergraduate volunteers work with medical students, various healthcare professionals from numerous programs & universities, and faculty from the UCR School of Medicine to provide basic healthcare to the underserved population of Riverside.

This group was formally known as the Student Run Health Clinic and is now known as the Riverside Free Clinic.

Applications come out in the Spring, so if you're interested, please visit our website in May!
summary: The Student Run Health Clinic began in 2005, growing from the collaborative concerns of students and doctors disturbed by the number of Riverside locals without basic healthcare.

name: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP)
description: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) is a diverse group of students, faculty, staff, and community members working towards educating the public on the plight of the Palestinian people, combating Zionism in all its forms, and promoting the liberation of the Palestine. We are organized on democratic principles to promote justice, human rights, liberation, and self-determination for the Palestinian people.
summary: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) is a diverse group of students, faculty, staff, and community members working towards promoting Palestinian Liberation.

name: Tau Beta Pi (The Engineering Honor Society)
description: Founded in 1885, Tau Beta Pi is the second oldest national honor society and the only honor society that represents all engineering disciplines. This prestigious honor society accepts members with distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering.
summary: Founded in 1885, Tau Beta Pi is the only honor society that represents all engineering disciplines. This prestigious honor society accepts members with distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering.

name: Men's Club Soccer at UCR
description:
To provide a competitive soccer team to students and college athletes, and to develop talented soccer players, skills, and abilities. We compete in the SoCal Premier Division where we play against other schools across Southern California. 

Follow our Instagram to keep up with IMPORTANT DATES & TRYOUTS!
summary: This club is for students still looking to play intercollegiate soccer without having to be consumed by the competitive soccer lifestyle that is D1.

name: Ultimate Frisbee Club at UCR
description:
Ultimate Frisbee Club at UCR

It’s not just for dogs.

 

Come On Out!

-We play every Tuesday and Thursday from 8-10pm at the Field next to lot 26.

-You haven’t played before!? No problem, the club is open to players of all skill levels, from brand new player, to UFA prodigy and everything in between.

-We are looking to get more people to learn and enjoy the game of Ultimate Frisbee, and improve our skills while we are at it.

 

What We Do.

-Practice/pickup games every Tuesday and Thursday from 8-10pm

-Enter tournaments and compete in leagues such as Lei-Out, St. Pat’s Hat, Trouble in Vegas, and Beach, Winter, and Summer Leagues.

-Also starting a USA Ultimate college team to compete in the official college division and play other schools such as Cal Poly Pomona, UC Davis, Cal, UC Irvine, etc. (some time in the future)

 

And Why!?

-It isn’t called Ultimate just so it sounds cool.  This game combines the stamina and running of soccer, the quick feet and agility of football, the defense and hops of basketball, and the smarts and foresight of baseball.  It truly is the Ultimate sport!

-We play to better ourselves as athletes and stay in shape.  We have fun doing it, and it is a great way to meet people at UCR!  Undergrads play, graduate students play, post-doctorates play, and even professors!

 

Contact Us!

-Follow our Instagram and join the linked group me to get notifications and updates.

-Find us and other clubs on Highlander Link: highlanderlink.ucr.edu  There you can officially join clubs, sign waivers, and see what events various clubs are hosting.
summary: We host pickup games every Tuesday and Thursday from 8-10pm at the UCR Sports Complex (the fields at Canyon Crest and Blaine).  Ultimate Frisbee is for everyone! We have players of all skill levels and if you haven't played before, we will teach you!

name: Student Homeless Aid Relief Project at UCR
description:
https://youtu.be/LMNHzebQcY4

To provide food, clothing and hygiene products for the homeless of the Riverside community while providing students the opportunity to partake in community service and encouraging them to take initiative in helping to meet the needs of the community.

Check out our Instagram @sharpucr for more information on our meeting dates!
summary: To provide food, clothing and hygiene products for the homeless of the Riverside community while providing students the opportunity to partake in community service and encouraging them to take initiative in helping to meet the needs of the community.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
description:
Who We Are

The Association of Latino Professional For America, ALPFA, is a student organization open to all majors and ethnic backgrounds. We are recognized nationally as an organization to empower and develop leaders in every sector of the economy.

As a chapter, our main goal is to prepare our members with the skills they’ll need in order to succeed in the business world. We provide insight on topics such as; resumes and cover letters, interview techniques and preparation, networking tips and follow-ups, Linked In, business etiquette, etc.

We also invite professionals from a wide range of companies to help you master those skills in a more personal environment. By talking to these individuals we hope to provide you the opportunity to build and grow your network.
summary: Our purpose is to promote the success by advocating professional development and fostering relationships with professionals and peers.

name: Graduate Student Association
description:
The Graduate Student Association exists to advance, through our advocacy and programs, the academic, social, and physical environment of current and future graduate students at the University of California, Riverside.

-----------------------------------------------------------

Land Acknowledgement: In honor of Rupert and Jeanette Costo’s founding relationship to our campus, we would like to respectfully acknowledge and recognize our responsibility to the original and current caretakers of this land, fire, water, and air: Íviatem (Cahuilla), Máara'yam (Serrano), Tóngva, and Payómkawichum (Luiseño) peoples, and all of their descendants past, present, and future. Today this meeting place is home to many Indigenous peoples from all over the world, including UCR faculty, students, and staff, and we are grateful to have the opportunity to live and work on these homelands.
summary: The Graduate Student Association exists to advance, through our advocacy and programs, the academic, social, and physical environment of current and future graduate students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: Highlander Statistics Society
description: The Highlander Statistics Society (HiSS) is a student organization whose purpose is to form unity and friendship among students majoring or interested in statistics. HiSS works to promote scholarship and interest in statistics while informing people about opportunities and challenges that are present in the field. Through social, academic, and service events, HiSS strives to build a strong community among its club members in addition to encouraging involvement and success in statistics.
summary: An organization bringing together students interested in Statistics for Learning and Fun.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description:
Preamble

The Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is a non-profit organization and is dedicated to serve the Chinese community in University of California, Riverside. 

Name of Organization

The name of this organization shall be Chinese Students and Scholars Association, hereinafter referred to as CSSA, as it will appear throughout the constitution. 

Purpose of Organization

The purposes of this organization shall be: 

  • To promote communication, friendship and academic exchange and to improve mutual understanding among its members.
  • To preserve and maintain cultural identity among Chinese community.

  • To help all Chinese International students who live on UCR Campus solve their difficulties.
summary: To Promote understanding and cooperation among Chinese students and scholars as well as with other groups in the university.

name: Sigma Pi Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description: Sigma Pi Alpha Sorority was founded on May 2nd, 1996 by nine women at the University of California, Berkeley. They found the need to create a close network of Latinas who wanted to make a difference in their community by beginning with themselves. Beta chapter, at UC Riverside has been making strives on campus since June 2001. Although we use Greek letters, we are not affiliated with the Greek system. The Greek letters have been selected by our founding mothers to symbolize our Chicana/Latina heritage by spelling Ella, meaning she/her in the Spanish language. The sorority is Chicana/Latina based but we do consider ourselves to be multicultural. We have three pillars that we pride ourselves on. They are campus involvement, community service, and sisterhood. Lastly, if you are interested in joining, we would like you to know that we do not rush! We have a motto that says “you choose us, we don’t choose you!”
summary: To create a close network of Latinas who want to make a difference in their community. We address the academic, cultural, communal and social needs of our members. We are Chicana/Latina based but we do consider ourselves to be multicultural.

name: Hillel at UCR
description: Hillel at UCR is a Jewish cultural, social, educational and religious organization. Hillel provides an opportunity for both Jewish and non-Jewish students, faculty and staff to explore Jewish identity within the university community.
summary: Hillel at UCR is a Jewish cultural, social, educational and religious organization. Hillel@UCR provides an opportunity for both Jewish and non-Jewish students, faculty and staff to explore Jewish identity within the university community.

name: Pakistani Student Association
description: This is a student group open to all undergraduate & graduate students, staff, and faculty. We seek to bring together those members of the community that share our interest in the social, cultural, and political milieu of Pakistan and Central & South Asia. The club was founded with hopes of fostering diversity and understanding amongst the campus. Follow us on our social media accounts for updates and events -- Instagram: @psa_ucr
summary: We are a student organization open to all undergraduate & graduate students, staff, and faculty. We seek to bring together those members of the community that share our interest in the social, cultural, and political milieu of Pakistan.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Association at UCR
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to represent the chemistry graduate students at UCR by promoting an interest in chemistry while providing a sense of unity throughout the department. This will include a series of social and academic events involving the graduate students as well as faculty, staff, and/or members of the community.
summary: Organization for chemistry graduate students to socialize, professionalize, and spread an interest in chemistry to the community.

name: Best Buddies at UCR
description:
Best Buddies was born in the fall of 1987, when Founder Anthony Kennedy Shriver observed the lack of opportunity for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) to socialize with their peers without IDD. He recognized the power of one-to-one friendship to develop their self-esteem, improve their outlook on life, and increase their integration in society. A college student himself at Georgetown University, Shriver knew that his peers had the energy and commitment to positively transform their communities. His inspiration to bring both groups together created the Best Buddies concept. 

The college program provides an opportunity for college students to be matched in a one-to-one friendship with individuals who have IDD. Social experiences and relationships are a part of life; unfortunately, individuals with IDD have historically been excluded from many of the social opportunities that most people enjoy. The Best Buddies College program works to expand these opportunities through community involvement and friendship, offering people with IDD the chance to explore life in a new way.

There are Best Buddies chapters like ours at other colleges and universities all over the country, all of which are part of Best Buddies International. As students, we are in a unique position to make a real change in our community by joining this growing movement of volunteers. You are not just committing to a club, but to a person.

There are 9.3 million people with intellectual disabilities in the U.S. who have traditionally been segregated from society. Each pair commits to keep in touch at least once a week through phone calls, emails, or seeing each other at school. Buddy Pairs get together on their time and socialize twice a month. They also agree to attend Best Buddies meetings, activities, and fundraisers.

Through participation in Best Buddies, people with and without disabilities are given the chance to explore new friendships and widen their social circles. Best Buddies helps us make a difference in someone else’s life and our own, and we become role models for the rest of our school.
summary: Best Buddies Colleges fosters one-to-one friendships between college students and adults with IDD and college students without IDD. Best Buddies is providing the opportunity for people with IDD to be involved in campus and community.

name: Sociology Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the SGSA is to serve as a resource and aid for the successful development of graduate students within the sociology department at UCR. SGSA seeks to accomplish this mission by making students aware of the different facets of the sociological discipline and through active involvement in the educational experience of students in our department. By bringing together individuals from different backgrounds, experiences and values, who share a commitment to serve their society, SGSA serves as a learning experience that challenges students to continuously respond to a basic underlying question: How can we as individuals and scholars contribute to a better understanding of our society?
summary: The mission of the SGSA is to serve as a resource and aid for the successful development of graduate students within the sociology department at UCR.

name: Sigma Kappa Sorority
description: Consistent with our Founders’ thoughts in 1874, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our values of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our organization. We encourage an atmosphere which will foster high ideals of friendship among college women, promote their educational and cultural interests, create in them a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them high qualities of character.
summary: The women of Sigma Kappa strive to trust and empower our sisterhood to achieve success in unity, learning, and involvement. We exercise the rights of good citizens and the support of worthwhile civic, social, and philanthropic projects.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: Pre-Dental Society at UCR (est. 2010) offers dental exposure and leadership/mentorship opportunities for undergraduate students who are passionate about dentistry. Students will have the opportunity to go on dental school tours, volunteer at the Riverside Free Clinic for hands-on dental experience, join our mentorship program, learn more about the dental school application process, and participate in other volunteer opportunities within our community. Our PDS family offers a supportive community open to all. Sign up for 2025-2026 membership here!
summary: Pre-Dental Society is a pre-dental organization at UCR that offers dental exposure and leadership opportunities for undergraduate students who are passionate about dentistry.

name: La Union Estudiantil de la Raza (UER)
description:
La Unión Estudiantil de la Raza, Inc. was founded on October 6, 1992 at the University of California, Riverside by ten individuals. They had an idea that there needed to be a place where one could feel comfortable and respected without any animosity for what one thought, felt, or believed. The members analyzed the purpose and decided to create an organization that would create leaders by focusing on incorporation of ethnicity with indigenous cultures. It was decided on this day that the members would work together to incorporate in the university a Chicano-Latino/Indigenous organization that was different from the Greek system.

La Union Estudiantil de la Raza, Inc. has a distinctly different agenda that differs from the existing fraternal order. Our pledge ship is a non-hazing learning process of integration where incoming members can comfortably enter our Brotherhood. Our social atmosphere consists of recreational activities as well as scholastic and moral support. Our social activities involve unification gatherings of Chicano/Latino students, cultural trips, retreats, fundraisers, and intramural sports with other organizations on campus.

name: Muslim Student Association
description: Asalamu Alaikum, Peace and Blessings upon all of those who read this message. Muslim Students Association (MSA) is a religious organization devoted to strengthening the Muslim community through service and activism, educating both Muslims and people of other faiths about the religion of Islam, and facilitating a better environment for students on campus. MSA aims to serve Muslim students during their university careers by creating a strong, diverse network of students who are conscious of their Islamic identity, well-trained, and committed to serving Islam.
summary: Muslim Student Association at UC Riverside

name: Latino Union
description:
Latino Union is a non-profit organization with a principal focus on community service, promoting higher education, and community building. Our community service ranges from, but is not limited to, local street clean-ups, food drives, and care packages to the local community. We also collaborate with different student organizations to do community service. Our organization offers members a variety of opportunities to participate in local community service events. 

Latino Union is also a social organization. We hold activities for members throughout the year, which consist of events such as potlucks, bowling nights, movie nights, and the list goes on! We also have socials with other organizations on campus to get to know other organizations on campus and to make meaningful connections. As an organization, we encourage support toward other organizations' fundraisers and activities.

Academics are crucial to the mission of Latino Union, as we believe in the advancement of our members through higher education! If a member is struggling, we have support through Library Hours and guidance. We also host guest speakers from the local community to educate our members on different career paths. We want all members to be at their best when it comes to their academic coursework!

Another big part of Latino Union is that we are first-generation and transfer student-friendly; we provide many on-campus resources for our members. We also provide resources that include scholarships, internships, career readiness workshops, and bring awareness to our members about campus resources. Our organization does offer a scholarship to our members called the “Latino Union Scholarship,” which is awarded to a member at our End of the Year Banquet. Our overall goal is to be a bridge to our members’ success while fostering a supportive and connected community. 

Meetings are held weekly on Wednesday nights at 8pm at Olmsted 1212. Contact us and join in on the fun!
summary: Latino Union's purpose is to unite Latinos at UC-Riverside through community service, and to embrace our cultures in unity. We promote higher education, and strive to build long lasting friendships through doing good in our community! Meetings Wed. @ 8pm

name: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) at UCR
description:
The Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) at the University of California, Riverside (UCR) is a technical organization with a focus on computer science that is open to all years and majors. Our focus includes knowledge growth, professional development, and leadership. With project-based programs, technical workshops, hackathons, and networking, ACM provides students the tools and experience to further their education and future.

All ACM at UCR links: acmucr.carrd.co
summary: ACM at UCR is a technical organization with a focus on computer science that is open to all years and majors.

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description:
The purposes of SASP are to:

  • encourage and promote collaboration and community among school psychology students and faculty, the UCR community, and other school psychology programs and organizations, both locally and nationally
  • provide opportunities for networking and additional training in the field of school psychology
  • encourage and promote participation in research that increases the educational and psychological well-being of children and youth
  • promote the field of school psychology and the doctoral program in school psychology at UCR

name: Association of Graduate Students in Hispanic Studies
description: Preamble As graduate students in the Department of Hispanic Studies at the University of California, Riverside, we recognize the need to form a cohesive association to address issues related to graduate students and to continue the growth and development of our department. We believe that all students have the right to participate in decisions that affect the conditions and quality of their education. This participation requires meaningful, active, and respectful discourse among students, as well as positive and productive interaction between students and faculty. As decisions reached by both students and faculty impact the nature of the learning environment, it is the intention of the Graduate Students of the Hispanic Studies Department at UCR to ensure that the rights and responsibilities of all persons involved in the learning environment are not abridged. The name of this organization shall be Association of Graduate Students in Hispanic Studies, hereafter referred to as the AGSHS, as it will appear throughout the constitution.
summary: The Association of Graduate Students in Hispanic Studies seeks to promote the interests of the graduate students in the UCR Hispanic Studies Department.

name: Neuroscience Graduate Student Association
description: We vow to be an academic/social organization, with its own independent finances, empowered to represent graduate students, and to affect academic program policy. The purposes of this organization shall be: To create an association that will allow students of the UCR graduate program in Neuroscience to participate in the administrative affairs of the program; To foster an open atmosphere conducive towards the advancement of the students higher education; To provide a forum where members of the organization may voice their opinions and express their views; and To hold events that will promote program identity and unity.
summary: We vow to be an academic/social organization, with its own independent finances, empowered to represent graduate students, and to affect academic program policy.

name: Running Club at UCR

We host casual practices on tested routes around the surrounding area, offer opportunities to participate in competitive races throughout the year, and organize social events/collaborations. Our Runs are held Monday through Friday at 5 PM (or 4 PM during winter daylight savings) outside the SRC at the tables near the north entrance. Join us whenever you can—we’d love to see you often!

If you'd like to learn more about the club or keep up to date with our events, follow us @runningclubatucr on Instagram.

OR check out our Discord or our email list by clicking join from this page.

Our website: runningclubatucr.wordpress.com
summary: We’re a casual, social, running club based at UCR, open to anyone regardless of experience level! Providing a friendly, welcoming, and safe environment for everyone. Join us outside the SRC at 4pm Mon-Fri, no commitment required!

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC)

-

name: African Americans United in Science (AAUS)
description:
The purpose of AAUS is to increase the educational and professional opportunities available to African American science students at the University of California, Riverside; stimulate the dissemination of valuable information to both student and the overall community, and provide social, cultural, and volunteer activities that serve to enrich the undergraduate experience of AAUS members.

To familiarize AAUS members with graduate and professional school information and career opportunities in the sciences.

accessible to students.

To obtain and disperse information for and encourage participation in) professional mentoring, summer programs, research opportunities, and conferences pertinent to the success of AAUS members in their particular fields of study.

name: TEDxUCR
description:
Welcome to TEDxUCR! We are a project-based organization working under TEDx to connect the UCR and greater Riverside community to speakers worldwide. Our primary focus is producing in-person and virtual TEDx talks with the help of our four committees: selections, logistics, marketing, and sponsorships. 

For more information and follow us on Instagram (@tedxucr). Also, check out our previous talks on YouTube by searching up 'tedxucr'.

Do you want to help create an official TED talk on UCR's campus? If the answer is YES to any of those questions, then you can find our member application coming out soon!

If you are interested in being a speaker, shoot us an email at jraba014@ucr.edu with a bio about yourself and a short outline of what you would like to talk about. You may get an opportunity to speak at our next talk! You can also email us if you have any questions about anything TEDxUCR!
summary: In the spirit of ideas worth spreading, TED has created a program called TEDx. It is a program of local, self-organized events that bring people together to share a TED-like experience. Our event is call TEDxUCR, where x=independently organized TED event

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
NSBE Vision

We envision a world in which engineering is a mainstream word in homes and communities of color, and all Black students can envision themselves as engineers. In this world, Blacks exceed parity in entering engineering fields, earning degrees, and succeeding professionally.

NSBE has grown from six to more than 17,000 members, and its annual meeting has blossomed into the Annual Convention, hosting more than 13,000 attendees. NSBE has more than 500 active chapters in the U.S. and abroad: 148 NSBE Jr. (pre-collegiate), 291 collegiate and 84 NSBE Professionals chapters.

Headquartered in Alexandria, Va., NSBE offers academic excellence programs, scholarships, leadership training, professional development and access to career opportunities for thousands of members annually and provides opportunities for their success that remain unmatched by any other organization.The main goal of the Society’s current 10-year Strategic Plan is to lead the United States to produce 10,000 Black Engineers annually by 2025.

 

NSBE Mission & Objectives

The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black Engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

The National Society of Black Engineers strives to accomplish the following objectives for our organization:

  • Stimulate and develop student interest in the various engineering disciplines
  • Strive to increase the number of minority students studying engineering at both the undergraduate and graduate levels 
  • Encourage members to seek advanced degrees in engineering or related fields and to obtain professional engineering registrations
  • Promote public awareness of engineering and the opportunities for Blacks and other minorities in that profession
  • Function as a representative body on issues and developments that affect the careers of Black Engineers  

Innovative project ideas are generated and implemented throughout the year on the chapter, regional and national levels. Some of NSBE's present activities include tutorial programs, group study sessions, high school/junior high outreach programs, technical seminars and workshops, a national communications network (NSBENET), two national magazines (NSBE Magazine and NSBE Bridge), an internal newsletter, a professional newsletter (Career Engineer, a supplement in NSBE Magazine), resume books, career fairs, awards, banquets and an annual national convention.

 

The NSBE Torch

The NSBE torch symbolizes our everlasting burning desire to achieve success in this competitive society and to affect a positive change on the quality of life for all people. The lightning bolt represents the striking impact that will be felt by the Society and industry due to the contributions and accomplishments made by dedicated members of the National Society of Black Engineers.

The official colors of "The Torch" are as follow:

  • The "N" is green - Pantone Matching System (PMS) 361
  • The letters "SBE" & the handle of The Torch shall be black
  • The flame of The Torch shall be red - PMS 485
  • The lightning bolts shall be yellow (CMYK process)
summary: Our mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

name: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women
description:
The founders of Pi Beta Phi® created the Fraternity to cultivate lifelong friendships among its members and inspire them to achieve the highest and best in life. Throughout its history, Pi Beta Phi has helped young women develop meaningful relationships while reaching their personal potential through leadership opportunities.


These relationships help members adapt to college life by providing a sense of mutual support and guidance. But, finding a place to belong on campus is only the beginning. Pi Beta Phi’s many programs offer a variety of services and benefits to its members including a lifetime of sisterhood, personal development, philanthropic service, leadership opportunities, mentor relationships and networking.


MISSION
The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.
VISION
The vision of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to be recognized as a premier organization for women by providing lifelong enrichment to its members and contributing to the betterment of society.
CORE VALUES
•Integrity
•Lifelong Commitment
•Honor and Respect
•Personal and Intellectual Growth
•Philanthropic Service to Others
•Sincere Friendship

READ. LEAD. ACHIEVE.
Today, the Fraternity continues to be a vehicle for serving others in the field of literacy. Pi Beta Phi’s 100-year literacy vision reads: Pi Beta Phi leads the way to a more literate society. The vision is supported by our Read. Lead. Achieve.® literacy platform, which includes the following initiatives:
• Champions are Readers (CAR): Pi Phi’s hands-on program, CAR is a monthlong reading enrichment program for third-grade elementary students. CAR encourages students to develop daily reading habits and provides Fraternity members with an easy-to-implement program encouraging family literacy.
• First Book: Pi Phi is proud of its corporate partnership with First Book, an international nonprofit organization founded in 1992 with a single mission: to give children from low-income families the opportunity to read and own their first new books. In five years, Pi Phi and First Book donated nearly 3 million brand-new books to children who would otherwise not have a book of their own.
• Fraternity Day of Service: Pi Beta Phi celebrates Fraternity Day of Service in an effort to advance literacy on or around March 2 in honor of Dr. Seuss’® birthday. Every year, Pi Beta Phi sponsors a Fraternity-wide event in order to achieve maximum impact in fundraising and awareness.
• Arrow in the Arctic: Pi Phi’s Canadian philanthropy, Arrow in the Arctic, supports literacy in Canada’s Northern library systems: the Yukon, Northwest Territories and Nunavut. Arrow in the Arctic also helps to preserve the language and culture of the north’s Inuit and First Nations. Pi Beta Phi is the only National Panhellenic Conference (NPC) group to have its own Canadian philanthropy.
• Local Literacy Initiatives and Service: Local literacy initiatives promote and improve literacy in Pi Phi communities, reaffirm the Fraternity’s commitment to and impact on youth education, and provide an opportunity for Pi Beta Phi chapters and alumnae organizations to see the direct impact of their work how it affects children in their communities.

 

If you are interested in learning more about this Fraternity, please visit our National website at pibetaphi.org.

 

If you wish to contact our national headquarters, please see the information below:

Pi Beta Phi Headquarters

1154 Town & Country Commons Drive 
Town & Country, Missouri 63017 
PH: (636) 256–0680
FAX: (636) 256–8095
summary: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity is an organization for women, which seeks to promote friendship, development women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.

name: Coptic Orthodox Christian Club at UCR
description: The Coptic Church is about 2000 years old - it is enriched with knowledge and tradition taken all the way from St. Mark, an apostle of the Lord Jesus Christ. St. Mark ended up being a martyr for our church, as so have been most of our preachers and believers, but he did spread the faith to us and we are here to learn more about it as well as spread that knowledge to all others! The purpose of our club is to reach out to all students, no matter what religion or ethnicity at UCR. We exist so that we may provide a means by which the Orthodox community can grow spiritually. Our goal is to make sure that we all grow stronger in knowledge, faith, unity, and love. We do so by providing fun activities, spiritual meetings, prayer meetings, Bible studies, community service, etc. It is not just limited to Copts, but to everyone who is seeking God and would like to find Him; we welcome questions and curiosity! Please ask and contact us if you would like to learn more!
summary: The purpose of our club is to reach out to all students at UCR. We exist so that we may provide a means by which the Orthodox community can grow spiritually in togetherness through learning about the Bible and Christ!

name: Delta Chi Fraternity
description:
Mission

We, the members of The Delta Chi Fraternity, believing that great advantages are to be derived from a brotherhood of college and university men, appreciating that close association may promote friendship, develop character, advance justice, and assist in the acquisition of a sound education, do ordain and establish this Constitution.

Founding

On October 13, 1890 eleven law students at Cornell University formed a Law Fraternity, Delta Chi. In 1922, the Fraternity officially went "general", thus opening its membership to men in all areas of study. Delta Chi, in 1929, became the first fraternity to officially abolish the hazing practices associated with "hell week".

Eleven Basic Expectations of a Delta Chi

  1. I will strive for academic achievement and practice academic integrity.
  2. I will respect the dignity and worth of all persons. I will not physically, mentally, psychologically or sexually abuse or haze any human being.
  3. I will protect the health and safety of all human beings.
  4. I will respect my property and the property of others; therefore, I will neither abuse nor support the abuse of property.
  5. I will meet my financial obligations in a timely manner.
  6. I will neither use nor support the use of illegal drugs. I will neither abuse nor support the abuse of alcohol.
  7. I will acknowledge that a clean and attractive environment is essential to both physical and mental health; therefore, I will do all in my power to see that the chapter property is safe, properly cleaned and maintained.
  8. I will know and understand the ideals expressed in my fraternity Ritual and will incorporate them into my daily life.
  9. I will exercise compassion and understanding in dealing with all persons.
  10. I will sustain my commitment to and involvement with the Fraternity throughout my lifetime.
  11. I will challenge all my fraternity members to abide by these fraternity obligations and will confront those who violate them.
summary: Delta Chi is an internationally recognized social fraternity with more than 130 chapters. The organization has a high appreciation for academic and social excellence that will greatly enhance a man's college experience.

name: Lambda Sigma Gamma
description: While Lambda Sigma Gamma remains active in a wide variety of philanthropic services, the sorority’s main objective is to bring awareness and support to early Education. summary: Our organization's objective is to instill the desire for self-improvement and to promote unity and higher education among women. We want to empower women from all different backgrounds. We believe in beauty through diversity and unity through sisterhood

name: Black Student Union
description:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide a space for all students to gain personal knowledge and information about African/African American/Black populations in an effort to help bring down the cultural barriers and stereotypes that have plagued the African/African American/Black minority for centuries, as well as to foster a sense of community. BSU aspires to do this through programming, outreach, meetings, events, and any other means to ensure that all who desire the information shall receive it.

        In the context of the University of California, Riverside community as well as the greater Inland Empire, BSU serves to be an all-inclusive outlet in which any people who desire to share knowledge and enrich our mission statement.

       BSU will provide whatever resources necessary to assist other cultural groups within the University. The outlying community will also receive whatever resources necessary to help expand and enrich the mission statement.
summary: To provide a space for all students to gain personal knowledge and information about African/African American/Black populations in an effort to help bring down the cultural barriers and stereotypes that have plagued the Afrikan minority for centuries.

name: Kendo Club @ UCR
description:
The purpose of our organization is to introduce the martial art of Kendo to UCR.

Our organization would like to find students who are interested in Kendo throughout the University. Since other UC's have their own Kendo organizations, we are able to connect UCR to other schools through Kendo related activities. Our main goals are to practice Kendo and to teach cultural values, traditions, and the art at UCR.  

We participate in multiple tournaments throughout the year such as Yuhihai at UCLA, and Shoryuhai at Harvard University. In past years, we have consistently placed highly at these tournaments. For example, at the 2018 Yuhihai Tournament, our members placed First in the Co-ed Division, Second in the Kyu Individuals Division, and Third in the Kyu Team Division.

Beginners are welcome and encouraged to come out to practice. No prior experience is necessary. Wear clothes that are easy to move around in and an open mind.

 

Our Current Practice times are: 

Tuesday 8:30-10:30PM at MPR D at the Student Recreation Center

Thursday 8:30-10:30PM at MPR D at the Student Recreation Center

Discord Server Link:

https://discord.gg/BpqAMgtUFb

We will have all our announcements on Discord!
summary: We meet and practice Kendo, no prior experience necessary.

KASA is a club that not only aims to strengthen unity among the student body, but to also promote awareness of Korean/Korean American culture and serve as a channel for students with similar backgrounds to come together through various social outreach events.  

On a less formal note, KASA is an all-inclusive club started to bring students with similar interests closer together by experiencing Korean and Korean American culture, as well as a “home away from home” :)
summary: To promote the educational and enrichment of its members and the University community with aspects of Korean culture through involvement and participation in various social, cultural and political activities.

The purpose of this organization is to seek out those who are interested in the medical field and provide them with the resources to directly interact with physicians. APPLICATIONS FOR FALL ARE AVAILABLE BY WEEK ZERO & ARE DUE BY WEEK 2

bit.ly/ShadowMDApp

 

CLICK HERE TO VISIT OUR NEW WEBSITE!

description: Alpha Pi Sigma Sorority, Incorporated was established at the University of California, Riverside on March 5, 2000. A group of Latina women from a variety of backgrounds came together to form an organization that would unite and support women on university campuses. Our sisterhood is composed of educated, professional, diverse women committed to the empowerment of women and the Latin American community. We achieve these accomplishments through academic enrichment programs, community service, leadership development opportunities, and other activities geared towards the appreciation, promotion, and preservation of our surrounding communities. If you are interested in joining please reach out to us on Instagram or by email!

description:
1) To create an association that will allow students of the UCR graduate program in Genetics, Genomics, and Bioinformatics to participate in the administrative affairs of the program

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a national coeducational service organization founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship and Service. It provides its members the opportunity to develop leadership skills as they volunteer on their campus, in their community, to the nation, and to the organization. With more than 400,000 members on over 375 campuses, Alpha Phi Omega continues to provide more service on more campuses than any other collegiate service organization.  
 
It is the VISION of Alpha Phi Omega to be recognized as the premier service-based leadership development organization.
 
It is the MISSION of Alpha Phi Omega to prepare campus and community leaders through service.
 
The VALUES of Alpha Phi Omega are to develop leadership, promote friendship and provide service.
 
The OBJECTIVES of Alpha Phi Omega are Share, Grow, Improve and Invest.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a national coeducational service organization founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship, and Service. It provides its members the opportunity to develop leadership skills as they volunteer in various areas.

name: Associated Students Program Board
description:
The office of ASPB is dedicated to programming quality campus entertainment for the undergraduate population at the University of California, Riverside that appeals to the diverse communities and enhances campus life. 

The Associated Students Program Board (ASPB) is a student-run programming entity on the UC Riverside campus. Established in 1986, ASPB is funded by quarterly feeds paid by undergraduate students. The board is comprised of 16 full-time undergraduate students who work primarily, though not exclusively, in the following seven divisions: Concerts, Special Events, Films & Lectures, Contemporary Culture, Marketing, Membership, and Leadership. The first four programming divisions plan events (both large and small); all programs are voted on and approved by quorum of the board. The other two divisions provide support essential to the success of the events and the respectively. All 13 directors, the Vice Chairperson and Marketing Chairperson hold votes in program requests proposed by the board.
summary: The office of ASPB is dedicated to programming quality campus entertainment for the undergraduate population at the University of California, Riverside that appeals to the diverse communities and enhances campus life.

description:
We, as Undergraduate Members of women’s fraternities, stand for good scholarship, for guarding of good health, for maintenance of fine standards, and for serving, to the best of our ability, our college community. Cooperation for furthering fraternity life, in harmony with its best possibilities, is the ideal that shall guide our fraternity activities.

We, as Fraternity Women, stand for service through the development of character, inspired by the close contact and deep friendship of individual fraternity and Panhellenic life. 5. Having fun! :)

name: Formula Society of Automotive Engineers
description:
Please fill out this form if you're interested: Fall Applications open until October 17th 

Currently, we are mostly looking for EE/CS to build custom circuit boards and code our inverter, but we will accept all majors for either our mechanical (ME) team or our business team. We close applications 3 weeks into every quarter. We will dm you on Discord towards the end of the application period so watch out for our dm! You can join our Discord and Instagram for more information!

 

Since 2014, the Society of Automotive Engineers at UC Riverside has been empowering engineering students to learn and gain experience that will be used after graduation in many industries worldwide. Although traditionally thought of as a Mechanical Engineering Organization, our club has evolved into a multi-disciplinary team of engineers and business professionals throughout the years.  We have several cars under our belt and have transitioned to building formula-style electric vehicles.  Our alumni network includes professionals in highly competitive companies such as Tesla, Rivian, SpaceX, Anduril, etc. 

 

Through the years of operation, our program has encouraged the growth and development of bleeding-edge technologies, including the design and development of AC Synchronous Electric Motors, Lithium-Ion battery packs, complex mechanical drivetrain systems, air intake manifolds, and so much more.

 

As we continue to venture forward, we expect to build the second competitive electric vehicle as a team! We travel to competition every year to compete against 75 other colleges and this year it is in Michigan. 

Our sponsors include Tesla, Chicas Motorsport, SolidWorks, Hesse Mechatronics, Airtech, Ansys, Altium, IMS, Shiftclickmedia, GRI pumps, South Coast Foam, Duda, Kenny Leung, GCAPUCR, ASUCR.
summary: FSAE is a collegiate chapter of SAE International, focused on providing students with experiences you can't get in the classroom. The Formula SAE project allows students to come together to design, build, and race a formula-style race car.

name: Philosophy Club at UCR
description:
The Philosophy Club at UCR provides a fun and informal environment open to anyone seeking discussion, the exchange of ideas, or the pursuit of the examined life alongside a community of friends.


We help our members cultivate their critical, analytical, and interpretational skills through dialogues, talks, and reading groups, with the hope of shaping well-organized and systematic thinkers and presenters. We hope to contribute to this project of understanding one’s self and the larger world at every Club meeting, both for generally interested students and for those considering graduate school in philosophy.


Our members can expect a welcoming environment prizing the respectful exchange of ideas, where nothing is left unchallenged and where everyone walks the path to understanding critically and carefully.

Please join our discord if you are interested! https://discord.gg/mkar9pSYg3

name: Student Association of Graduate Anthropologists
description:
Welcome!

What is Anthropology at UCR?

"UCR Anthropology is committed to a socially engaged, critical anthropology...with a finely tuned sense of historical temporality that views change as normal as reproduction...The faculty and students are committed to an integrated and integrating concept of the discipline. They view the traditional subfields -applied, archaeological, sociocultural, biological, and linguistic anthropology- as cross cut by foci that bring faculty and students together in ways that reinforce the unity of the discipline rather than its divisions."

What is SAGA? 

SAGA is the graduate student arm of the Anthropology Department at UCR. The mission of SAGA is first and foremost to foster a sense of community among our anthropological scholars. 

We do so in a number of ways: 

1. We facilitate communication between graduate students and faculty members 

2. We collect and disseminate vital educational and funding information 

And

3. We increase the public's awareness of the department and of anthropology in general

Most of all we are here to provide social, professional, and economic support to our graduate students through resources linked to our page and through various events organized throughout the year.

Stay tuned for a calendar listing upcoming workshops, brown bag lunches, as well as the date and theme for what will be the 37th Annual James Young Colloquium.
summary: Graduate students are encouraged to share ideas and raise awareness regarding our Anthropology Department in SAGA. The purpose of SAGA is to foster a community of support that will help our graduate students succeed intellectually and professionally.

name: Chinese Student Association
description:
The Chinese Student Association at U.C. Riverside is a social, cultural, and service-orientated organization that strives to promote the Chinese American culture throughout the Riverside community. CSA not only emphasizes cultural and political awareness as Chinese Americans, but also community awareness. We provide various opportunities throughout the quarter that allow our members to develop leadership skills through our service-oriented activities. CSA also provides a social outlet for students with similar backgrounds and interests to form connections with one another. 

 

Our logo is the Chinese character "xin" which stands for heart. The theme that we have been promoting is "Two Worlds, One Heart” which is a representation of our Chinese Heritage fused with the American Culture that defines the Chinese American. 

 

Chinese Culture Night is a proud tradition that presents the Chinese American identity while showcasing the uniqueness of our culture in a live stage performance. Culture Night significantly influences all those that are involved because it allows us to come together to celebrate our heritage, acknowledge our culture, and by doing so, we understand that those constituents are an underlying definition of who we are as Chinese Americans; Celebrating the past, understanding the present, looking to the future.

name: Global Medical Brigades at UCR
description:
Stay connected by joining our 2025 Fall Quarter General Meetings!

 

They will be held on Thursdays of Weeks 2, 4, and 8 from 6–7 PM

 

Global Brigades is the world’s largest student-led global health and sustainable development organization. Since 2004, Global Brigades has mobilized thousands of university students and professionals through nine skill-based programs that work in partnership with community members to improve quality of life in under-resourced regions while respecting local culture.

 

Currently, Global Brigades has ten disciplines. Medical, dental, public health, water, microfinance, business, environmental, human rights, engineering, and architecture brigades all work in rural communities and represent the holistic model that Global Brigades follows. Students can have hands-on-experience in working in communities in any of these disciplines while respecting the culture and having first-hand experience of seeing the people's lives they change through volunteer work.

 

Visit our Website for additional information!

Add to our Spotify playlist!
summary: Global Medical Brigades has mobilized students to work alongside with professionals and community members to improve the quality of life in under-resourced communities both locally and globally in countries such as Honduras, Panama, and Nicaragua.

PISA seeks to provide a space for its members to articulate their experiences and share their individual cultures with each other and the larger university and community populations. This in turn, will serve as an avenue to promote and establish cultural solidarity, leadership, diversity, tolerance and respect. The students and associates of the Pacific Islander Student Association want to promote both Pacific Islander culture and Pacific Islander unity in the community. For instance, hosting a Pacific Islander High School Conference will help promote the benefits of higher education.

name: American Medical Student Association
description:
Hello there! Are you interested in pre-medicine or pre-health? What, you are? Well then, AMSA is definitely the organization for you!

What is AMSA you ask?

The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is the largest Pre-Medical student organization in the nation, and is completely student governed, to best meet the needs of its audience as well as the largest pre-med/pre-health club on campus. As a nationwide medical on-campus organization, we host events that provide resources, knowledge and leadership opportunities in a vast array of committees that supports your aspiration to go into medical school. Pretty cool, right?

Wait, there is more!

AMSA is made up of a diverse group of students all running and working together through their own committees and interests, with a common shared goal: to help our fellow and neighboring students have the best chance at getting into the medical school of their choice and also shed light to another pre-health pathways through guest speakers, hands-on events, and more! This allows AMSA members to meet new and awesome colleagues and provide a support system throughout their years here at the University of California, Riverside.

With that said, Welcome aboard, AMSA!

For more information visit our website and sign-up for the mailing list at the URL below and follow us on Instagram @amsaucr! 

https://www.amsaucr.org
summary: To provide an environment that supports the creative ideas of physicians-in-training, knowledge and resources that guides students who are interested in the medical field.

We are here as a resource for all students to inquire about the many ways marketing can be beneficial to one's career, no matter what field they plan on heading toward.
summary: A business student run organization focused on developing students into successful leaders in the field of marketing, management, advertising, public relations, sales, and other related fields. We participate in professional events, workshops, and others

We are committed to advocating for student needs, and organizing events that enrich the graduate experience including workshops, outreach events, and socials.

name: Queer Alliance
description:
Welcome to Queer Alliance!

Our mission is to create inclusive, safe spaces on campus that empower UCR's queer LGBTQIA+ community, and to promote the acceptance of diverse identities (relating to sexuality and gender) and intersectionalities through advocacy and education.

We organize large-scale queer events on campus that include:

The Coming Out Dance (a safe interactive space where people of all orientations and identities can mingle and let loose), The Coming Out Monologues (an educational and empowering performative program speaking on the experiences of UCR's queer population), The Dragalicious Drag Ball (our largest program offering an evening of drag performance entertainment and a chance for student to participate), and Pride Prom (an outreach event geared towards creating a safe and inclusive version of Prom that queer youth in the surrounding area can enjoy and be themselves).  

Members can engage either actively through the organization, planning, and execution of the listed events, or more passively by enjoying regular socials and outings with other members and queer people on campus.
summary: Queer Alliance aims to create inclusive spaces that empower the LGBTQIA+ community at UCR and youth of the surrounding area, and to promote the acceptance of diverse sexual and gender identities through advocacy and education
