name: Sustainable UCR
description:
For over a decade, Sustainable UCR has been working towards building a better, more sustainable life and community at UCR. In the past, we have worked on installing solar panels during Solarthon, collecting/composting coffee grinds, organizing events during Earth Week, and working with other organizations to make their events more sustainable. Today, we are focused on continuing the legacy of our great work, specifically in the aspects of food, water, waste, energy, and culture. 

 

Join our sustainable movement as we change our community!

Visit our Linktree!

Sustainable UCR is open to any ideas that our members present! We welcome any ideas for projects, events, or collaborations.
summary: Be the change that this world needs! Join us in advocating for sustainability at UCR and in our local communities.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi Fraternity
description: Alpha Epsilon Pi was founded to provide opportunities for the Jewish college man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi was founded to provide opportunities for the Jewish college man seeking the best possible college and fraternity experience.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description:
Our mission as the National Residence Hall Honorary at the University of California, Riverside is to aid the development and maintenance of a strong, diverse and academically successful residential community within the UCR Residence Halls. We believe that in order to accomplish this we must do the following:

  • Maximize opportunities for residents to become leaders and explore their unique leadership styles.
  • Create and support initiatives that focus on diversity awareness, tolerance and celebration.
  • Develop effective educational and social programs.
  • Encourage academic excellence through role modeling and the development of academic initiatives.
  • Recognize resident leaders for their service to the residence hall community.
  • Find and direct residents to appropriate resources and leadership opportunities in Housing Services, on campus and in the surrounding community.
  • Find and direct residents to appropriate resources and leadership opportunities in Housing Services, on campus and in the surrounding community.
  • Be a channel through which recognition of the Residence Halls is known on a local, regional and national level.
summary: To aid the development and maintenance of a strong, diverse and academically successful residential community within the UCR Residence Halls.

name: Interfraternity Council (IFC)
description: The object of this organization shall be to promote the general welfare of all member fraternities; to encourage the development and growth of the Inter-Fraternal Council at UCR members; to provide educational, social and philinthropic activities to enhance our member's University experience.
summary: The object of this organization shall be to promote the general welfare of all member fraternities; to encourage the development and growth of the IFC at UCR members; to provide educational, social & service activities that will enhance member experienc

name: Graduate Students in English Association
description: Our primary purpose is to ensure the welfare and representation of graduate students' concerns at all relevant levels of the English Department's business. We organize a variety of events such as lecture series, book sales, and the occasional get-together.
summary: Our primary purpose is to ensure the welfare and representation of graduate students' concerns at all relevant levels of the English Department's business.

name: 909 Dance Troupe
description:
We, the members of 909 Dance Troupe (formally known as 909 Hip Hop Dance Troupe), at the University of California, Riverside, in conjunction with Hip Hop’s core principles, strive to honor Hip Hop’s roots in Black culture and to further the development of dance through both educational and social means, with the cooperation of administration, faculty and students.

In 1997, the seeds of Hip-Hop were planted at the University of California, Riverside forming 909 Dance Troupe. Representing Riverside, CA, 909 opens its membership to all dancers in the community. For 29 years, 909 has created an image that is recognized and respected for its connection to underground hip-hop music. Although the team has evolved throughout the years, 909 respects its roots in underground hip-hop and continues its expression to the masses through originality, unique styles of music, and the art of dance. “Strength in our roots, power in our number: 909.”
summary: To better the awareness and development of hip-hop dance through educational and social means, with the cooperation of administration, faculty and students. Strength in our roots. Power in our number: 909.

name: Native American Student Association (NASA)
description: Maintain a strong network with neighboring Native communities, increasing the number of Native students at UCR and providing a space for our Native students to promote cultural awareness of the various tribes to the campus.
summary: Native American Student Association

name: Mujeres Unidas de UCR
description: As mujeres of the University of California, Riverside, we strive for the betterment of la RAZA!  We realize the importance of unity amongst ourselves and strive to maintain positive interaction with other organizations.  While empowerment of la mujer at UCR is our main goal, we are also concerned with educational, social, and economic issues within our communities.  As a women’s organization, we welcome all individuals who agree with our goals and objectives and who are willing to fight for la causa. As educated mujeres we realize the responsibility we have to our hermanas y hermanos to provide support, open and educate minds, maintain la cultura, and those traditions that do not oppress us. LA MUJER VENCERA!!!
summary: We are concerned with educational, social and economic issues within our community while empowering la mujer of UCR.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description:
Alpha Chi Omega is a national women's organization that enriches the lives of members through lifetime opportunities for friendship, leadership, learning, and service.

Alpha Chi Omega's national philanthropy supports Survivors of Domestic Violence and promotes domestic violence awareness. To support our cause, Iota Xi held our annual Next Top Alpha in the Spring of 2025. In this pageant-like event, our campus' fraternities, sororities and other greek organizations raised money through fundraisers in the weeks leading up to the event and our sisters, friends and family all donated as well. Organizations on campus and people of all ages are invited to our community wide event to contribute to the cause and buy tickets to watch their peers compete for Next Top Alpha. Together, this philanthropic event worked to fight domestic violence by raising awareness and money for the Riverside Area Rape Crisis Center. As an Organization we have raised over $100,000 for NORA.

This past Winter, Iota Xi sent the Chapter President, Vice President of Chapter Relations & Standards, Vice President of Risk Management, and our Vice President of Membership Programming to a Leadership Academy in Indianapolis, Indiana where they advanced their skills as leaders and got the opportunity to make connections with sisters from chapters across the nation! We are recognized for focusing on diversity, equity, boosting member moral, and mental health programming. We encourage diversity and inclusion for all of our members!

Every year, Iota Xi chapter celebrates Healthy Relationships Week during the week of Valentine's Day. This year it was on February 11-17, 2025, where we took part in a nationwide recognition to promote healthy relationships, and by doing so, help prevent domestic violence and sexual assault. We held workshops about what a healthy relationship should look like and how respect is a two-way street. Also, our Vice President of Public Relations introduced a social media challenge in which everyone was able to participate, encouraging members to promote healthy relationships with photos on Instagram.

During the Summer, we prepared for fall recruitment. We have a pre-recruitment week where we are able to practice and prepare for recruitment as well as hold many bonding activities!

We are fortunate that Fall Recruitment is during the month of October, as it is just one of the many reasons to recognize Domestic Violence Awareness Month! We hold weekly events and every member works to empower and impact others through our philanthropy. We cannot wait to share our values, philanthropy, and sisterhood with so many amazing women and welcome in a new member class! We prepare for recruitment year round, and we hope the recruitment process is as empowering for potential new members as it is for us!
summary: Alpha Chi Omega is a national women's organization that enriches the lives of members through lifetime opportunities for friendship, leadership, learning, and service.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
We are brought together by heritage, social responsibility and desire to improve the equality of all people through the use of science and technology. We value excellence in education, professional pursuits, and leadership. We obtain excellence through integrity, empowerment, achievement, diversity, and continuous improvement. SHPE is a national organization whose primary function is to cultivate the potential of the Hispanic community through technology, engineering, math, and science. We will fulfill our mission by increasing educational opportunities, promoting professional and personal growth, and reinforcing our reputation as a vital Hispanic organization.

To learn more, follow us on Instagram @shpeucr and join our discord at https://discord.gg/5EXaCJz for updates.
summary: We obtain excellence through integrity, empowerment, achievement, diversity, and continuous improvement. We are the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the potential of Hispanics in STEM.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
In 1904, Alpha Kappa Psi was founded on the principles of educating its members and the public to appreciate and demand higher ideals in business and to further the individual welfare of members during college and beyond.

College men and women everywhere are discovering that Alpha Kappa Psi is much more than just another organization or club—it is a unique, prestigious association of students, professors, graduates and professionals with common interests and goals. They join Alpha Kappa Psi to take advantage of valuable educational, friendship and networking opportunities.
summary: Developing well-trained, ethical, skilled, resourceful, experienced business leaders.

name: SOON Movement Global
description: The purpose of SOON Movement Global is to help fulfill the Great Commission at UCR and to all parts of the world. Our vision is for everyone in UCR to know at least one person who truly follows Christ. We want to share the good news of Jesus Christ to our community and campus and build a community of passionate followers of Christ.
summary: The purpose of SOON Movement Global is to fulfill the Great Commission at UCR and to all parts of the world.

name: Christians on Campus
description: To provide an environment for seekers of the truth to know God and His economy, and to grow, function and be built with other believers.
summary: To provide an environment for seekers of the truth to know God and His economy, and to grow, function and be built with other believers.

name: Sakura-bu
description:
Purpose
Sakura-bu is an organization at UCR where domestic and international students from Japan exchange cultural ideas and languages.

Aim
Sakura-bu aims to welcome incoming exchange students from Japan, connecting them with UCR students.

Proposal
Sakura-bu proposes allowing international students to make new friends and precious memories.

Environment
Sakura-bu fosters an everlasting acquisition of Japanese culture and language for UCR students.

Intention
Sakura-bu intends to bring students from a diverse subset of backgrounds to immerse themselves in different cultures.

Contact

- Instagram

- Discord

- Facebook

- Twitter
summary: Japanese Culture, Language Education, and Exchange Organization

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Article II of the Fraternity Constitution reads:

The objects of this Fraternity shall be to foster high ideals of friendship among college women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create in them a true sense of social responsibility, and to develop in them the best qualities of character.

Delta Gamma’s primary purpose is to create an environment for its members in which lasting friendships are established and in which members find the processes, the experiences and the disciplines that will stimulate clear thought. Its aim is to foster an atmosphere in which women will develop a deeper love and consideration for mankind, a more profound understanding of the purpose of life and a basic wisdom upon which to build their lives.

Delta Gamma’s purpose is accomplished through planned collegiate and alumnae programs that provide intellectual motivation, the opportunity for graciousness in daily living, and community awareness which will enrich the lives of members and instill in them a respect for the enduring values to be gained from the Fraternity.

Mission Statement: Delta Gamma offers to women of all ages a rich heritage based on principles of personal integrity, personal responsibility and intellectual honesty. Its primary purpose is to foster high ideals of friendship, promote educational and cultural interests, create a true sense of social responsibility, and develop the finest qualities of character.
summary: Article II: To foster high ideals of friendship among college women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create in them a true sense of social responsibility, and to develop in them the best qualities of character

name: Gardening Club
description:
Gardening Club is a group of students who are passionate about seeking sustainable initiatives on our campus and in our communities. The role of our organization is to raise awareness of local sustainable food systems and environmental justice, while empowering students to take collaborative action! We hope to create a safe space for gardeners of all levels. Join our org to work with other students, local nonprofit organizations, and community activists in growing a community of dedicated gardeners! 

 

Everyone is welcome and please connect to receive updates about our events!

We can't wait to see you all!
summary: An organization open to UCR and non-UCR individuals who are seeking to foster skills surrounding community gardening and to educate others about agriculture.

name: Men's Club Volleyball at UCR
description:
In recent years, the volleyball community at UCR has grown. This club is designed to provide a competitive environment for students in the sport of Volleyball. The club provides a space to meet people with the common interest of volleyball, provide opportunity for community service, compete against other club teams, enjoy recreational volleyball, teach comradery, and more. 

Please follow our Instagram for more info about tryouts, tournaments, and open gyms: @ucrmensclubvb
summary:
Men's Volleyball Club at UCR is designed to provide a competitive volleyball environment for students. 

Follow our Instagram for more info about tryouts, tournaments, and open gyms: @ucrmensclubvb

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE)
description: AIChE is a professional affiliation consisting of Chemical and Environmental Engineers. AIChE at UCR provides opportunities for all students to tour facilities, meet professionals in industry, and advance academically and professionally through a series of workshops.
summary: AIChE is a professional affiliation consisting of Chemical and Environmental Engineers, but all majors are welcome to join!

name: Student Disability Union
description:
Welcome! We are the Student Disability Union at UCR!

 

Our purpose is to create an inclusive, safe, and engaging group on campus that empowers UCR's disabled community and promotes acceptance through advocacy and education. We hold events that actively collaborate with students and other diverse organizations/clubs to bring the disabled student population together to establish a community, encourage discussion, and participate in fun socials. The ultimate goal is to build relationships, cultivate campus connections, and give a collective voice to an underrepresented and misunderstood minority.  

ALL UCR students, with or without disabilities, are welcome to participate!

We organize events/activities on campus that include:

  • Socials (individuals can chat and chill while participating in fun-themed activities)
  • Ability Ball (a lively evening showcasing the talents of both individuals with or without disabilities) 
  • Empower Your Peers Groups (caring and empowering groups of different types of disabilities/disorders/illnesses conversing on personal experiences and learning mental health exercises. Ex. Mobility, Epilepsy, Diabetes, ADD/ADHD etc.)

Event and meeting details will be shared through our instagram: @sdu_ucr
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to work actively and personally with students, other diverse organizations, and faculty to support students with disabilities in being involved on campus and to build community and campus connections.

name: Life
description: Our mission is to show students that the Bible can be applied to everyday life and to challenge students to look further into the Bible as a wellspring of knowledge. There are several meetings during the week where students are invited to participate in Bible discussions (regardless of their religious affiliation). Personal bible studies are centered on individual schedules and are flexible for different time and location arrangements. The club centers around the Bible and active fellowship with one another to build friendships!
summary: The purpose of Life is to provide students with a small group setting that is a safe place to discuss relevant topics from the Bible in an informal setting for people of all kinds of backgrounds whether Christian or not.

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: We are the Women's Club Soccer team at UC Riverside! Our team competes against universities and colleges located throughout California. We practice twice a week on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 7:30pm to 9:30pm at the Glen Mor Fields located on campus by the residence halls. The players that participate have prior soccer experience, ranging from youth soccer, club soccer, high school soccer, and even former intercollegiate athletes. We promote learning as much as we can by practicing our soccer skills, but also focus on teamwork, leadership, and respect. We encourage everyone to join if you have the passion and drive to play soccer!
summary: Women's Club Soccer is a competitive sports team at UC Riverside. We practice twice a week and play competitive games against other universities on weekends. We promote fitness, friendship, and leadership.

name: Psi Chi Psychology Honor Society
description:
Our general meetings are typically held every other week of the quarter. For more information on becoming a member and other frequently asked questions, please visit our website.

____________________________

With over 1,000 chapters in U.S. colleges and universities, Psi Chi is the National Honor Society for Psychology. It was founded in 1929 for graduate and undergraduate students who strive to maintain academic excellence and advance the science of Psychology. 

 

Psi Chi is a purely academic honors society based on academic merit and scholastic achievement. Psi Chi is not a fraternity or sorority. We are the primary psychology organization on campus and we provide numerous opportunities for professional development, networking, and leadership experience through our general meetings, workshops, socials, and volunteering events throughout the year. 

 

Psi Chi is known for hosting the Fall Lab Fair, the new Psychology Winter Camp, and R’PSYC Undergraduate Research Conference, which are some of the ways we prepare our members to succeed in research, graduate school, and future careers.

 

If you want to make new friends with similar academic and professional interests, Psi Chi is the right place for you to find a safe and supportive community at UCR!

The scholarship of all Psi Chi members is valued. An official membership card and certificate are awarded to each member. Medals, honor cords, and stoles can be purchased to wear at graduation. 

____________________________


Dr. Ditta and Dr. Hendley, professors in the area of Psychology, are the faculty advisors. They provide many useful resources for students preparing for graduate school and exploring opportunities in psychology on campus.

 

Our Psi Chi chapter hosts many events to better equip students for success. Professors, graduate students, Clinical Psychologists, and guidance counselors from the Career Center represent some of the talented speakers who share their knowledge at Psi Chi events. In addition, we host numerous socials, volunteer events, and general meetings.
summary: “The purposes of Psi Chi shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.”

name: History Graduate Student Association
description: The History Department mini-GSA organizes and represents the needs and activities for graduate students in the Department of History.
summary: The History Department mini-GSA organizes and represents the needs and activities for graduate students in the Department of History.

name: Taiwanese Graduate Student Association
description: This organization serves as a platform to support the academic, professional, and social development of its members. It facilitates peer networking, educational programming, and information sharing to enhance engagement, collaboration, and access to relevant resources. Activities are designed to encourage knowledge exchange, community connection, and informed participation within the university environment.
summary: Provides networking, educational resources, and information exchange to support member development.

name: Collaborative Multicultural Council
description:
The Collaborative Multicultural Council (CMC) prides ourselves on being the largest and most diverse council on campus among four other councils.  

​The council is comprised of 15 organizations (fraternities and sororities): some that belong to the National Association of Latino Fraternal Organizations (NALFO), some to the National Asian Pacific Islander American Panhellenic Association (NAPA), and some are local and/or regional organizations. 
​
The goal of the Collaborative Multicultural Council is to establish a symbiotic relationship between the cultural fraternities and sororities.

Check out the Collaborative Multicultural Council at UC Riverside:
Instagram | Website
summary: To facilitate and strengthen the relations among the Sororities and Fraternities who compose it. This council will promote academic excellence, cultural awareness, alliance, community service, and integrity while developing leadership skills.

name: Music Graduate Student Association
description: The purposes of this organization shall be: a) to serve as a medium of communication between members of the MGSA, the GSA, and the Music Department; b) to promote and coordinate the interests of the members of the MGSA; c) to facilitate valuable intellectual exchange.
summary: The purposes of this organization shall be: a) to serve as a medium of communication between members of the MGSA, the GSA, and the Music Department; b) to promote and coordinate the interests of the members of the MGSA; c) to facilitate valuable intellect

name: Skateboard Club at University of California Riverside
description: This organization exists to give skateboarders and its followers chances to advance themselves in places other than a local park. Trips and events will be organized to feed the need of skateboarders at UCR and to promote the culture of skateboarding. This organization is a healthy environment for skaters of all levels to grow and cultivate, even those who are new to skateboarding and would like to try it out. Alongside that, we are open and more than accepting of individuals who are interested in the culture of skateboarding and its various aspects outside the physical sport itself, such as: art & design, fashion, photography, music, editing skate videos, watching skate videos, etc. Lastly, this organization likes to put an emphasis on academics over skateboarding, because we are really here at UCR to further our education and to obtain a degree that will benefit our members lifelong. We accept skateboarders of all levels, beginner or experienced; you are welcome here! MAIN CONTACT IS THROUGH OUR INSTAGRAM @skateclubucr
summary: This organization is about skateboarding, teaching students new to riding how to skateboard, and having a great time while doing it! We aim to spread the skateboard culture to everyone and create an environment for people to be themselves!

name: Tau Sigma National Honor Society
description:
Tau Sigma is an induction based national honors society focused on recognizing and promoting the academic excellence and involvement of transfer students.

 

The University of California- Riverside (UCR) branch of Tau Sigma provides its member's opportunities to earn scholarships, attend leadership conferences, take leadership positions, and serve others, while joining a robust community of other academically motivated transfer students.
summary: Tau Sigma National Honor Society – a national honor society designed exclusively for transfer students!

name: Lebanese Student Association at UCR
description:
The Lebanese Student Association (LSA) at UCR is a non-religious, non-political, non-profit organization that represents a group of individuals that come together to participate in social activities, academic workshops, conventions, and more. LSA provides opportunities to teach & enrich our community with the diverse culture of Lebanon and the Middle East. While learning and connecting with one another, this club expands people's knowledge on what Lebanese traditions and customs provide. LSA aspires toward community integration and seeks to promote an understanding of this region in the Middle East.

https://youtu.be/SokG6aVXzR0
summary: The Lebanese Student Association at UCR represents individuals that come together to participate in social activities, academic workshops, & more. LSA provides opportunities to teach & enrich our community with the culture of Lebanon and the Middle East.

name: Student Veterans Organization
description: To provide information and guidance to student Veterans, active duty, retired, reservist, National Guard members or any students receiving military educational benefits on current issues affecting them; help those members in need by providing scholarships (when available), based on achievement as stated on a Scholarship Application, to help them continue their education at UCR; create a network where members can share their knowledge with family members, the UC Riverside community and other organizations outside the school.
summary: Our purpose is to provide service, guidance and support to all Veterans, Service Members,students, or anyone receiving military educational benefits and their families.

name: Order of Omega
description: To recognize members of the greek community that have embody the values of the FSIC and have attained a high standard of leadership in inter-greek activities to encourage them to continue along this line, and to inspire others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment.
summary: To recognize greeks that have attained a high standard of leadership in inter-greek activities, to encourage them to continue along this line, and to inspire others to strive for similar conspicuous attainment.

name: La Familia de UCR
description:
Follow us on Instagram for the most up-to-date information @LaFamiliaDeUCR

La Familia exists because our experiences as a people, and our historical and continued struggle for liberation gives rise to particular needs and interests which require a different framework of analysis than that of the mainstream Latinx and queer communities. Recognizing the issues of religious bigotry, heterosexism, and ignorance in the Latinx communities and racism and ignorance in the larger Queer communities, La Familia demands a safe space of its own to explore, support and educate ourselves and others in imperative issues that affect us simultaneously, e.g. racism, sexism, heterosexism, and classism. La Familia commits itself to self-determination, self-respect, and dignity; not just mere survival. Our goals are to fight for self and group respect, to ensure cultural preservation, community activism and empowerment, community service, and unabashed, unrestrained self-expression.
summary: La Familia as a whole has developed into a dynamic advocacy that is based at universities and in local communities. La Familia de UCR (a.k.a. LaFa) establishes networks with other campuses and creates the necessary Queer space.

name: Catholic Student Fellowship
description: Hi there! We are a Catholic group associated with UCR, RCC, and the local St. Andrew's Newman Center whose goal is to make faithful, passionate disciples of Jesus Christ and leaders of the Catholic Church! We hold general meetings every Monday at 7:00 pm, table on Wednesdays at the UCR bell tower, and hold other events such as socials, bible study, retreats, and much more throughout the quarter! Many if not all of our events take place at the St. Andrew's Newman Center (we provide rides!). The address is 105 W. Big Springs Rd. Come check us out and make new friends! All are welcome!
summary: We are a Catholic group associated with UCR, RCC, and the local St. Andrew's Newman Center. We aim to help students learn more about the Catholic faith and how to practice it.

name: Evolution, Ecology, and Organismal Biology Graduate Student Association
description: To facilitate the EEOB graduate student community, intellectual exchange, and provide a forum for addressing graduate student concerns in the EEOB department at UCR.
summary: To facilitate intellectual exchange and provide a forum for addressing graduate student concerns in the Biology department at UCR.

name: Highlander Mock Trial
description:
DO YOU. . .
WANT TO BE AN ATTORNEY?
WANT TO GET BETTER AT PUBLIC SPEAKING?
WANT TO LEARN MORE ABOUT THE LAW?

JOIN THE MOCK TRIAL TEAM AT UCR!
We are a diverse group of UCR students with various interests, levels of experience, and career goals. On our team, you can be an attorney or a witness. Attorney roles are for anyone interested law, debate, or public speaking. Witness actors receive a character to personify in court- whether it be a grieving mother, a business tycoon, or the number one suspect in a murder case!

WHAT DO WE DO?
FALL: At the beginning of Fall Quarter, we recruit new members to join our team. ***No experience required.*** Together, we analyze a case given to us by The American Mock Trial Association. We then teach you everything to prepare the best case possible!

WINTER: Our team takes our case to trial! We travel to places like the University of California Los Angeles, Cal Poly San Luis Obispo, and Arizona State University to compete in huge tournaments. Each of our trials are judged by real legal professionals.

SPRING: The competitive season is over, so a second wave of recruitment allows new members to learn everything in a more relaxed environment. This gives new members a great advantage to making it onto the following year's competitive team!
summary: We are a student-run organization that enables members to train and compete in trial tournaments hosted by The American Mock Trial Association. Check out our instagram @ucrmocktrial or email us at mocktrialucr@gmail.com for more information!

name: Society of Women Engineers
description:
SWE at UCR is a professional engineering organization specializing in professional and academic development, open to all genders, and outreach opportunities by encouraging young students to pursue STEM fields. We also frequently collaborate with other engineering organizations to help expand students' professional networks. Three of our larger annual events are as follows:

  • Bourns Science and Engineering Day is the largest engineering outreach event at UCR. This past year, over 2500 children, parents, and teachers registered to participate in an all-day event with a distinguished keynote speaker, tours of the college, and exciting, interactive STEM-related activities/experiments showing students and their parents the importance of math and science.
  • Evening With Industry is a professional development event where company representatives and recruiters dine with students to share their experiences, provide industry insight, and discuss possible job/internship opportunities.
  • Research Networking Night is an academic development event where distinguished engineering professors and graduate students dine with students to discuss their research, open undergraduate research positions, and their experiences.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us, and we will be happy to provide you with more information!

Facebook Group: www.facebook.com/groups/5046061607

Instagram: swe.ucr
summary: SWE at UCR is a professional engineering organization specializing in professional and academic development, increasing the female presence in BCOE, and outreach opportunities by encouraging young students to pursue STEM fields.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc.- Xi Rho Chapter
description: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority's aim is to enhance the quality of life within the community. Public service, leadership development and education of youth are the hallmark of the organizations, programs and activities. Sigma Gamma Rho addresses concerns that impact society educationally, civically, and economically. Soaring To Greater Heights Of Attainment Around The World, Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc., as a leading national service organization, has met the challenges of the day and continues to grow through Sisterhood, Scholarship and Service. Look for updates on our facebook page and our instagram page @xirhopoodles
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority's aim is to enhance the quality of life within the community. Public service, leadership development and education of youth are the hallmark of the organizations, programs and activities.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
description:
MISSION
Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional pre-law fraternity
advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school,
the profession and the community.

VISION
We are the preeminent law fraternity promoting the bonds of fraternalism and we are the
leader in the development and advancement of professional ideals.

CORE VALUES
Bound together by tradition and our common interest in the law, we share these core values:
Integrity, Compassion, Courage, Professionalism, Service, Diversity, and Innovation.

DECLARATION OF PURPOSE
The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law
with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty
and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion
and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members;
and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a
lifetime of honorable professional and public service.

tion to professional advancements. We host an annual Lawyer Banquet in the Fall, which allows new and active members exposure to speak and network with lawyers. In Winter, we have winter retreat (a small trip we take to bond with one another)! Then, we have Spring formals and Alumni Networking in the Spring! These main events are hosted in conjunction to smaller events that help to provide students with career opportunities and professional development. The difference between Phi Alpha Delta and other pre-law organizations is our preeminent role in creating strong networks and relationships; we have a mentor mentee program, similar to a big/little program that establishes lifelong relationships with other students who are entering the legal profession as well. This dynamic may help to provide employment and internship opportunities, in addition to advice in every aspect. Many mentor mentee relationships help towards assimilating into university life and into the legal community in terms of law school or gap year opportunities. With this in mind, Phi Alpha Delta is the perfect place to begin learning about law! By providing members with events and resources, as well as individual experiences, Phi Alpha Delta is a diversified organization that provides opportunities in every aspect!
summary:
Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity
advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school,
the profession and the community.

name: Earth and Planetary Science Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a channel for communication between the graduate students, the faculty of the Department of Earth and Planetary Sciences, and the UCR Graduate Student Association (UCR GSA). The organization shall also enhance interaction and communication among the student body.
summary: The EPS-GSA represents the graduate students of the Earth and Planetary Science Department.

name: Collective Faction
description:
Looking to branch out and join a dance team? Look no further! Collective Faction is a competitive, co-ed dance team here at UCR, open to all experience levels. There’s a place for everyone here—we’re all about growth and community.

 

If you're not ready to commit to a team just yet, no worries! We also host weekly workshops open to everyone where we bring in awesome guest choreographers to teach fun, challenging pieces. The choreographers we bring are known throughout the community and have been on other teams, including Team Millennia, Hall of Fame, Natural St8, GRV, and more.

 

Make sure to check out our Instagram to stay up to date on events and learn more about the team

 

;)

 

- @collectivefaction
summary: The purpose of this dance team is to connect dancers of all experience levels and bring out the potential dancer within each of its members. This team also aims to support each member’s growth and dance journey, regardless of their experience.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc.
description:
Lambda Theta Alpha serves to provide a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect. Our goal is to provide social and cultural activities and to carry on charitable and educational programs; all while strengthening leadership, maintaining high academic standards, and ultimately serving as a voice for UCR community. 

     Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority Inc, was established at the University of California, Riverside on May of 2001 on the basis of academia achievement with goals of community and political activism. Lambda Theta Alpha’s desire for progress, dedication to its community, and the aspiration for advancement of the Universal Women, has paved the way for many successful events in strengthening women of all creeds since its establishment here at UCR. Although we are traditionally a Latina Sorority, we do not define as so, for many independent, and strong women of all races, ethnicities, and creeds have come into our organization and succeeded as educated women.

      Furthermore, amongst our many achievements we have also been recognized nationally for our philanthropy: St. Judes Research Hospital, as the Latina Sorority of the Year by NALFO, and Latinogreeks.org, and even more recently, as the winner of Voto Latino's 'Rep Ur Letters' Challenge by Successfully Implementing a Political Education Initiative in this past November’s Presidential Elections. Not to mention, we were the recipients of the 2011-2012, Scholarship Pillar Award in recognition of our commitment to outstanding academic achievements, at the Pillars Banquet this past year.

     Ultimately as an organization, Lambda Theta Alpha thrives in its academic achievement and our annual Strong Women, Strong Minds Scholarship giveaway/banquet is our biggest and most recognized event all year. It is a way for us as women, who often times do not come into the University with all the necessary resources or financial capabilities to reach our fullest academic potential, be able to unite as an organization and give back to young ladies who are continuing their academic career into the University in order to ease their financial burden a bit, and ultimately empower the Universal Woman inside them.
summary: LTA serves as a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect. To provide social and cultural activities and to carry on charitable and educational programs, while strengthening leadership, and ultimately maintaining high academic standards.

name: Providing Opportunities Dreams and Education in Riverside
description:
Our Mission:

Established in 2008, PODER seeks to provide assistance to undocumented students through mentorship, financial assistance, and community building. PODER is aware that the Riverside community lacks such resources for the immigrant population; therefore PODER serves as a pipeline of information for those in need.

Goals of PODER:

  • Provide a safe environment where undocumented students can feel at home with a group of people who understand similar struggles.
  • Educate the community about Assembly Bill 540, CA DREAM Act, Deferred Action, and immigration reform.
  • Assist undocumented students in the transition from high school or community college and provide mentorship.
  • Provide financial assistance through scholarships and/or other resources.
  • Advocate for the rights of all undocumented immigrants.

What We Do:

Annual Events:

  • “Dreaming of a Higher Education” Conference: Our annual conference brings students from the Inland Empire and Coachella Valley to UC Riverside for a day of workshops and presentations regarding AB540, CA Dream Act, DACA, Access to Higher Education, immigration policy updates, among other topics that are crucial to immigrant communities!
  • Immigrant Awareness Week: We dedicated a whole week to bring awareness to UC Riverside about immigrant communities and their intersectional identities.
  • Coming Out of the Shadows: During Immigrant Awareness Week, PODER hosts “Coming Out of the Shadows” event at the Bell Tower. Students have the safe space to come out publicly as undocumented and share their stories. On April 3rd, 2014 PODER hosted the first “Coming Out of the Shadows” event at UC Riverside to empower our undocumented peers to be “Unafraid and Unashamed”.
  • “Investing in the Dream” Fundraiser Banquet: Our annual banquet helps raise scholarship funds for undocumented students.

Other Work:

  • Workshops/ Presentations: PODER provides presentations on DACA, AB 540, CA DREAM Act and the experience of undocumented students at high schools and other community events.
  • Advocate for undocumented Students: PODER advocates for equal opportunities and resources for undocumented students. In October 2013, we were able to get ASUCR to pass a resolution in support of undocumented students. PODER also advocates for undocumented students at the University of California Office of the President through their student representative. As part of a UC wide undocumented student coalition, UC Riverside and other UC campus were allocated $5 million to support undocumented students.
summary: Established in 2008, PODER seeks to provide assistance to undocumented students through mentorship, financial assistance, and community building.

Members are given the opportunity to network and connect with fellow pre-optometry and optometry people. Members can gain hands-on experience and volunteering through community service events.

description: Established at The University of California at Riverside on November 7th, 2007, the Society of Physics Students (SPS) provides undergraduates and graduates from all disciplines the opportunity to expand and further their knowledge of physical phenomena through the following: Dynamic, interactive meetings which bring together members for the purpose of discussion of physics-related topics Organized field trips to laboratories, particle accelerators, observatories, Universities and a plethora of other institutions which demonstrate physics in the real world Philanthropy/community service, Guided research, Colloquial Projects, and group collaborations aimed to showcase the creative and inginuitive thought of every individual. It is the goal of SPS to to help students transform themselves into contributing members of the professional community or help students expand and further their knowledge. Course work develops only one range of skills. Other skills needed to flourish professionally include effective communication and personal interactions, leadership experience, establishing a personal network of contacts, presenting scholarly work in professional meetings and journals, and outreach services to the campus and local communities.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon International Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a lifelong commitment to our guiding principles of philanthropy, deity, and education through fellowship, service, mentoring, and formal training in leadership, science, and ethics. As UC Riverside's only co-educational medical fraternity, we take pride in our close camaraderie and unparalleled diversity.

UC Riverside's pre-medical chapter, formally known as the California Delta Chapter, was officially chartered in the spring of 2013. UC Riverside students with the aspiration to one day become physicians now have the opportunity to become part of a network of prestigious undergraduate universities and medical schools all over the globe. CA Delta is eagerly awaiting the chance to welcome new brothers and sisters into our fraternity so that we may build new traditions together that will last for the remainder of UC Riverside's history. 

If you are interested in more information about Phi Delta Epsilon, our California Delta Chapter, or joining our family, please feel free to contact us through our email since we limit our HighlanderLink roster to active members only.
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon is an International Co-Educational Medical Fraternity. We pride ourselves in our motto, "Facta Non-Verba, Deeds Not Words."

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity - Sigma Delta Chapter
description:
The purpose of this fraternity shall be to develop and to maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

This fraternity shall operate as a local professional engineering fraternity with the object of petitioning Theta Tau to become a colony and then a chapter of that national professional engineering fraternity. This process was started early 2012 with the founding fathers forming an interest group within the college. The interest group was then colonized on May 19th, 2012.

Now, we are pleased to announce that our chapter petition has been voted on and approved. As of April 26, 2014, we are now established as Sigma Delta Chapter.
summary: The purpose of this fraternity shall be to develop and to maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

name: California Alliance for Minority Participation
description:
Our Motto
Scientists are best developed by other scientists serving as mentors who exhibit and expect scholarly excellence.

The California Alliance for Maximizing Potential is designed for students in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics [STEM] fields pursuing graduate (Master/Doctoral) degrees.

CAMP was established at the University of California, Riverside in 1991 and is funded by the National Science Foundation [NSF].

CAMP is a program that works to encourage NSF-declared underrepresented students in the STEM fields to successfully complete undergraduate science degrees and further pursue their studies at the graduate and professional level. The National Science Foundation considers an underrepresented minority in STEM as students with Black/African-American, Hispanic/Latino, Native American, and Non-Filipino-Pacific Islander ethnic backgrounds reported at UCR at the time of admission.

CAMP exists at nine of the UC campuses and at various California State Universities, California Community Colleges, independent colleges and universities, and national laboratories that together work to achieve a goal of doubling the numbers of minority students receiving a B.S. or a B.A. degree in the many science disciplines.

 

CAMP, in addition to being a federally funded program for maximizing participation in scientific research, is a student organization that welcomes everyone! We will be revamping our meeting styles in the following school years with professional development workshops, fun social events, and faculty networking opportunities!

2025-2026 Officers:

  • President - Yuna Aguilar
  • Vice President - Talmage (TJ) Cobb
  • Treasurer - Jason Herrera
  • Social Media Coordinator - Galilea Flores
summary: The California Alliance for Maximizing Potential (CAMP) is designed for students in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics [STEM] fields pursuing graduate (Master/Doctoral) degrees.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated. is a private, not-for-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world. The organization is a sisterhood of predominantly Black, college educated women. Since its founding, more than 300,000 women have joined the organization. The sorority currently has 1,000 collegiate and alumnae chapters located in the United States, England, Japan (Tokyo and Okinawa), Germany, the Virgin Islands, Bermuda, the Bahamas, Jamaica and the Republic of Korea.

The major programs of the sorority are based upon the organization's Five Point Programmatic Thrust: Economic Development, Educational Development, International Awareness and Involvement, Physical and Mental Health, and Political Awareness and Involvement.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Associated Students of UCR
description:
Hello Highlander!

Thank you for your interest in ASUCR. In order to be apart of the ASUCR HighlanderLink’s roster, you will need to have a position in ASUCR. This can be achieved by being elected, becoming a First-Year Fellow, or becoming an intern in the office.

The Associated Students of University of California, Riverside (ASUCR) is the official undergraduate student government of UCR.

ASUCR acts as the liaison between students and administrators by providing an outlet for the student body to express concerns, questions, and suggestions about the university.

ASUCR advocates, represents, and educates the student body as official units of the university on three main levels: internally, externally, and financially.

Keep an eye on our HighlanderLink page for news and events, as well as our website, Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us!
summary: The Associated Students of University of California, Riverside (ASUCR) is the official student government at UCR representing over 18,000 undergraduates.

name: Commuter Programs at UCR
description:
Many students choose to commute to UCR, meaning that they live at home or maybe in the nearby apartment complexes. To help them in their daily lives, Commuter Programs helps to students make friends with other commuters. We also strive to educate them on campus events and services that might make their time at UCR a little easier.

We have several events that we plan during the academic year such as the Commuter Welcome, Commuter Pit Stops, Traditions Keeper events, meetups with other commuter students, and more! 

If you have questions about ways to save money on travel, nearby apartments that might be suitable for you, or what's happening around, get connected with Commuter Programs!

We're always looking for volunteers and new ideas to support commuter students, so if you have an idea or want to create an event for other commuter students, we would love to hear it! Feel free to send us an email at ucrlife@ucr.edu or message Kerri in our Discord.
summary: Commuter Programs at UCR aims to help those students that walk / bus / bike / drive / or otherwise travel to get to campus.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description:
IEEE Promotional Video

 

What is IEEE?

IEEE stands for the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers. It is abbreviated to IEEE [ahy-trip-uhl-ee] and is officially referred to as such.

Our Mission

Our mission as the local chapter is to provide engineers with technical, social, and professional development in the engineering field. We aim to be the leading engineering organization on campus, providing more opportunities for student improvement outside the classroom. We are committed in providing engineers with career enhancement, engineering social events, and growth in their respective professions.

 

Projects

Our organization hosts four student projects: Micromouse, Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), RoboSub, and Solar Car. They are all open to everyone to participate.

 

Micromouse

An autonomous robot built from scratch that can navigate through a 16x16 cell maze. Meetings and workshops are held once a week. The IEEE@UCR Micromouse teams compete in multiple collegiate competitions across Southern California.

Contact: robotics@ieeeucr.org

 

RoboSub

UCR RoboSub is one of IEEE’s large-scale projects that consists of multidisciplinary teams composed of around 20 active members that work year-long to collaboratively create an autonomous submarine from scratch used to compete in the international RoboSub competition hosted annually by RoboNation in San Diego in the summer.

Contact: robosub@ieee.edu

Website: https://robosub.ucr.edu

 

Unmanned Aerial Systems

A highly motivated group of students designing, constructing and programming an autonomous aerial vehicle to compete in the annual AUVSI-SUAS competition in Maryland. Through UCR-UAS, students have the unique opportunity to hone their engineering skills and develop a product from start to finish.

Contact: uas@ieeeucr.org

Website: https://ucr-uas.weebly.com

 

Solar Car

A multidisciplinary team comprised of highly motivated undergraduate students that aim to build a solar-powered vehicle. Their mission is to promote a future of alternative energy and sustainable transportation projects at UC Riverside, contribute to Southern California’s growing technology ecosystem, and raise community awareness of the power of solar energy.

Contact: solarcar@ieeeucr.org

Website: https://ucrsolarcar.com

 

Come Visit Us!

Our clubroom, Solarium, is located in Winston Chung Hall, Rm. 221. It's open 24/7 with card access. Closed during the summer.

We also have a Discord Server!
summary: IEEE at UCR is an organization dedicated to providing engineers with technical, social, and professional development in the engineering field.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Involvement Center
description: The Fraternity and Sorority Involvement Center (FSIC) at UC Riverside is home to dozens of fraternities and sororities that epitomize the diversity, high ideals, and excitement of a multifaceted campus community. As the center for all single-gendered fraternities and sororities at UCR, the FSIC focuses on four (4) pillars: scholarship, leadership, friendship, and service.

name: First-Year Mentor Program
description:
First-year students that register for the First Year Mentor Program are paired with a trained peer mentor (sophomore, junior, or senior student). The mentors will help first year students with their transition to the university and make them feel at home in the academic environment. Students will meet with their mentor bi-weekly throughout the entire first year as they acquire the skills needed to become successful in college.

 

If you have any questions about becoming a Mentor or having a mentor, please contact Laura Lopez at laura.lopez@ucr.edu or at 951-827-0625.

name: US Army ROTC
description:
Everyone in the UCR Cadet Recruiting team shares the aspiration to one day serve as an Officer in the United States Army. Our goal is to spread awareness of the program to any Students at UCR that share our same aspirations.

While there are many ways to become a U.S. Army Officer, ROTC is a method that allows Cadets to attend school and earn a degree while participating in the training necessary to one day commission. 

The work can be time consuming and weariness may certainly result, but the lessons learned, friendships, and accomplishments made can be invaluable for your life that lies ahead. 

It is important to note that ROTC at UCR is mostly done through California State University San Bernardino and Claremont McKenna College. This certainly serves as a means to meet people from many other schools around the Inland Empire including California Baptist University, Azusa Pacific University, Cal Poly Pomona, and many others. However, having a means of transportation can make life in the program much easier.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L4q6_sN46xQ
summary: We work to inform any UCR students about the many options and methods available to commission as an Officer in the U.S. Army and the many career fields UCR students can experience to bolster their resume for the any life plans.

name: Test Group
summary: Test

name: Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC)
description:
The Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC) serves students with permanent and temporary disabilities. Our staff will assist you with general information about all SDRC services, as well as any required paperwork and applicable documentation.

 

Services are free, voluntary and confidential to all regularly enrolled students with a verified disability. A personal consultation will help us individually tailor a plan to meet your disability-related needs to access academic and campus programs, including but not limited to classes, housing, parking, residence hall dining, etc. We are offering a new mentor program for incoming students.  We also collaborate with other campus offices and departments, and Student Disability Union.
summary: Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC) is committed to an accessible, equitable, and inclusive experience for students with disabilities through advocacy, empowerment, and collaboration with the UCR Community to reduce barriers for all students.

name: Highlander Gaming
description:
2022 HighlanderCon Recap Video

Who We Are

Founded in April 2012, Highlander Gaming is a social club at the University of California, Riverside whose aim is to promote and involve the campus and surrounding areas in esports and the gaming industry. Through Highlander Gaming, we hope to create a strong community that dedicates itself to a competitive spirit, while maintaining a strong sense of sportsmanship and social inclusion.

​

Our Story

Highlander Gaming began as "Esports at UCR" in 2012 with a small group of friends, focusing mostly on Blizzard's feature title of that time, Starcraft 2. Our organization's goals at the time was to create a community where gamers at UCR have a place to flourish and connect with each other.

 

Now, Highlander Gaming connects over 3,500 gamers on campus together, creating events such as Highlander Con that bring the entire SoCal community together. Our esports teams are one of the best in the nation, and cover games like Valorant, League of Legends, Super Smash Bros Ultimate, and more! We participate in leagues such as Collegiate Star League, cLoL, and Collegiate Esports Association, all of which cover schools from across the nation.

​

Currently Highlander Gaming’s staff consists of a dedicated group of students who are determined to put in the time and effort to make our organization's goals happen. Our officers and directors are committed to promoting the widespread acceptance of gaming as a hobby, sport, and as an art. If you have any questions or comments, please don't hesitate to contact us. Thank you for being a part of this wonderful community!

 

Website: https://www.highlandergaming.com/

Discord: discord.gg/hlg

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/hlg_ucr/

Twitter: https://twitter.com/hlg_ucr

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/hlgucr/

Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/hlgucr
summary: Welcome to Highlander Gaming! We're here to present a social and competitive atmosphere for games such as League of Legends, Valorant, Super Smash Bros Ultimate, and everything in between! Join our Discord! https://www.discord.gg/hlg

name: Sikh Student Association
description:
Vaheguru Ji Ka Khalsa, Vaheguru Ji Ki Fateh.

The Sikh Student Association (SSA) at University of California, Riverside (UCR) is an organization to create a safe and empowering space for Sikh and non-Sikh Students at UCR. We get together for sangat (company), seva (selfless service), simran (meditation) and academics. The Sikh Student Association at UCR welcomes all students from various backgrounds and makes a positive impact on its members that lasts a life time.
summary: The goal of our organization is to create a safe and empowering space for Sikh Students at UCR by hosting various community-building events, discussion nights to address social issues affecting our community, and tabling events to connect with our peers.

name: LGBT Resource Center
description:
You can sign up for our weekly E-Newsdletter if you are a UCR students, staff or faculty:

https://out.ucr.edu/newsletter

OUR MISSION STATEMENT

The LGBT Resource Center provides support, education, and advocacy regarding sexual orientation and gender identity for the UC Riverside campus community.

Our Learning Outcomes

We define our effectiveness by ensuring the following outcomes:

 Community members…

  • are able to define and describe their own identities
  • collect information about their multiple identities in order to define themselves, apply the knowledge they gain concerning their identities to other aspects of their education and personal lives, and to relate their own experiences with those of other members of the LGBT community
  •  formulate new ideas regarding the LGBT community
  • are able to explain how their multiple identities intersect within themselves and with other communities
  • are able to describe their own culture(s) and relate to them on different levels than they may have thought about before
  • will show a desire to give back to their communitie(s) by participating in LGBT related or non-LGBT related student/employee organizations
  • formulate their own sense of civic responsibility by assuming civic, political, or social responsibilities through opportunities presented by the University
  • blend their understanding of themselves with how they may fit within a society where they may be from a cultural group(s) that is marginalized
  • are able to explain and recognize important social and political issues in their own community as well as different cultural beliefs and behaviors in the larger world
  • support one another in the Center’s open, diverse and public space and learn about themselves and others in this process
  • prioritize their time and understand where their personal lives and education/career may intersect at the Center since there is always social and educational interactions occurring
  • navigate on and off campus resources with the assistance of Center staff
  • are able to receive the support that they need in order to prioritize and persist through their educational/career and personal life with staff that advocate on their behalf and truly care about their future

In addition, LGBT leaders will formulate an understanding of leadership and collaboration with other campus constituents by participating in their respective organizations and/or Center programs.

These learning outcomes are adapted from the work of the UCLA and UCSD campus LGBT resource centers.
summary: The LGBT Resource Center provides support, education, and advocacy regarding sexual orientation and gender identity for the UC Riverside campus community.

Our mission is accomplished by the following:

name: Ballet Folklorico de UCR
description:
Stay up-to-date with our most recent updates and announcements by following us on Instagram @BF_UCR and/or on Facebook!

The goals of Ballet Folklórico de UCR (BFdeUCR) are to develop performance skills, increase the dance repertoire, and educate participants on the richness of Mexican cultural and dance.  BF de UCR intends to promote cultural awareness through dance on the UCR campus community, in Riverside, and the surrounding communities. The primary way for BFdeUCR to inform others as to the Mexican heritage existing in our community is through performance. Other ways include fundraisers, attending conferences and workshops, and working together with other cultural groups. Practices are open to any student willing to join, and is updated on our social media pages, email, and our Discord chat!

Check out our Schedule of Events (practices, performances, events, etc.): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1meiQWY7ErwcJWxIRYUF3shtw_M130njmURMi3qE6A-I/edit?usp=sharing

Check out our Syllabus of Practices: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kWfwWurf3D5zXfxG4YttybLTYYQ415h35CXGyvKQOSE/edit?usp=sharing
summary: El Grupo Ballet Folklórico de UCR strives to educate audiences about Mexican Folk dance, through traditional dance, music, and ethnic attire.

name: Golden ARCHES
description:
Advocating Responsible Choices through Health Education and Support

  • Golden ARCHES is a peer health education group at UC Riverside, focused on the topics of alcohol and other drugs, safe partying, sexual health, and nutrition & fitness.
  • Golden ARCHES believes in providing UC Riverside’s students with accurate and honest health information, skills, and resources, so students can make informed decisions that enhance their personal health and well-being.
  • Golden ARCHES peers are advocates for responsible decision-making when it comes to individuals’ health, and for creating a campus community that nurtures and supports these decisions.
  • Golden ARCHES peer educators gain valuable experience in health education programming, and serve as positive role models for their peers.
summary: Golden ARCHES involves and empowers UCR students to comprehensively and effectively address a wide variety of social health, wellness and leadership issues. The organization is made up of Lead Peer Health Educators and Volunteer Health Educators.

name: The Salsa Club at UCR
description:
The purpose and mission of The Salsa Club at UCR is to create a passionate and supportive student community that aims to share the skill, knowledge, and joy of salsa dancing and related dances. We provide instruction in the basics of salsa, bachata, tango, and other latin dances which are growing in popularity within local salsa clubs and Latin dance clubs. We aim to provide an environment where practice of the above dances can be executed, to provide a social network that focuses on dancing events and activities, and to provide a portal for individuals with similar interests to meet and interact. 

This organization originally began back in the summer of 2001 when some of the UCR student members of the Ballroom and Swing Club of UCR (BaSC) branched off towards a love of salsa. The club lasted several strong years before losing active status due to various issues, but was reformed in Fall of 2012 by UCR students who were inspired to give new life to the Salsa Club at UCR after hearing stories from the club alumni of their life-changing experiences during their time in the organization.

Our goal as the new generation of the Salsa Club at UCR is to ensure a continued, thriving existence for the club with the addition of a very supportive network of alumni in the salsa community as a way for the members of the Riverside Salsa Family to continue growing and supporting this gateway to a life-long gift of dance, friendship, and perhaps love!

 

We meet every Friday from 5:00-7:00pm at SRC north MPR B! No need to register in advance! See you there!
summary: Our club aims to share our knowledge of salsa dance and related styles (Bachata, Tango, Cumbia, etc). Our group provides dance instruction, practice, as well as a network that knows and provides many community social dance opportunities.

In addition, our goal is to service the community that exemplifies art in a socially responsible way.

name: Chicano Student Programs
description:
¡BIENVENIDOS A NUESTRA FAMILIA DE UCR!

We celebrate the traditions of Chicano/Latino culture while sharing the spirit of La Raza with our campus and community. To our Chicano/Latino students, we are one big familia, and like a family, we fully support your academic, cultural and social success.

WE:

SUPPORT - We create a positive environment for Chicano/Latino students at UCR by offering supportiveservices and programs that encourage academic success and retention. We provide service referral when necessary, and serve as a resource for advocacy, as well.

SHARE - We educate our campus and community about Chicano/Latino culture through our exciting events, active student organizations, and rewarding outreach programs, too.

CONNECT - We collaborate with other UCR departments with shared goals; encourage faculty and staffmentorship; reach out to our local community; and maintain a helpful campus, community and alumni network that help our students grow.

INSPIRE- Our outstanding faculty, staff and students inspire future members of our familia and demonstrate true orgullo y corazón.

Programs that need you: 

  • Chicano Link Peer Mentor Program
  • Community Outreach
  • Nuestra Cosa - Chicano/Latino Newspaper
  • Radio Aztlan on KUCR 88.3 FM
  • Student Organizations 
  • UCR Chicano Latino Alumni
  • Volunteer Event Committes

Visit us or contact our office for more information.
summary: ¡Bienvenidos a nuestra familia de UCR! We celebrate the traditions of Chicano/Latino culture while sharing the spirit of La Raza with our campus and community. We are one big familia, and like a family, we fully support your success at UCR!

name: Chicano Link Peer Mentor Program
description:
FIND A MENTOR:

Are you a new student? We can pair you with a trained peer mentor who can help you to:

  • Build a strong network of support
  • Navigate UCR and learn about your campus and community resources
  • Connect with clubs and organizations
  • Attain major-specific advice on professors, projects and key opportunities
  • Hone your study and time management skills
  • Develop cultural awareness
  • Adjust to campus life

If you are interested in being a mentee in our Chicano Link Peer Mentor Program, apply here or follow the link go.ucr.edu/chicanolink.   

 

 BE A MENTOR

Are you a current student interested in becoming a mentor? Your role as a Chicano Link Peer Mentor is to connect with new UCR students. You would serve as a positive role model/mentor and assist incoming students with their transition to UCR and connect them to our UCR familia.  It is important that incoming students receive information on campus support services like Chicano Student Programs.

Chicano Link Peer Mentors must...

  • Be a UCR student (junior or higher) with a cumulative and quarter GPA of 2.5 or higher
  • Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Be aware of issues specifically related to Chicano/Latino students
  • Be open-minded and flexible
  • Be energetic, motivated, and positive
  • Be willing to learn, grow and attend training as needed
  • Work with mentees for at least 30 minutes every two weeks

 

Apply today and discover how rewarding mentorship can be. If you are interested in volunteering as a Chicano Link Peer Mentor, you can bring your application to Chicano Student Programs. 

APPLY to be a MENTEE or MENTOR: You can apply here.
summary: Our program extends a bienvenida into the UCR familia to first year undergraduate and graduate students. One-on-one meetings, study sessions and academic workshops all support a positive transition to UCR and promote new students’ success.

name: Campus Safety Escort Service
description:
The Campus Safety Escort Service (CSES) dispatches from the first floor of the HUB and the Tomas Rivera Library every Sunday through Thursday, from dark to 11:30 p.m.  To request a Safety Escort, call (951) 827-3772, or use the red direct-dial telephone located on the first floor of most campus buildings.  When the Service is not in operation, your call will be answered by the Campus Police.  You can also contact UCRPD directly at (951) 827-5222 for an escort.

Interested in volunteering?  Apply online at www.escortservice.ucr.edu
summary: Trained UCR student volunteers escort you to your car, on campus or nearby campus destination at night.

name: Graduate Student Association Teacher Education Program Student Representative Committee
description: This organization represents students of the Teacher Education Program at UC Riverside. Our aim is to engage in student affairs and take on leadership roles in our campus community while pursuing careers as future educators.
summary: The TEP SRC provides a voice for, and advocates on behalf of the Graduate School of Education TEP's students. We serve as a medium of communication between the UCR GSE administration, the UCR GSA, and all five cohorts within the GSE TEP.

name: Fencing at UCR
description:
Fencing at UCR aims to teach and practice the sport of Fencing at UCR. We have 2-3 practices a week in the UCR REC center, and compete in intercollegiate tournaments against UCI, UCLA, UCSB, ASU, Caltech, and more. If you are interested in joining, just stop by during practice or contact: ucrfencingclub@gmail.com

Practice: 8:30pm Mondays SRC room E and Wednesdays at SRC room A.

No experience necessary
summary: Fencing at UCR aims to teach and practice the sport of Fencing at UCR. We have 2-3 practices a week in the UCR rec center, and compete intercollegiately. If you are interested in joining, just stop by during practice or contact: ucrfencingclub@gmail.com

name: Central American Student Alliance
description:
Central American Student Alliance (CASA) is a family of students committed to preserving Central American history, culture, and identity. We work to build a supportive community that celebrates our heritage and empowers unity through workshops and cultural engagement. 

CASA's mission is to foster community, represent, and empower the Central American student body. We affirm identities, cultures, and histories that are often overlooked in higher education, and we commit to creating a space rooted in belonging, resistance, and empowerment.

We’d love to have you join us!
summary: Central American Student Alliance (CASA) is a student-led organization that empowers and unites the Central American Community at UCR. The mission is to bring cultural awareness and build space for students that identify as Central American.

name: Active Minds
description:
Active Minds

Active Minds is a UCR student-lead group committed to helping you feel your best and removing the stigma surrounding mental health. We provide guest speakers, information tables and outreach programs to help you manage stress and find help when needed. You'll get real answers from peer mentors you can relate to, feel safe and connected, while living well and taking part in fun and rewarding activities.

If you are interested in volunteering with us, our applications will be available during Week 8. Applications are open quarterly. If you have any questions, please email us at activeminds@ucr.edu.
summary: Reduce the stigma that often surrounds mental health issues on college campuses by increasing awareness, coordinating interactive events, and providing mental health and wellness resources and support.
