name: Stress Busters Peer Educators
description:
The Stressbusters are undergraduate students trained by the UCR Counseling and Psychological Services to provide stress and time management, relaxation,and sleep hygiene workshops, as well as tabling events, to the campus community. The workshops are fun, interactive, and relaxing. Our services are available for FREE to any group on campus, including clubs and organizations, residential halls, fraternities/sororities, among others. You are welcome to contact us at Ccpeers@ucr.edu or visit our website below for more information on requesting a workshop or tabling event for your group. We look forward to hearing from you!

Please visit our website: http://counseling.ucr.edu/education-training/Pages/StressBusterPeerEducators.aspx.
summary: When stress strikes, call the StressBusters! We are available to present workshops on stress management, relaxation, time management, and sleep hygiene for your campus org. To schedule a presentation, email your request to ccpeers@ucr.edu.

name: Biofeedback Technicians
summary: Welcome to the Biofeedback Technicians Page!!! The Biofeedback Program is one of many services offered by the Counseling Center. As a technician, you will develop the skills to teach clients various relaxation techniques to manage stress and anxiety.

name: TRIO Scholars
description:
The mission of the University of California, Riverside TRiO Scholars Program is to promote the academic, social, personal, and professional success of all our students by building a strong sense of community and drawing on resources both within and outside the program.  We strive to provide students with tools and support to succeed and excel at UC Riverside and beyond.

TRiO programs have a rich history dating back to 1964, and continue to open the door of opportunity and access to higher education for eligible students across the country regardless of race, ethnicity, or culture.

The TRiO Scholars Program, also known as Student Support Services (SSS) is a federally funded by a grant from the U.S. Department of Education to improve the retention and graduation rates of first-generation and income –qualified students and students with disabilities at UC Riverside.
summary: The mission of the UCR TRiO Scholars Program is to promote the academic, social, personal, and professional success of all students by building a strong sense of community and drawing on resources both within & outside the program.

name: Geology Student Organization
description:
This organization strives to motivate students to learn about their surroundings and local geology regardless of major. We accomplish this goal through informative trips that unite us with nature either through hiking, rock hounding, or camping trips. 

GSO is also a resource for students. Members can obtain advice on classes and even assistance on any geology related coursework. In addition, we encourage students to network with faculty, peers, and local geoscience professionals to gain insight into this underrepresented field.

If you appreciate and enjoy learning about the outdoors, then join us on our next trip! We welcome all students.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to advocate and promote geology amongst students in the major and non-majors alike.

name: Residence Hall Association
description: Residence Halls Association (RHA) is a student run organization that operates under Resident Life and Housing Services at UCR. RHA works closely with both of these departments to ensure that the greater needs and desires of the residents are heard and communicated by the organization to the respective department. We are the voice of the residents, and work in many areas to fulfill that responsibility. Most members describe RHA as the student govenment within the Residence Halls. Our goal is to make the residents’ stay here comfortable, fun, and memorable. Whether it is by hosting numerous social and educational events, granting residents their facility needs, or even lending a helping hand to the individual or other organizations, we are glad to do it. Joining RHA is easy. All you have to do is come to a RHA senate meeting that happens once a week in your residence hall. Just click on the Residence Halls tab above to find out when your RHA senate meetings is happening in your residence hall. We look foward to seeing you at our next meeting!
summary: Residence Halls Association (RHA) is a student run organization that operates under Resident Life and Housing Services at UCR. RHA works closely with both of these departments to ensure that the greater needs and desires of the residents are heard.

name: UCR Recreation
description:
Visit us at the SRC and let fit happen to you!

UCR’s newly expanded Student Recreation Center (SRC) is bigger and better than ever — and it’s all yours! As a registered UCR student, you are automatically a member of this on-campus, 155,000-square-foot facility.

  • 21,000-square-foot weight area: Includes 9 Olympic platforms
  • 100 cardio machines: With Internet and virtual courses
  • Fitness classes: Zumba, yoga, TRX and cycling offered daily
  • 237,000-gallon pool: Lap swimming, lessons and scuba
  • Drop-in: Basketball, volleyball and badminton
  • Intramural sports and clubs: Soccer, softball and flag-football
  • Recreation classes: Including martial arts, dance and bagpipes
  • "The Rock": 32-foot climbing wall and 12 ½- foot bouldering area
summary: The Student Recreation Center's mission is to enhance mental, physical and social development through recreational services, programs and facilities.

name: Latino Medical Student Association +
description:
The Latino Medical Student Association Plus (LMSA+) at UCR aims to support all pre-health students through professional and academic development experiences.

Our chapter offers various events, workshops, conferences, and more each quarter to ensure pre-health students are best prepared for their journey post-undergraduate school. 

To be an active member, apply at the start of fall quarter! Active members get to know first of all the opportunities that become available and more!

Our chapter is the home of 2 mentorship programs: The Undergraduate Mentorship Program (UMP) & the Medical School Mentorship Program (MSMP). Applications open once a year in the Fall. 

To learn more about our chapter, visit our website or stay up to date with our socials!
summary: Created to support all pre-health students at UC Riverside.

name: The Barn
description:
As our casual dining location, the Barn is UCR's social hub where students, faculty and staff meet for lunch. Come and visit our inviting patio for a relaxed study over lunch, or chat with friends in our cozy dining room.

Check out our Western-themed menu featuring salads, burgers, pizza & grilled sandwiches.

Do you need a special venue for a large catered event
summary: As our casual dining location, the Barn is UCR's social hub where students, faculty and staff meet for lunch. Come and visit our inviting patio for a relaxed study over lunch, or chat with friends in our cozy dining room.

name: Inland Empire Scholar Program
description: Students who are a part of this scholar program are those with passion to succeed and graduate here from UCR. This program targets those of Hispanic background who are in the mind set of persevering.
summary: The Inland Empire Scholars are students who will succeed in attaining their education and help make a difference in today's world.

name: Middle Eastern Student Center
description: The Middle Eastern Student Center fosters intellectual exchange, encourages the study of Middle Eastern culture and history, and promotes the advancement of higher education. We seek engagement and civil discourse as a means to promote mutual understanding among our students. The MESC staff creates alliances and methods of meaningful interaction to meet the needs of the Middle Eastern student population at UCR.
summary: The Middle Eastern Student Center

name: Asian Pacific Student Programs
description: We are Asian Pacific Student Programs: We are a community of Asian and Pacific Islander students, staff, interns and peer mentors dedicated to preserving and promoting the rich cultural fabric of diversity at UCR. When we face challenges, when we celebrate, we do so together. We build programs, events and social activities around three guiding principles: leadership, consciousness, and community. We foster open and educational dialogue that respects and embraces our unique histories and cultural identity. We encourage the academic and professional excellence of all UCR students by providing a peer-mentoring program, internship opportunities through an alumni network, and a safe welcoming place where anyone can study or just hang out. Through involvement at APSP, students become leaders — and leaders become decision makers — shaping a path for UCR and beyond!
summary: Staff and students work together at APSP to plan and implement activities, events, and programs for you. We also provide a strong community of support, a safe and welcoming environment, and a positive, culturally rich college experience.

name: Highlander Union
description: The Highlander Union is a gathering place where you and your friends can meet, eat, relax and study. It features dining and retail facilities, meeting areas, lounges and event spaces. The HUB also provides offices for student government, student organizations, and student affairs. But, if you have a question or need directions to your next campus destination, ask a service associate at the Welcome Desk, located on the first floor of the main building.
summary: The Highlander Union is a gathering place where you & your friends can meet, eat, relax & study. The HUB has its own  programs team who produce events within the HUB area, including Latitude 55, and events such as Chalk the Walk & Heat Music Festival.

name: International Students and Scholars
description: For over 50 years, the International Students and Scholars office has offered a place for UCR students to engage in a full range of international activities. ISS serves as a home for international students and their families from over 60 countries by helping them adjust to U.S. culture and life at UCR, as well as by providing expert advice and guidance on how to navigate complex immigration regulations and processes. Stop by our office or visit our website to get involved. To sign up for the R'World newsletter, email Shanon Langlie at Shanon.Langlie@ucr.edu.
summary: The International Students and Scholars office supports and promotes the success, wellness, and personal growth of international students through expert advising, intercultural programming and advocacy.

name: African Student Programs
description:
Born from the historic struggles against oppression in all forms, African Student Programs was created in 1972 to sustain a socially just and inclusive campus community. At a time when students of African descent experienced a lack of support due to low numbers in population, African Student Programs served as a safe haven for students to congregate, discuss, plan and share as a community. As people of the African Diaspora, we honor our multiple identities and cultures and advocate for their inclusion in defining the values of the university.

 Recognizing the connection between individual academic success, social responsibility and community success, we promote multiple opportunities for students and the University of California, Riverside to engage in continuing growth as a learning community. 

 Our purpose is to promote academic excellence by providing opportunities for students in their development as role models, leaders, scholars and professionals.

 In the context of the campus and student populations we were created to serve, we carry out our mission by providing the following:

·         Academic enrichment and development

·         Student leadership development

·         Non-academic advising, counseling, and crisis intervention

·         Educational and cultural programming

·         The Academic Mentorship Program

·         Assessment and enrichment of campus climate

·         Community outreach and involvement

·         Collaborative programming with student organizations, academic departments, faculty and alumni
summary: African Student Programs is a campus resource open to all students. Whether you want to get involved, attend a cultural event, volunteer, learn about a different culture or receive assistance, we are here for you!

name: Native American Student Programs
description: Welcome to Native American Student Programs, where you can unite as a community with other American Indian students at UC Riverside. This is your home away from home - a place of support, friendship, and belonging. Celebrate your native culture. Find answers to your questions. Get help and encouragement with school work. Find an internship. Get involved in events, activities, and UCR student groups. Hang out. Have fun. This is your place on campus. Welcome home.
summary: Welcome to Native American Student Programs, where you can unite as a community with other American Indian students at UC Riverside. This is your home away from home - a place of support, friendship, and belonging. while celebrating your native culture.

name: Project RISHI at UCR
description:
In partnership with local community members and social enterprises, we identify issues that are central to the villages we support and then provide the resources to implement solutions. We do this by fundraising throughout the year in hopes of being able to afford important supplies for the village and by taking trips as a group every summer to first-hand offer help, deliver supplies, and implement new helpful systems. UCR's chapter of the organization works specifically with a village Uttarakhand. Every year we continue to build off of our efforts in hopes of one day bringing this village to a place where they can support themselves

Project RISHI
summary: We are a national non-profit organization that works to promote the sustainable growth and development of rural Indian villages.

name: STEM Connections Club
description:
Our Mission:

The mission of the STEM Connections Club is to foster academic excellence, community, and professional growth among STEM students, with a particular focus on supporting community college transfer students within the College of Natural and Agricultural Sciences (CNAS). We are dedicated to creating an inclusive and collaborative environment that promotes meaningful connections across STEM disciplines.

Through peer mentorship, skill-building workshops, academic transition support, and social engagement opportunities, STEM Connections empowers students to navigate the challenges of university life, strengthen their sense of belonging, and thrive both academically and personally. Our organization is committed to improving student retention, cultivating leadership, and uniting STEM majors through shared learning experiences, interdisciplinary collaboration, and a strong culture of support.

Values:

Inclusion: We are committed to cultivating an inclusive environment that embraces diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, ensuring all STEM students feel welcomed, valued, and supported.

Academic Excellence: We promote rigorous learning, intellectual curiosity, and academic engagement by encouraging collaboration, skill-building, and continuous growth.

Community & Belonging: We believe student success is strengthened through connection. We foster camaraderie, peer support, and meaningful relationships across STEM disciplines.

Leadership & Empowerment: We empower students to develop leadership skills, pursue research and professional opportunities, and become advocates for themselves and their communities.

Collaboration & Service: We value collaboration with faculty, campus partners, and student organizations to enhance access to resources and create impactful programming that supports student success.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to foster the academic and social growth of community college CNAS and STEM transfer students.

name: Hallyu At UCR
description:
To join, you can find us on Instagram under @hallyucr and for more information and updates you can join our discord: https://discord.com/invite/r35JRB9

Welcome to HallyUCR

Intro to Hallyu

Purpose of the Organization

1. To carry out the goals mentioned in the Preamble, as well as the rest of the constitution.
2. To bring awareness towards the increasing popularity of the K-pop culture in North America, specifically the University of California, Riverside.
3. To encourage and support the students in their pursuit of cultural and social interests.

Name of Organization

The official name of this organization is Hallyu At UCR. This is a combination of “hallyu” and UCR. Hallyu is a word for the spread of the “Korean Wave” or South Korean culture. We plan to provide a space for people to make new connections and learn more about the culture.
summary: Hallyu at UCR's mission is to promote the awareness and development of K-pop culture through cultural,educational, and social means. We aim to provide a social outlet for students with similar interests and to celebrate the Korean heritage.

name: Asian Americans of Riverside Theatre
description:
Asian Americans of Riverside Theatre provides a space for unrecognized and under-appreciated groups in mainstream media, with an emphasis on Asian-American voices, to tell stories through theatrical and film arts.

We strive to welcome all artists and storytellers of all backgrounds to share their creativity and vision, no matter the level of experience or background.

Our organization focuses on giving students the opportunity to collaborate on creating theatrical and filmed performances while also traveling as a community to view and appreciate the performing arts.

We run workshops, put on productions, and hold talkbacks with artists currently working in the industry. 

As a student-led non-profit, we accept donations through our GoFundMe to help our organization's efforts to keep the show running! No funds are put to any independent or personal gains; all funds go toward funding production aspects, including (but not limited to) costumes, props, and licensing, as well as nurturing a healthy environment for our community through field trips in consuming and analyzing artistry.
summary: Celebrating diverse voices through theater and film. Welcome to AART!

name: Hylander Financial Group
description: Hylander Financial Group is UCR's premier investment finance student organization. Our goal is to provide students with invaluable hands-on portfolio management, equity research, and investment experiences through our two student managed investment funds. Students of all disciplines and levels of financial expertise are welcome to come learn about the basics of investing and the field of finance. Through our educational, social, and career-related events along with our professional competitions and portfolio teams, HFG serves as a valuable opportunity for students to elevate their careers and financial prowess. Our general meetings are open to anyone interested and are on Mondays from 7 pm to 8 pm, followed by HSIF from 8 pm to 9 pm. We look forward to seeing you!
summary: Founded in 2013, Hylander Financial Group (HFG) was established to help UCR students learn successful investing techniques, build networks in finance, and provide practical knowledge to the UCR community about the financial industry.

name: The Highlander Newspaper
description: The Highlander is UC Riverside's student-run newspaper. The Highlander Newspaper is committed to the pursuit of truth, the free exchange of information and ideas, and maintaining a fair and independent student voice. T
summary: Welcome to the Highlander newspaper's HighlanderLink page! If you're interested in joining, stop by our meeting every Monday at 5:15 p.m. in HUB 101.

name: The Deejays and Vinylphiles Club
description:
Our Story

The DVC at UCR was started by Richard Nguyen (DJ Simplex) in Jan. 2014 as a means to create UCR's first network of deejays. A club extended from San Diego to Riverside, the DVC brings people together with a similar passion for music. Today, the club continues to act as a network, while additionally providing new and aspiring deejays the lessons and experience required to become a great deejay.

In the past, DVC has played at UCR's Block Party, Heat Music Festival and Spring Splash concerts. Our alumni & current deejays have gone on to deejay at Coachella, Sevilla of Riverside, Teregram Ballroom Los Angeles and Brownies & Lemonade.

 

Who We Are

No prior deejay, music production or music experience is required to join DVC at UCR. General meetings range from discussions about deejay philosophy to mixing demonstrations to modern music analyzation to marketing yourself as an artist at UC Riverside.

DVC also hosts a mentorship/house programs, where members are assigned into "houses", to better streamline learning and artist growth. The house system also creates a sense of competitiveness in the club, while the general events creates a family around the DVC community.

DVC holds in-house competitions, production workshops and general mixing sessions to showcase the art of deejaying and music. DVC also hosts events called Fireplaces; live-streamed events where DVC members socialize and play out their diverse music choices.

For more information about our events, booking a DVC deejay or meeting agenda, please email: dvcucr@gmail.com
summary: The Deejays and Vinylphiles Club will provide educational sessions and workshops which will elaborate on DJ techniques and skills. We are like minded individuals that share a passion for music and its incorporation into the DJ art form.

name: The Tzu Chi Collegiate Association at UCR
description:
The Tzu Chi Collegiate Association at UCR (Tzu Ching) is the collegiate chapter of a larger foundation known as the Tzu Chi Foundation. Tzu Chi Foundation is a non-profit international charitable organization founded in 1966 by Dharma Master Cheng Yen in the impoverished east coast of Taiwan. The Foundation has dedicated itself in the field of charity, medicine, education, environmental protection, as well as the promotion of humanistic values and community volunteerism in Taiwan and globally for over forty years. Today, Tzu Chi has nearly ten million members around the world.

Tzu Ching directly translates to “youths with compassion and joyous giving.” UCR Tzu Ching members thus engage in the Tzu Chi spirit of compassion by carrying out the Tzu Chi missions of Charity, Medicine, Education, and Culture on campus. Tzu Ching members desire to bring love and care to all people we meet and help. We support one another on the path to becoming better people through retreats, camps, study groups, sharing events, and volunteering. We hope college students, in their prime years to grow as leaders and advocates for change, can gain more knowledge about their own community, as well as a deeper respect for all people. Everyone is welcome to join so that we can take action through compassions and wisdom.
summary: A volunteer organization that aims to educate and spread the compassion of individuals by exposing them to diverse volunteer opportunities, including fields in charity, medicine, education, environmental protection, and humanistic culture.

name: Phi Beta Kappa
summary: please update

name: English Majors Association
description:
https://youtu.be/tDmntV68ccQ

 

The purpose of the English Majors Association (EMA) is a group open to all students regardless of major aiming to promote positive interactions amongst the students and faculty within the English Department, create an active literary community, and increase participation in both on-campus and off-campus English related events and activities. The English Majors Association extends its membership opportunity once per quarter in the hopes of increasing interconnectivity amongst students. With passion and dedication put forth towards the English discipline, the EMA actively seeks out substantial opportunities for student involvement and recognition amongst peers and faculty. Increased participation and influence in the English department is available to members of the EMA, as well as an open-minded environment which values individual intellect, opinion, and creativity.
summary: The English Majors Association (EMA) provides an active literary community that fosters ideas, awareness, activities and friendships at UCR. Our focus is the study of the English discipline, and we strive to make an impact through the literary discourse.

name: Destino
description:
For Spring 2026

We meet every Tuesday @ 7pm in SSC 109

Park in Lot 1 if you have a permit.

Check us our on Instagram! @destinoucr for updates and events AND/OR contact ereye107@ucr.edu for more information!

Our Mission
To journey with students and faculty to follow Jesus and fulfill their God-given destiny.

Our Vision
We envision spiritual of Latino students, faculty, and community everywhere, so that everyone on earth knows someone who truly follows Jesus.

Our Values

We value faith, growth and fruitfulness in a compassionate community committed to being

-Culturally connected.
-Academically achieving.
-Financially responsible.
-Empowered spiritually.

Our Roots 

Destino is a ministry of Cru, and so our roots stretch back to the founding of Campus Crusade for Christ in 1951.
summary: Raising up a generation of Latino leaders to change the world!! God has a purpose for your life! ¡Es tu Destino!

name: Secret Swords and Sorcery Society
description:
This is a group for people who want to play roleplaying games, like Dungeons and Dragons, Shadowrun, Pathfinder, and Warhammer 40k. Our club provides a space for both new and veteran players to come together and group up for sessions, and offers a place to exchange new and creative ideas for the tabletop community. For more information, feel free to email our president, Arya Patel, at 'apate238@ucr.edu'. If you don't receive a response in 8 hours, feel free to send another.

Meeting Times Spring 2024: 6:00pm at HUB 265 Tuesdays and Fridays. Check the club's discord for updates on each quarter.

https://discord.gg/zduVcpz

Currently, the club offers existing games of Dungeons and Dragons 5th Edition, Middle Earth Roleplaying, Shadowrun, World/Chronicles of Darkness (Vampire the Masquerade/Requiem, Werewolf the Apocalypse/Forsaken, Mage the Ascension/Awakening, etc) and the Warhammer 40k War-game (Deathwatch and More). We're always looking to try new systems amidst our various groups, so if you've got a fun and interesting idea and the desire to run or play in a game, feel free to come on by and join us!
summary:
The purpose of this group is to organize tabletop and live role playing games such as D&D.

As of Spring 2024, 
Meetings Times: 6:00pm to 10:00pm Tuesdays and Fridays
Place: Hub 265

Check out our Discord for more info!
 https://discord.gg/53Sp6Qz

name: Physician Associates of Tomorrow
description:
The purpose of this organization is to educate students about the role of Physician Assistants (PA) in the medical field and the requirements for PA schools. We guide and prepare students to become better PA school applicants by providing information, networking and volunteer opportunities. Our goal is to bring students closer to becoming a Physician Assistant. 

 

Please follow our instagram for regular updates about events and opportunities: @PATATUCR
summary: The purpose of this organization is to educate students about the role of Physician Assistants (PA) in the medical field and the requirements for PA schools.  We aim to bring students closer to becoming a Physician Assistant.

name: Gamespawn at UCR
description:
Hello everyone! Welcome to Gamespawn, a professional engineering organization within BCOE for UCR students who are interested in video game development. We educate students in skills related to game art, programming, writing, and sound design through interactive workshops. Our organization is here to introduce the different aspects of game development and its relation to the game industry. Every year, members pitch game ideas and form groups to develop their project pitches into playable video games! We also hold game jams for people that want to experience game development, but might not have the time commitment for a long-term project. To stay up to date on Gamespawn news and updates please join our Discord! Students with any level of interest, experience, and background are welcome to join our club and our projects :)

 

Join us on our Discord server here!

 

Looking to get involved? We're looking for another Social Media Coordinator for the 2025-2026 academic year! Join our Discord and message Jordan or Kian to learn more!
summary: Welcome to Gamespawn, a professional engineering organization within BCOE with a focus on video game development! We host projects, events, industry speakers, and workshops in a variety of disciplines related to game design. All levels/majors welcome!

name: R'Stage Productions
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide students of all majors an opportunity to participate in the creation of a theatrical production. Actors, directors, writers, stage managers, and designers can all find a home. R'Stage Productions welcomes students of all backgrounds to build new relationships and provides a means for artistic collaboration, as well as professional experience in the realm of theatre.
summary: Student actors, directors, writers, stage managers, and designers of all majors are welcome. R'Stage Productions aims to showcase student work and provide a means for artistic collaboration between students.

name: Iranian Graduate Student Association
description: The Iranan Graduate Student Association (IGSA) is a non-political voluntary student organization whose objective is to sponsor Iranian social and cultural activities and events, to promote an understanding of Iranian culture, to help foster friendship among different cultural groups, and to provide a source of union and support for the Iranian community at UCR.
summary: IGSA is a voluntary student organization with the objective of promoting Iranian culture by sponsoring and holding social events, and to create a platform to introduce Persian culture to the non-Persian community.

name: CHASS F1RST
description:
Welcome to CHASS F1RST: First Year Experience in the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences. Our priority is your success. We offer a broad range of academic learning experiences from the National Award winning year-long CHASS Connect program to the Fall only Gateway Lecture and the First Year Learning Communities. Our programs fulfill college breadth requirements while preparing you for success and the University of California, Riverside and beyond.

Stay tunned for our CHASS Annual Theme Lectures and Film Series throughout the school year!!

If you'd like to visit the CHASS F1RST office, come on over to Humanities and Social Sciences Building 1609.

Nelly Cruz
