summary: CHASS F1RST provides students with the tools, opportunities, and experiences to be successful at UCR and beyond.

name: CHASS Transfers First Program
description:
Transfers F1RST provides workshops, resources, and mentoring to ensure that students are equipped for a successful transition. The Transfers F1RST Program holds workshops that assist students by raising awareness of campus resources and academic activities. The workshops promote effective educational practices and campus engagement, which increases transfer students' chances of success at the University of California, Riverside.

For more information, please contact: 

Stella Rocha, CHASS Transfers FIRST

transfers1st@ucr.edu
summary: CHASS Transfers F1RST Program aims to provide useful information and resources to help incoming first-year transfer students navigate their life at UCR.

name: Case Manager
description:
If you're struggling with an issue, whether it’s medical, psychological, psychiatric, social, financial or academic, a Case Manager is here to offer a listening ear, help you brainstorm, problem solve, and ultimately connect you to the right resources either on or off campus. The Case Manager works closely with the Counseling Center, Student Health Services, Resident Housing, Student Conduct and Academic Integrity, Student Special Services, UC Police Department, the AVC Dean of Students office, etcetera.

So, don’t delay. Please schedule your one-on-one appointment with a Case Manager today.
summary: Are you looking to make some cool friends? Are you experiencing financial hardships? Are you feeling stressed out about classes/grades? Do you need medical, psychological, or psychiatric assistance? A Case Manager can meet with you to go over your options

name: Men's Club Water Polo at UCR
description:
We practice every Monday and Wednesday (8:00-10 PM) @ the UCR Student Recreation Center Lap Pool. 

To join, just show us at the pool, we welcome everyone, no requirements of any skills or techniques. Or simply talk to Jack (hnie003@ucr.edu) and the UCR Waterpolo instagram account @ucr_mens_wp

We have full scrimmages and play in tournaments with other clubs. DM us on Instagram @ucr_mens_wp for any informations of questions!
summary:
Have fun, get in shape, learn water polo skills, scrimmage, compete!

Active on instagram @ucr_mens_wp
DM us!

name: A. Gary Anderson Graduate School of Management Student Association
description: AGSMSA strives to enhance the value of the A. Gary Anderson Graduate School of Management experience by creating student-centered programs, services, and involvement opportunities which celebrate diversity, advertise the UCR AGSM branding, promote alumni retention, foster leadership, civic engagement, and student development. We develop and enhance communication and interaction between students, faculty, administrators, alumni, business leaders, and our broader network of contacts.
summary: The AGSM student association aims to enhance the graduate experience and lay a foundation for growth by supporting a network of students, faculty, staff, alumni and the UCR community.

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
The American Medical Women’s Association (AMWA) is an organization of women physicians, medical students, undergraduate students, and other individuals dedicated to serving as the unique voice for women’s health and the advancement of women in medicine. The organization was founded by Dr. Bertha VanHoosen in 1915 in Chicago, at a time when women physicians were an under-represented minority. To redress the decline in the percentage of women in senior leadership positions, AMWA has carried out ambitious advocacy efforts including research, constituency building, mentoring, leadership development, and policy reform to enable environmental and institutional transformation.

 

AMWA functions at the local, national, & international level to advance women in medicine & improve women’s health by providing & developing leadership, advocacy, education, expertise, mentoring, & strategic alliances. As a pre-med branch of the nationally established American Medical Women’s Association at UCR, our purpose is to bring together those in medicine and health-related fields on campus to promote women’s health and to support members’ professional and personal development. We also provide two mentorship programs, a MD one with the AMWA UCR School of Medicine, and a DO one with the Western University of Health Sciences, College of Osteopathic Medicine, to allow our members to form connections with current medical students. We hope to provide all our members with philanthropic opportunities and events to help them become active members of the community and go on to serve as our future physicians.

 

To join us, become a member here on Highlander Link! Stay updated on what's going on by following us on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/amwa_ucr/! Be sure to join our GroupMe as well: https://groupme.com/join_group/63566563/qn7eejFB!

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Eu-XmhSg1rw
summary: To bring together those in Medicine and Health-related fields to promote Women’s Health and to support members’ professional and personal development.

name: Change in Scientific Importance for Youth
description: Change in Scientific Importance for Youth, also known as "Delta SIFY," is a movement intended to provide immersive educational opportunities for students in underserved communities to explore the fields of STEM. We believe that access to a quality, fulfilling education should be a fundamental human right, not a privilege.
summary: Delta SIFY aims to provide immersive educational opportunities for students in underserved communities to explore the fields of STEM. We believe that access to a quality, fulfilling education should be a fundamental human right, not a privilege.

name: National Black Law Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide community support for students who desire to obtain a deeper understanding of the legal field through various services such as mentorship opportunities with a local bar association, the opportunity to shadow practitioners of the law who encourage academic excellence, as well as to provide the essential tools needed to assist students in their advancements into the professional community. This club is not limited to those that are in pursuit of a J.D., but for everyone intrested in law and related fields.
summary: Established to unite students with their peers who have a similar interest in the legal field, to provide students a better understanding of careers within the legal field, and to give opportunities for mentorship with current practitioners in law.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description:
About Beta Alpha Psi

Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses with over 300,000 members initiated since Beta Alpha Psi's formation.

 

Purpose

The purpose of Beta Alpha Psi is to:

  • Recognize outstanding academic achievements in the field of accounting, finance, and information systems;
  • Promote the study and practice of professional fields related to these disciplines;
  • Provide opportunities for self-development and association among members and practicing financial professionals;
  • Encourage a sense of ethical, social, and public responsibilities

 

Vision

Beta Alpha Psi will shape the financial and business information professions by developing members into ethical, professional, and confident leaders.

Mission

The mission of Beta Alpha Psi, the premier international honor and service organization for financial and business information students and professionals, is to inspire and support excellence by:

  • Encouraging the study and practice of accountancy, finance, and information systems;
  • Providing opportunities for service, professional development, and interaction among members and financial professionals; and
  • Fostering lifelong ethical, social, and public responsibilities.

 

Benefits:

  • 300+ Chapters and Growing!
  • Professional Reputation
  • Bonding Through Activities and Service
  • Communication Skills
  • Scholastic Achievement
  • Mentors
  • Enhanced University Experience
  • Networking Opportunities
  • Future Contacts

 

Professional Activities

The transition from college to the professional world can be daunting for some students. The Nu Rho Chapter hosts a variety of events geared towards making this shift as seamless as possible. These activities can help prepare you for the entering the field of business with an advantage by developing and refining essential skills while simultaneously providing opportunities to connect with industry professionals.

 

Service Activities

At Nu Rho, we believe civic engagement is the foundation to being a contributing member in society. By combining knowledge, skills, values, and motivation to consciously create a positive impact, we strive to make a difference in our communities. Our chapter participates in a variety of events targeted towards assisting disenfranchised families and individuals in an attempt to have everyone, regardless of their walk of life, reach their full potential.

 

Reaching Out Activities

Building meaningful relationships and sharing information are two critical components of effective networking, the cornerstone of a successful business career. At Nu Rho, we organize activities that connect us with local Beta Alpha Psi chapters and alumni in order to foster personal, professional, and academic growth. Additionally, we host events to inform future generations about careers in financial information in an effort to inspire the leaders of tomorrow. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1WtESQ-JyKw
summary: Beta Alpha Psi is a National Honors Organization for Accounting, Finance, and Information Systems students. We seek to recognize our members' scholastic excellence and provide professional development and self-growth opportunities.

name: Undergraduate Education
summary: Undergraduate Education provides students and instructors with resources that promote academic excellence and student success. The Office of Undergraduate Education serves as the central administration for a number of organizational units across campus.

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is the largest leadership honor society in the United States. Our NSLS chapter at University of California, Riverside was started in 2015. Our local chapter is part of the national organization with 700+ chapters and over 1.4 million members. 

Students are selected based on exemplary academic achievement. As of 2021 we have 1,930 members who have access to:

·       More than $400,000 in scholarships.

·       An exclusive NSLS job board.

·       A personalized letter of recommendation. 

The NSLS leadership program teaches the interpersonal skills that employers seek to give you an edge in the job market. Hear directly from current members as they share how their experience has changed their life.

If you become a member, some of the other benefits are:

·       Learn from exceptional leaders like former President Barack Obama during exclusive Speaker Broadcasts.

·       Meet new friends and make lifelong connections (on-campus and virtually).

·       Overcome challenges with success coaches shortcutting your way to success.

·       Access to private networking events.

Visit the NSLS website to learn more about and accept your nomination. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

Some of the common questions we receive are listed below. If you have any other questions, visit nsls.org to chat with a team member or email help@nsls.org.

What do I have to do to become inducted?

Our five-step program takes approximately 12 hours over the course of the semester at your pace. 

Is this recognized on campus?

We are a Registered Student Organization (RSO). As of 2021, we had 1,930 in our chapter and there are more than 1.4 million members nationwide.
summary: The NSLS is the largest leadership honor society in the United States. Our NSLS chapter at University of California, Riverside was started in 2015. Our local chapter is part of the national organization with 700+ chapters and over 1.4 million members.

name: CSGSA
summary: Graduate Student Association for Dept. of Computer Science graduate students

name: La Hermandad de UCR
description:
La Hermandad de UCR (HUCR) is a cultural organization with the goal to create a welcoming environment where students at UCR can thrive and succeed while contributing to the Chicane/Latine community.

We focus our efforts towards increasing the matriculation, retention, and graduation rates among the Chicane/Latine student population. We will achieve this through the guidance of our three pillars: Academic Excellence, Community Service, and Social Networking.

As “La Nueva Persona”, we will strive to spread our philosophy of Hermandad as a stepping-stone towards creating a sense of familia on the UCR campus and in the (local) Riverside community. With the understanding that Chicane/Latine students traditionally face institutional, societal, and internal struggles, HUCR aims to address and surpass these historical adversities. Our mission is to impact our community and serve as leaders to incoming Chicane/Latine students. By becoming contributing members of our society, we can encourage others to follow in our footsteps.
summary: HUCR is a healing, growing, and empowering cultural non-profit organization. Anyone can join, our efforts are focused on increasing the graduation & matriculation rates among the Latino male population

name: Club Baseball at UCR
description:
The purpose of this organization is create an inclusive environment for baseball fanatics while also promoting competition on the field. We are registered with the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA) and will compete against other university club baseball teams in both conference and non-conference games. Follow our Instagram account to stay updated on any and all events! "@ucrclubbaseball". Click the link below or in bio on our Instagram page to join our Discord for updates and information!
https://discord.gg/9SUKFB6Y4j
summary: Club Baseball at UCR strives to create an inclusive environment for baseball fanatics while also promoting competition on the field. We host games, socials, and trips to MLB games to create a community for all UCR baseball lovers.

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: Established in 1851, Alpha Delta Pi is committed to sisterhood, values and ethics, high academic standards and social responsibility. Our open motto, We Live for Each Other, is something that our sisters live by every day.
summary: Established in 1851, Alpha Delta Pi is committed to sisterhood, values and ethics, high academic standards and social responsibility. Our open motto, We Live for Each Other, is something that our sisters live by every day.

name: Taekwondo Club @ UCR
description:
Welcome to Taekwondo Club at UCR!

The mission of the Taekwondo Club is to provide training for those of all skill levels. We welcome all those who have previous experience, zero experience, are martial arts enthusiasts, or generally curious of the sport. We aim to teach the rich Korean culture of Taekwondo to all members, as well as the development of competitive skills while having fun.

Come join us! Our practices are Mondays and Wednesdays from 9:15 PM - 10:55 PM, and Fridays from 6:00 PM - 6:55 PM.

Any questions? Contact us!

Join our Discord server! https://discord.gg/ungzmJyVyY

Email us (ucrtaekwondo@gmail.com) or message us on Instagram (@tkdatucr)!
summary: The mission of the Taekwondo Club is to provide training for those already experienced in self-defense, introduce the martial art to new members, and teach the Taekwondo culture to its members.

name: Environmental Science Club
description:
As an integral part of UCR's vibrant academic community, the Environmental Sciences Club offers students - both those majoring in Environmental Science and those with a keen interest - a unique platform to explore the pragmatic implications of their studies. We strive to illuminate the practical essence of environmental science by organizing a diverse array of enriching activities.

Our club conducts immersive field trips, allowing students to observe and analyze environmental science principles in action. By visiting relevant sites, members get a first-hand experience of real-world challenges and solutions, deepening their understanding beyond textbooks and lecture halls.

Moreover, we host engaging discussions with seasoned professionals from various environmental science disciplines. These insightful dialogues provide students with an opportunity to delve into specific fields, ask pressing questions, and receive career guidance directly from industry experts.

In essence, the Environmental Sciences Club is more than just a club; it is a conduit to practical learning and professional networking, aimed at nurturing the next generation of environmental science leaders right here at UCR.
summary: UCR's Environmental Club bridges academics with reality. We host field trips, site tours, and expert talks, fostering an understanding of environmental science beyond textbooks.

name: The Literacy Initiative
description: The purpose of this to organization is to raise child literacy rates in the Riverside community to the child’s respective grade reading level or above. We wish to empower our students by giving them the fundamental tool of reading to aid them in their educational path. We also aim to be positive role models for the children and to foster a passion for reading and higher education.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to raise child literacy rates in the Riverside community to the child’s respective grade reading level or above through an incentive program.

name: Math Graduate Student Association
description:
The MathGSA represents the interests of the mathematics graduate students at UCR. We want to provide a forum to discuss ways that we can work together to improve the department, and we also aim to create a sense of community among the math graduate students. Meetings are usually held monthly, first Friday @ 2pm in Skye Hall 284.

For organization details, proceedings, and a list of helpful links for math graduate students, visit https://mathgsa.github.io/. Feel free to reach out by email.
summary: Graduate Student Association for the Department of Mathematics

name: Dean of Students
description:
Celebrating UCR's Outstanding Students

Each year the office of the AVC/Dean of Students coordinates the selection and recognition of outstanding seniors to recognize extraordinary accomplishments and contributions by students both in and out of the classroom.

The Dean Thomas L. Broadbent Commencement Award The Dean Thomas L. Broadbent Award was established in 1965 by the Associated Men Students in memory of UCR’s first Dean of Students. It is presented to a graduating male-identified undergraduate student for outstanding participation in co-curricular campus activities and leadership in student organizations. A cumulative GPA of 3.2 is required at the time of application. A personal plaque and a $200 award will be given to the student. Due: April 14, 2025.

The Dean Loda Mae Davis Commencement Award Established in 1964 by the Prytanean Society, a women’s honorary society, in memory of U’s First Dean of Women, Loda Mae Davis. It is presented to a graduating student for outstanding participation in campus life and leadership in student organizations. A minimum GPA of 3.2 is required. A personal plaque and a $200 award will be given to the student. Due: April 14, 2025

The Dean of Students Office

Our staff of educational professionals combines a unique and diverse blend of backgrounds and experiences, but we all have one thing in common: An abiding passion to encourage college students to thrive while providing the resources for academic and personal success. If you have any questions, encounter any challenges, or think we can help in any way, please don't hesitate to contact us!

We're happy that you've found us. And we can't wait to make your UCR student experience as supportive and culturally enriching as possible.

 

Office of the AVC/Dean of Students

Phone: 951-827-6095 
Email: deanofstudents@ucr.edu
Web: deanofstudents.ucr.edu
summary: Our staff of educational professionals all have one thing in common: An abiding passion to encourage college students to thrive, while providing the resources for academic and personal success.

name: UCR Admissions
description: UCR Undergraduate Admissions is committed to providing you with the highest level of service. We serve prospective students, their families, and educators, and enroll a highly diverse and academically accomplished student body.
summary: UCR Undergraduate Admissions is committed to providing you with the highest level of service. We serve prospective students, their families, and educators, and enroll a highly diverse and academically accomplished student body.

name: Athletics
description:
With a valid Undergraduate Student ID card, Highlanders can attend any regular season home sporting event for FREE! Bring your R'Card to the ticket booth and be a part of the Highlander experience. 

 

UC Riverside Athletics have won multiple national championships along with scores of conference championships. More than 300 of your fellow Highlanders represent UCR in athletics. The Highlanders have participated in intercollegiate athletics for more than 60 year, and since 2001, have competed at the NCAA Division I level - the highest level of intercollegiate athletics competition in the nation. UC Riverside is a member of the Big West Conference, which is rated the strongest non-football conference in NCAA. 

 

GoHighlanders.com

#ThisisRHouse
summary: Show your support for UC Riverside Athletics by attending games, wearing your blue and gold and cheering on your Highlanders while they compete in NCAA Division I Sports.

name: Highlanders Against Sexual Violence Peer Education Program
description:
The Highlanders Against Sexual Violence (HASV) Peer Education Program at UC Riverside was launched in the spring of 2015 to raise awareness throughout the UCR community about occurrences of sexual violence. It is the program’s goal to partner with on- and off-campus community resources in an effort to educate on the topics of sexual assault, dating/domestic violence, stalking and healthy sexuality. One of the objectives of the program is to promote activities and attitudes that will contribute to the elimination of acts of violence and to foster creating a safe atmosphere for all UCR community members. Peer educators play an important role by acting as student leaders on these issues, having the unique ability to access student populations, and are dedicated to working with the student body to create a campus culture that values respect and consent.

Students who have significant leadership experience and have demonstrated a commitment to sexual violence education and prevention (typically through commitment as peer educators) may serve as a peer coordinator and work in collaboration with the assistant vice chancellor of health and wellness, the Well’s interim director, Devon Sakamoto, and other campus administrators whose work focuses on sexual violence education and prevention such as the CARE advocate, Women’s Resource Center, LGBT Resource Center and Title IX, to fulfill the program’s goals.

Highlanders Against Sexual Violence is part of a larger cohort of student leaders participating in peer education called Power of the Peers. Students in this program are exposed to a variety of topics as they interact with the Power of the Peers network and have the ability to attend workshops, speaker series, and trainings led by professionals and fellow peers.
summary: Are you passionate about sexual violence education and prevention? Do you want to get involved on campus in a meaningful way? Help us educate the UCR community on sexual assault, dating/domestic violence, stalking, and healthy sexuality!

name: Astronomy Club @ UCR
description:
The Astronomy Club @ UCR strives to promote knowledge and passion of the space-related sciences through our outreach and engagement of all members of UCR and the general public. We intend to bring together those curious about astronomy and share our excitement and wonder with each other and the community.

Join our discord with SPS!

https://discord.gg/mcSc4WGa

Follow us on Instagram!

@astroclubtucr
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to bring together those throughout UCR and the general public that share a same level of appreciation of the space-related sciences to facilitate a diverse supportive environment.

name: Phi Chi Theta
description:
Phi Chi Theta at UCR is a co-educational business and economics fraternity dedicated to creating well-balanced leaders through involvement, empowerment, and philanthropy. We are committed to building the next generation of business leaders.

https://ucrpct.com/

Follow our Instagram for more: @ucrpct
summary: Phi Chi Theta at UCR is a co-educational business and fraternity dedicated to creating well-balanced leaders through involvement, empowerment, and philanthropy. We are committed to building the next generation of business leaders.

name: South Asian Federation - SAF
description: The purpose of South Asian Federation is to culturally, socially, and philanthropically unite the South Asian student population at UC Riverside. We aim to be safe space for all those who identify as South Asian, in hopes to create a stronger community. We hope to one day have a South Asian Costo Hall center to help our community feel more represented here. We are mainly socially and culturally oriented, striving to have the South Asian student population celebrate the many different cultures represented through festivals and inclusive religious events. Additionally we are philanthropically associated as well, providing resources and opportunities for students here on campus year round.
summary: The purpose of South Asian Federation is to culturally, socially, and philanthropically unite the South Asian student population at UC Riverside. We aim to be safe space for all those who identify as South Asian, in hopes to create a stronger community.

name: Archery Club at UCR
description:
If you have inquiries about the club, please email archery.ucr@gmail.com

Archery, unlike most other sports, requires you to outperform your opponent by being in and maintaining your most calm and steady state. A collegiate archer is expected to maintain this mental and physical discipline on the field and in the classroom.

 

Archery Club at UCR is an organization that aims to bring the unique aspects of archery to UCR by introducing students new to the sport and by training and exchanging insight with UCR students who have prior experience. As one of the only UC campuses without an archery team, our current main focus is on archery development for people new to the sport. We are also training those with prior experience in preparation for assembling a competitive team to compete in national and intercollegiate events in the future. 

Practice Location: Glen Mor Fields
Practice Times: Fridays 2:00 pm- 5:45 pm

Members!!! If you can come early, please come 10-15 minutes before the start of the practice and help us set up the field.
summary: Archery Club at UCR is an organization that aims to allow students with an interest in archery to learn the sport, to hone their skills, and to enter into local competitive events as a collegiate archer.

name: California College Republicans
description: We are an Independent chartered Republican Organization with many local and state connections. We put on discussions, host speakers, and help campaign for Republican candidates. Our fundamental mission is to change the conversation at UCR and make students aware that it’s okay to hold populist viewpoints outside the mainstream narrative. If you feel alienated by the extreme repetitive talking points that take hold in conversations at UCR, this club is here for you with open arms. We provide like-minded fellowship and opportunities to be more politically active in our state communities.
summary: We are a Republican activism group looking to change of the culture at UCR.

name: College Democrats at UC Riverside
description:
The purpose of the College Democrats is to pursue the principles of equity, justice, and freedom for all regardless of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation or income. We shall pursue these principles through support of the candidates and ideas of the Democratic Party. College Democrats will organize Voter Registration (VR), Get Out The Vote (GOTV), debates, speaker events, and other events that promote Democratic principles at UCR for the student body and surrounding community. 

 

Please refer to our Instagram page for updates.
summary: The College Democrats at UCR seek to promote the Democratic Party by hosting speaker events, debates, and Voter Registration/GOTV efforts on campus.

name: AGSM Women in Business
description: AGSM Women in Business (WIB) is an organization dedicated to creating and developing a powerful network of professionals. We strive to build a community for alumni and current students of ASGM to network and bridge the gender gap in businesses all over the world. As an organization, we host various professional development workshops, philanthropic projects, and networking events. Feel free to send us a message for more information.
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to empower aspiring business professionals and to both educate and combat gender inequalities in the professional setting. 

Please send us a message, if you are interested in joining or collaborating with us!

name: Healthy Hearts Student Organization at UCR
description:
The primary purpose(s) of this organization is to provide helpful services to the community. It shall also raise awareness of heart related illnesses and to prevent the spread of heart related illnesses by educating the public on Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) protocol, lifestyle modifications information, and stress relief techniques as well as raising awareness on this illness. Furthermore, this organization aims to provide free CPR training to students and the general public.

 

Check us out at UCRHealthyHearts.org and/or follow us on Instagram @ucrhealthyhearts for weekly updates
summary: It shall raise awareness of heart related illnesses and to prevent the spread of heart related illnesses by educating the public on Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) protocol, lifestyle modifications information, and stress relief technique

name: R'Pantry
description:
R'Pantry was established in 2015 as the UC Riverside Food pantry aiming to 'End Highlander Hunger'. A collaboration between on campus organizations and the UC Global Food Initiative allows us to provide non-perishable food, produce, personal care, and child care items for Highlanders in need. 

R'pantry aims to do much more than simply provide emergency food and basic needs items to Highlanders at UCR. We offer a variety of programs throughout the year to increase understanding of emergency resources and to promote learning of life skills which will promote immediate and long term wellness for our community.  

To volunteer with the R'Pantry you need to sign up a quarter before. For more information on volunteering contact us via email or phone. 

Follow us on facebook: 

https://www.facebook.com/ucrpantry/

 

Follow us on Instagram: 

https://www.instagram.com/rpantry.ucr/
summary: R'Pantry is a program on campus working to end Highlander Hunger. A collaboration between on campus organizations and the UC Global Food Initiative allows us to provide non-perishable food for Highlanders in need.

name: Residential Life
description: Our Mission is to provide a safe, inclusive and diverse living and learning experience that supports academic success and personal development.
summary: Residential Life at University of California Riverside (UCR) is committed to providing an environment that promotes the personal and academic success of each residential student.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description:
• Foster a unique community and fellowship among peers and mentors committed to medical mission and healing ministry.
  • Provide an opportunity to travel internationally alongside licensed medical professionals and assist them in the care of local patients in the community to further God's Kingdom.
  • Inspire students to live as faithful disciples of Jesus Christ in their future health professions.

https://youtu.be/So3C-eL8hzM
summary: GMMA strives to equip pre-health scholars with training and networking opportunities so that they may serve as effective health professionals of Jesus Christ.

name: Highlander Space Program
description:
Discord Link!!: https://discord.gg/7JF38aJ3zP

The purpose of this organization shall be to promote and advance the study and practice of aerospace engineering among students at the University of California, Riverside. HSP seeks to provide a forum for its members to explore, develop, and enhance their technical skills while fostering a culture of innovation and creativity. The club shall achieve these objectives by conducting regular meetings, work sessions, and other related activities deemed appropriate and beneficial for its members.

HSP aims to create opportunities for its members to collaborate with industry professionals and organizations, as well as engage in hands-on projects and research that are relevant to aerospace engineering. Through these activities, the club hopes to provide its members with valuable learning experiences and enhance their understanding of aerospace engineering theories and principles.

Additionally, HSP seeks to promote public awareness and interest in the field through various outreach activities, such as recorded demonstrations, tabling, and educational events. The organization also aims to inspire and encourage the next generation of scientists and engineers by actively engaging with the wider community, particularly in the STEM community.

HSP is committed to maintaining an inclusive and welcoming environment that values diversity of thought and respects the rights and dignity of all its members. HSP shall operate per the relevant laws and regulations and shall be guided by its constitution and bylaws.
summary: Highlander Space Program, formerly known as Aerospace Systems, aims to promote and advance the study and practice of aerospace engineering among students of the University of California, Riverside through hands-on experience with liquid rocket projects

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 5, 1911 on the campus of Indiana University. Our Fundamental Purpose is "Achievement in Every Field of Human Endeavor". We were found by ten illustrious founders by the names of Elder Watson Diggs, Ezra D. Alexander, Byron K. Armstrong, Henry T. Asher, Marcus P. Blakemore, Paul W. Caine, George W. Edmonds, Guy L. Grant, Edward G. Irvin, John Milton Lee.

The Eta Zeta Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated was chartered on March 22, 1974 and is a member of the Western Province.
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 5, 1911 on the campus of Indiana University. Our Fundamental Purpose is "Achievement in Every Field of Human Endeavor".

name: Hong Kong Students Association @UCR
description:
https://youtu.be/HaxPfQGkSsA

Hong Kong Students Association (HKSA), a student-based, school recognized cultural organization at UCR. We are dedicated to gathering and creating a sense of belonging for all Hong Kong students at UCR, as well as sharing and promoting traditional Hong Kong cultural values to students who are interested. 

#Culture #Friendship #Experience
summary: Hong Kong Students Association (HKSA), a student-based, school recognized cultural organization at UCR.

name: Queer Graduate Student Association
description: QGSA is meant to represent a diverse community and promote inclusivity and equity within UCR. This organization is for queer and genderqueer identified graduate students to discuss campus concerns related to LGBTQIA2S+ issues, hang out, and ultimately foster a sense of community and activism!
summary: This organization is a safe haven for queer and gender queer graduate students to connect, educate, and support one another throughout our graduate school journey.

name: Campus Advocacy, Resources & Education
description: CARE (Campus Advocacy, Resources & Education) is an intervention and prevention support program dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside. We seek to inspire and cultivate a culture of care by providing resources, advocacy and educational programming related to issues of sexual assault, relationship violence and stalking.
summary: CARE (Campus Advocacy, Resources & Education) is an intervention and prevention support program dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside.

name: R'Courses
description: R’Courses are 1 unit, S/NC offerings facilitated by UCR undergraduate students. Each course has a faculty instructor of record who provides mentoring and support behind the scenes. These courses are an opportunity to develop leadership skills, innovate the undergraduate curriculum, and promote democratic, experiential education on campus. Interested students submit a proposal 2 quarters before they offer the course, and complete a facilitator training course 1 quarter before.
summary: R’Courses are 1 unit, S/NC offerings facilitated by UCR undergraduate students. Each course has a faculty instructor of record who provides mentoring and support behind the scenes.

name: The Cultural Awareness Project
description: The Cultural Awareness Project is an opportunity for students to raise their level of Cultural Competence and have a positive impact on Campus Climate. Participation in CAP provides students the chance to learn the skills necessary to thrive and succeed in an increasingly globalized and diverse society. Participants will get a chance to learn more about their own identity—how it shapes their experience both on campus and outside of the University—and they will learn the skills necessary to have civil dialogue and collaborate with people different from themselves.
summary: The Cultural Awareness Project is an opportunity for students to raise their level of Cultural Competence and have a positive impact on Campus Climate.

name: Indian Students Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to build a community for Indian students and people interested in Indian culture wherein they find support and a homely environment. This would also give the students a chance to celebrate their culture in a foreign country. The Indian community is full of diverse cultures wherein people from different regions bring about a conducive and cheerful atmosphere to share with everyone. The large number of Indian students in the university will be able to bond and relate with students having similar interests and share their cultural heritage.
summary: The Indian Students' Association aims at creating a common ground for all students interested in culture and traditions of India.

name: AISES Chapter at UC Riverside
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to advance the objectives of the American Indian Science and Engineering Society; hereafter called the Society, in its own geographical location, which are as follows: To nurture building of community by bridging science and technology with traditional native values. Through its educational programs, AISES provides opportunities for other academic arenas. The trained professionals then become technologically informed leaders within the Indian community. AISES' ultimate goal is to be a catalyst for the advancement of American Indians as they seek to become self-reliant and self-determined members of society.
summary: To advance the objectives of the American Indian Science and Engineering Society in its own geographical location. To nurture building of community by bridging science and technology with traditional native values.

name: R'shorts
description: R'Shorts is a filmmaking club that helps facilitate the development of different skills such as: screenwriting, videography and/or cinematography, directing, acting, postproduction, etc. This also includes the different practices (location scouting, acting rehearsals, etc.) involved in making a short film. Members will get the opportunity to exercise their creativity, hone their crafts, network, build their portfolios, gain exposure, and make new friends. Being surrounded by like-minded, passionate people allows members to create new content, be alerted to new production opportunities, and improve skills that open doors into careers within the filmmaking and content creation industries.
summary: Film club at the University of California Riverside that allows students to develop and explore their creative skills through the film process.

name: Students Organize for Syria
description: SOS envisions a free Syria in which the Syrian people have the rights and freedoms that they deserve. Our mission is to stand in solidarity with the Syrian people in their struggle for freedom and human rights, to assist Syrians in their effort to build a self-determined and pluralistic society, and to raise awareness of and help alleviate the humanitarian crisis currently taking place in Syria. We are devoted to sustaining an organized student network that is unified for the cause of a free Syria.
summary:
Students Organize for Syria focuses on promoting student solidarity with the Syrian people’s struggle for freedom by holding nationally unified campaigns in the following three categories:

-Education/Awareness

-Advocacy/Solidarity

-Fundraising

name: Klesis Christian Fellowship
description: Klesis is a Christian fellowship that provides opportunities for students to explore life together, learn and live out their Christian faith in community. Our aim is to disciple Christians to grow in connecting with God, live out a biblical community, grow in our faith, and get questions answered about the Bible or Christianity.
summary: Klesis is a Christian fellowship that provides opportunities for students to explore life together, learn and live out their Christian faith in community.

name: School of Medicine Medical Student Council
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to establish an association in full support of representing the School of Medicine student body and interest group organizations, while ensuring adequate funds to be allocated upon application submission/review for student activities, research, presentation, conference or academic enrichment opportunities for the M.D. Students of the School of Medicine. The UCR SOM MSC will ensure complete transparency, easy communication, and fair allocation of funds and resources to the M.D. student body.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to establish an association in full support of representing the School of Medicine student body and interest group organizations.

name: Community Engaged Learning
description:
"Where academia meets the community"

UCR Community Engaged Learning is an educational initiative integrating community service, service learning through courses, academic internships, and community-based research! Many UCR students and orgs are involved in the community, and we want to support connections between that service and academic work. We will highlight student reflections about the great connections they are seeing between their studies, their service, and their future careers.

Our current focus is to assist with service learning opportunities in the local community by partnering courses with organizations from various fields – from law to education to environmental science and many more! Our goal is to give back to the community and help students get real life experience within their field.
summary:
"Where academia meets the community"

UCR Community Engaged Learning is an educational initiative integrating community service, service learning through courses, academic internships, and community-based research!

name: Financial Wellness Program
description:
UCR's Financial Wellness Program has the workshops, tools, and resources that can help you make smart choices for managing your money now and planning for the future.

Financial Wellness- One of the nine health and wellness dimensions- means you're satisfied with how you manage your money.

We provide workshops, individualized coaching appointments, and online education. 

Have questions on budgeting, credit, credit cards, student loans, basic investing, or more? Check us out today! Follow us on Instagram: @ucrfinwell
summary: Being financially fit is key to being a successful, well-balanced student.

name: UCR-Department of Music
description: Music Department
summary: Music Department

name: Project Sunshine at University of California Riverside School of Medicine Chapter
description:
Bringing joy and play to pediatric patients! 

Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization that provides free educational, recreational, and social programs to children and families living with medical challenges. We offer volunteering experiences both in-person and on Zoom.

"Project Sunshine brings volunteer-led programming to pediatric patients and their families in a variety of medical settings. We recognize the tremendous weight of managing a child’s illness or injury, whether it requires short-term or long-term care. Every child with a medical challenge is unique, and our programs seek to adapt to the individual needs of each child."

https://projectsunshine.org/?hsLang=en
summary: Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization that provides free educational, recreational, and social programs to children and families living with medical challenges.

name: Graduate Exam Prep Seminars
description: A longer description goes here, with links and whatever you want.
summary: The Graduate Exam Prep Seminars are offered by the Academic Resource Center.

name: Statistics Graduate Student Association
description: We aim to advance the academic, professional, and social lives of current and future statistics graduate students at UC Riverside, and to encourage collaboration on research with students that are both within and outside of the statistics department.
summary: The official GSA of the Statistics Department's graduate program.

name: Riverside Bhangra
description: We are a diverse cultural dance team that is dedicated to Bhangra, a folk dance originating from Punjab. We love to dance, expand our team, meet new people, compete, and hold workshops.
summary: We are a diverse cultural dance team that is dedicated to Bhangra, a folk dance originating from Punjab. We love to dance, expand our team, meet new people, compete, and hold workshops.

name: The Verbal Coliseum
description:
https://youtu.be/sTcJqtuKKq8

We're the Verbal Coliseum, the only poetry-based club at UCR! During meetings and events, we create, perform, and appreciate the art of poetry. It is a safe space for all creatives of all skill levels and backgrounds. We are always learning, practicing, developing, and exploring together. Weekly meetings, workshops, guest speakers, open mics, and showcases all contribute to the collaborative and comforting ways we use to connect people with the same passion for poetry!
summary: The Verbal Coliseum is a group of artists creating, performing, and revolutionizing spoken and written word poetry and other writing endeavors on campus.

name: Cybersecurity at the University of California, Riverside
description:
The mission of Cyber@UCR is the advancement of cybersecurity education at the University of California, Riverside. All students are welcome, no matter what year or major!

 

Our club offers: 

-General meetings and technical demonstrations to promote learning

-Networking events to build connections with like-minded peers

-Offensive and defensive cyber team competitions

 

It is our hope to provide you with the tools needed for your success. Please contact us if you have any more questions at our email, cyberucr@gmail.com. 

 

Discord is our primary form of delivering updates. Feel free to join here. After joining, please follow the steps provided to become a complete member of the Discord. 

Instagram : https://www.instagram.com/cyber_ucr/?hl=en

 

https://youtu.be/vOR4__1MKgc
summary: We hope to encourage beginners and experts alike to learn more about physical and digital security. Cyber@UCR provides multiple avenues for students to be involved in technology. Whether you are a computer science major or an arts major, all is welcome!

name: Unheard Cries Charity
description: The purpose of this club is to spread awareness of the Middle Eastern penury and persecution. This organization will fight poverty through specific programs that provide humanitarian assistance for people in need. We achieve our goal through our fundraiser events we host every year like Arabian Nights, Winter Wonderland, and many others. These events help us to gain funds necessary for the certain causes we donate to at that time. For example, our Arabian Nights event usually gathers around 500 people and last year it helped us to raise thousands of dollars for a community center that houses special needs/the elderly in Egypt. We welcome any and all supporters of every background that would like to help us with this great cause!
summary: The purpose of this club is to spread awareness of Middle Eastern penury and persecution. This organization will fight poverty through specific programs that provide humanitarian assistance for people in need.

name: PERIOD. @ UCR
description: The mission of PERIOD@UCR is to celebrate and promote menstrual health through advocacy, education, and service. We host period packaging events, educational workshops, and work on campus policy to eliminate period poverty and normalize conversations about periods.
summary: The mission of PERIOD@UCR is to celebrate and promote menstrual health through advocacy, education, and service.

name: Medical Emergency Education Project
description:
The Medical Emergency Education Project strives to help alleviate health issues by equipping UC Riverside students, faculty, and staff, as well as the Inland Empire community, with the necessary resources for medical emergencies. We offer Basic Life Support and First Aid certification classes in an effort to increase the awareness and knowledge of emergency skills and techniques that could help save a life. Our organization grants the leadership opportunity to become an instructor to continue spreading the knowledge of Basic Life Support and First Aid. This not only helps students pursue potential routes to emergency medicine or emergency medical services but also help them apply the knowledge to serve underserved communities within the Inland Empire and wherever their future may take them!

Follow us on Instagram @ucrmeep for updates!
summary: The Medical Emergency Education Project provides the Inland Empire community the knowledge and skills to be utilized in medical emergencies by offering Basic Life Support and First Aid classes and the opportunity for UCR students to become an instructor!

name: Academic Intervention Program
description: Long description here, including links and websites.
summary: Brief summary here.

name: Tutorial Assistance Program
description: Long description here, including links and websites.
summary: The Tutorial Assistance Program (TAP) offers free undergraduate tutoring in various subjects and disciplines. Students can make appointments online at arc.ucr.edu or come in for drop-in tutoring in select subjects, including math and chem.

name: Veterans Services
description:
The Veterans Resource Center (VRC) is a place created by and for UC Riverside veteran and military-affiliated students which is located at 125 Costo Hall. It’s where you’ll find (and give) support, camaraderie, and understanding. Drop by and take a break, access resources, study, and just visit with friends.

Take advantage of the study lounge complete with computers, free printer access, blue books, and scantrons. Refuel with a free cup of coffee or snack from the fridge. Put your feet up and watch a little TV between classes.

The VRC is also your connection to campus and community resources. Our full-time coordinator is here to provide guidance, help you access benefits, and get the tools you need to succeed. Peer-to-peer support, mentoring, and networking opportunities are available to help you keep up and get ahead.
summary: The Veterans Resource Center (VRC) is a place created by and for UC Riverside veteran and military-affiliated students. It’s where you’ll find (and give) support, camaraderie, and understanding. Drop by and take a break, access resources, and much more.

name: Education, Society, and Human Development

name: Natural History Museum Club
description:
The Natural History Museum Club at UCR is a local branch of the Natural History Collections Care Network. Our activities include volunteering in collections on campus (such as the geology collection, entomology museum, and herbarium), museum science workshops, and tours and/or field trips to museums on and off-campus.

 

The purposes of the Natural History Museum Club at UCR organization shall be:

 

I. To provide multidisciplinary education, training, access, and opportunities for museum work to students which would otherwise be inaccessible or limited in scope

II. To interface with a nationwide network of like clubs on other university campuses

III. To facilitate interactions between undergraduates, graduates, staff, and faculty in fields utilizing natural history collections

IV. To further the goals of campus natural history museums

 

*Updated May 2025
summary: The Natural History Museum Club is a local group in the Natural History Collections Care Network. Our goals are to expose students to museum studies and increase appreciation for natural history through volunteering, discussions, trips, and projects.

name: Graduate Student Soccer Club
description: Graduate Student Soccer Club (GSSC) intends to regularly arrange casual and non-competitive soccer games to promote participation by graduate students in relaxing sport activities. However, anybody is welcome to participate in the club and its games.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to encourage and enable graduate students in the UCR campus to participate in soccer as a mean of entertainment and stress relief.

name: R'Garden
description:
R'Garden is open to students, faculty, staff, and community members, to grow fresh produce while providing an opportunity to learn about social, environmental, and economic sustainability through a food systems approach.

R’Garden is three acres that includes a community garden, row crops, a Valencia orchard,  and a greenhouse area.  It serves as a sustainability hub to promote service learning, and community-based research around food systems through student-led projects and collaborations with the campus and community.
summary: Put a short summary here that appears in the directory.

name: Bourns College of Engineering
description:
Mission is to:
Produce engineers with the educational foundation and adaptive skills to serve rapdily evolving technology industries
Conduct nationally recognized engineering research focused on providing a technical edge for the United States
Contribute to knowledge of both fundamental and applied areas of engineering
Provide diverse curricula that will instill in our students the imagination, talents, creativity, teamwork, decision-making and problem-solving abilities
Be a catalyst for industrial growth in Inland Southern California
summary: We engineer excellence at the Marlan and Rosemary Bourns College of Engineering (BCOE) at UC Riverside.

name: UCR - Food Recovery
description:
Easy Steps: “SIGN IN” and select “JOIN” to subscribe.

 

 

GET MESSAGES ABOUT FREE FOOD ACROSS CAMPUS!

  • Live text/email alerts throughout the day
  • Campus-wide locations
  • Includes ready-to-eat meals, snacks and fresh produce

 

 

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS:

What kind of messages are sent out?

  • When various campus groups have extra food to share.

Who can subscribe to these messages?

  • Any UCR student (both undergrad and grad), staff, or faculty.

Why/how is this a thing?

  • After tuition, books, housing and other necessities are paid for, affording nutritious foods can be a struggle.
  • People have enough to deal with, being hungry shouldn’t be one of them.
  • Nutrition affects your performance. Feed your body and mind.
  • This helps reduce campus-wide food waste by ensuring food ends up in bellies and not dumpsters.

 

 

ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:

  • On-campus emergency food resource for students: rpantry.ucr.edu
  • Learn how to get money for food from CalFresh: basicneeds.ucr.edu/calfresh
  • How to secure life’s basic necessities: basicneeds.ucr.edu
summary: Join this group to receive text/email notifications when there is free food around campus. Our goal is to minimize your immediate food insecurity while also reducing campus-wide food waste.

name: Science Policy Graduate Student Association
description:
The Science Policy GSA bridges the gap between scientists and the general public, allowing STEM students to see their importance in the policy process. We practice communication skills, advocate for science, promote scientific collaboration, and create opportunities for students to connect with elected officials. We also educate students on the importance of the political process with respect to science and technology policy. We strive to allow students to see that their voice, vote, and expertise matter in the community, both local and national, and not just in academia.

 

We are the sister organization to the Science to Policy Program. To find out more visit s2p.ucr.edu
summary: This organization is for all students interested in STEM's role in policy, bettering scientific communication, and for connecting with local representatives.

name: Team Scotty
description:
Team Scotty is a fun-loving group of UCR students and staff that love hanging out with our school mascot, Scotty Highlander. We help make sure that Scotty makes it to every event and game that he can. If you’d like to get involved and find out how to become a member of the Team Scotty and try out mascotting, send us a message or come by Student Life in HUB 229 and ask anyone at the front desk how to get involved. We love welcoming new members!

            Until then, we will see you on and around campus! Don’t hesitate to say hi to Scotty when you see him!
summary: Team Scotty is a fun-loving group of UCR students and staff that love hanging out with our school mascot, Scotty Highlander. We help make sure that Scotty makes it to every event and game that he can.

name: EARS Student Group
description:
Straight from the constitution:

"The purpose of this organization shall be to facilitate diverse interdisciplinary collaboration centered around experimental acoustic projects. EARS Student group will facilitate interactive examples of our work on campus and in the community to inspire and challenge campus culture. All activities serve a symbiotic relationship with the EARS facility by UC Riverside."

 

          ☆♬○♩●♪✧♩　Welcome to the EARS Student Group!　♩✧♪●♩○♬☆

The EARS Student Group is known for conducting experimental research on acoustic sound to solve problems by offering innovative solutions. While social distancing has made acoustic sound research more of a challenge it has also proven that EARS is one of the most resilient student organizations on campus. 

 

If you are interested in collaborating in music production, developing your career path within the music business, joining a supportive and uplifting community, or discovering opportunities in places that you would’ve never thought to have looked, then EARS is an organization that can give you all of that... and more. 

Don’t miss out on this interdisciplinary goldmine of creative thinkers.

Join in the fun & our email list!

 

Create | Inspire | Build

Contact us: earsucr@gmail.com
summary:
MUSIC AND TECHNOLOGY PRODUCTION GROUP. We make sounds sound beautiful.

We want to apply YOUR UC education to the world of audio and acoustics! We're pushing the limits of music technology, production, artistic expression, and much more.

name: Underground Scholars Initiative
description: Underground Scholars Initiative (USI) was created to support all prospective and current UC Riverside students who have been formerly incarcerated and/or system impacted to create a safe space and provide opportunities for success at the University and within the community. It is to further the development of the Prison-to-School pipeline by supporting students impacted by imprisonment, incarceration, detainment, etc. The organization strives to help previously and/or currently incarcerated individuals transition into the UC system culture through resources, peer counseling to help students and their families find alternatives to incarceration with the empowerment of higher education.
summary: Underground Scholars Initiative (USI) was created to support all prospective and current UC Riverside students who have been formerly incarcerated and/or system impacted to create a safe space and provide opportunities for success in higher education.

name: Physics Organization for Womxn and the Under-Represented
description: POWUR's aim is to create a community focused on equity and self-advocacy for all members of the Department of Physics and Astronomy. We do this by holding different events, such as socials, movie nights, Inclusivity Seminars, Mental Health Panels, book clubs, and more! Check out our website to learn more
summary: POWUR is an organization committed to supporting under-represented members of the Physics department at UCR.

name: Southeast Asian Studies Graduate Student Association
description:
The Southeast Asian Studies Graduate Student Association (SEASGRAD) is an association of MA and PhD students affiliated with UC Riverside’s Southeast Asian Studies Program, SEATRiP (Southeast Asia: Text, Ritual and Performance.) SEATRiP brings together faculty and students who share an interest in the arts and humanities and are actively engaged with the languages and expressive cultures of Southeast Asia. Work within SEATRiP addresses regionally-specific texts, rituals and performances across time and space. The interdisciplinary program was founded in 2002 by scholars in the Ethnomusicology, Anthropology, and Comparative Literatures and Languages departments.

SEASGRAD’s mission is to build an inclusive, empowered and professional community of scholars in Southeast Asian Studies.

This is an interdisciplinary group comprised of members enrolled in Master and Doctoral programs at UCR who are also associated with the SEATRiP M.A. and SEATRiP Designated Emphasis programs, or otherwise affiliated with SEATRiP and Southeast Asian Studies generally. The purposes of SEASGRAD shall be:

-We contribute to scholarship on Southeast Asia, its people, and its diasporas.

-We promote interdisciplinary dialogues among scholars of Southeast Asian studies.

-We nurture communication between our graduate students, faculty members, and undergraduate students.

-We gather and disperse integral funding and educational information.

-We seek to heighten university and public awareness of the program of Southeast Asian Studies at UCR through events and outreach projects, such as our annual conference.
summary: SEASGRAD is an association of graduate students affiliated with the Southeast Asian Studies program (SEATRiP) at UC Riverside. Our mission is to build an inclusive, empowered and professional community of scholars in Southeast Asian Studies.

name: Leadership in Action Certificate
description:
The certificate is broken up into 3 levels of leadership - the Individual, the Group, and the Society. Each level has its own requirements. You must complete all requirements within 2 academic years (i.e. Fall 2018 - Spring 2020). Please see all requirements below: 

 

The Individual

  1. Complete the Social Change Model Seminar
    • The seminar is a 2 hour workshop—the first seminar will be scheduled for late-Fall, early Winter quarter. You will be emailed with options when scheduled.
  2. Complete 5 additional workshops (from pre-approved list)
    • PLEASE SEE THE LIST BELOW

The Group

  1. Complete 1 leadership series
    • Options Include:
      • Cultural Awareness Project
      • Bridge the Gap

The Society

  1. Attend the Inspiring R’ Leaders Conference
    • Saturday, Feb. 2nd, 2019 (go.ucr.edu/leadership)
  2. Complete 50 hours of community service
    • You are welcomed to identify your own service opportunities, or you can pick from our weekly/bi-weekly list. As the quarter goes on, we will email all certificate participants a list of service opportunities around Riverside and the greater Inland Empire.

name: The Arabic Culture Club
description: The Arabic Culture Club is a club geared towards Arabic speakers from any country or background, along with those interested in learning more about Arabic language and culture. We hope to enlighten our members with opportunities to attend cultural events and activities relating to Arabic culture and language along with many experiences that showcase Arabic in its true beauty. This club is open to all who are interested, and will provide many opportunities to enhance the knowledge of the Arabic language and Arab culture.
summary: This organization aims to bring together those who speak Arabic of all dialects and those interested in learning about Arabic language and culture. We hope to bring together Arabic speakers from all over the world and those who would like to know more.

name: Women in Computing at UCR
description:
Women in Computing (WINC) is dedicated to empowering and supporting a network of individuals in tech at the University of California, Riverside, with a focus on women and marginalized genders. We believe that every individual, regardless of gender identity, should have equal access to opportunities to pursue a career in Computer Science. WINC strives to achieve this by offering inclusive and welcoming member events, educational technical workshops, mentorship from role models in university and industry, research opportunities, scholarships, and more. Our goal is to increase the retention rate of marginalized genders pursuing computing and to provide a safe space for anyone interested in a tech career.

Our goal is to build a community of women programmers and provide a safe and empowering place for students to express themselves. We celebrate all programmers at UCR and encourage them to grow as students and future leaders in the computing fields. WinC is open to anyone: whether you're just looking to start coding or you have had multiple years in the industry, we'd love to have you!
summary: We aim to support, celebrate, and empower women, non-binary, and underrepresented groups in computing at UCR.

name: STAGE: Chinese Performing Arts Club
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to let more students get to learn about Chinese performing arts such as Chinese drama. STAGE would conduct at least one Chinese play in an academic year. STAGE gives students chances to be involved at school. It also provides opportunities for students to know more about Chinese culture. STAGE is the first Chinese drama club at UCR. It enriches students’ lives and offers more opportunities to express students’ talents. It is also helpful to enhance intercultural communication on campus.
summary: STAGE is an organization that students could learn about Chinese performing arts such as Chinese drama.

name: A Friend in Me
description:
Welcome to our club! We're so happy you're here!


A Friend in Me is an organization dedicated to providing pediatric cancer patients with a sense of normalcy and friendship. This club provides members with hospital volunteering, networking, and patient-interaction opportunities, fostering professional growth.

 

We meet several times a quarter with snacks, icebreakers, and more! Join us on HighlanderLink to receive updates about our meeting times and locations or email one of our officers (listed below) to learn more about our club! You can also text @afimucr to 81010 receive text updates.
summary: A Friend in Me is an organization dedicated to providing pediatric cancer patients with a sense of normalcy and friendship. This club provides members with volunteering, networking, and patient-interaction opportunities, fostering professional growth.

name: Neuroscience Club
description:
Thank you for your interest in UCR's Neuroscience Club. This club is for people with a common interest in the brain, its functions, and its effect on thought and behavior. Anyone is free to join regardless of what major you're in. There is no knowledge requirement or an expectation that you have taken any neuroscience classes at UCR. We hope we can provide you with a place to discuss our common interest as well as provide a more interactive and fun resource for learning about the brain. 

FAQ

When do you meet?

-- We meet every two weeks throughout the quarter from 7-8 pm with occasional socials or lab tours on off weeks. 

Where do you meet?

-- We meet in the Student Success Center Multi Purpose Room 1 (MPR 1).

What do you do in your meetings?

-- In our meetings, we discuss topics regarding the brain including brain structure, brain chemistry, and brain function as well as its applicability to fields such as psychology and child development. We host frequent socials and other events for connection and professional understanding.
summary: This club is meant for people with a common interest in the brain, its fuctions, and affect on thought and behavior. We hope to provide a place to discuss our common interest and provide a more interactive, fun resource for learning about the brain.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide underrepresented pre-medical students with knowledge, skills, and experience that are both prerequisite and concomitant to professional participation in health care fields. MAPS also serves to improve minority matriculation into all professional health related programs, with an emphasis on medicine.
summary: MAPS members are considered National Associate members and make up about one third of SNMA's membership. This initiative of the Student National Medical Association (SNMA) represents future underrepresented medical students.

name: Collective on Violence Prevention
description: The Collective on Violence Prevention (CVP) is a student network of diverse men and leaders on campus dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside. CVP seeks to create spaces on campus that explore and promote healthy masculinities through empowerment based dialogue, education and mentorship as it relates to sexual violence prevention. CVP works in collaboration with the CARE office and several student organizations on campus.
summary: The Collective on Violence Prevention (CVP) is a student network of diverse men and leaders on campus dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside. CVP seeks to create spaces on campus that explore and promote healthy masculinities.

name: Abhinaya
description:
Abhinaya is the premier competitive and local Indian Classical dance team at the University of California, Riverside. The competition team combines various types of dance styles including Bharatanatyam and Kathak to create pieces that tell an intricate and beautiful story. The local team combines all 8 classical dance styles of India to showcase their culture at UCR and in all of SoCal. Their purpose is to help classical dancers keep their passion alive throughout college.

Hi potential member!

We are so excited to have you here! Abhinaya is recruiting in the fall but we hold various events throughout the year, so feel free to join us whenever you can. Our main recruitment will be during the Fall and we will keep you most updated on our Instagram page: @abhinaya.ucr We also have a local team that auditions ever quarter and we love participating in competitions and cultural events during the year.

Follow us, send us a message, and we hope to see you at one of our auditions or workshops!
summary: Abhinaya is the premier competitive and local Indian Classical dance team at the University of California, Riverside established in 2018. This year Abhinaya enters the 3rd Generation of competition season.

name: Center for Conservation Biology
description: The purpose of this organization shall be for students from multiple disciplinary backgrounds to collaborate on activities which promote conservation of California’s natural resources. Our goal is to expand the knowledge and interest in conservation biology and provide opportunities to promote science to the public, as well as providing opportunities for fellow students with developmental training. We have the Center for Conservation Biology facility on campus as a direct resource for collaborative opportunities and events associated with conservation.
summary: We are an interdisciplinary group of students stemming from various departments, coming together to promote theta conservation and restoration of species and ecosystems. We also provide opportunities for outreach and professional development.

name: Highlander MTG
description:
At HMTG, we provide Commander and Modern friendly games along with occasional drafts. Our goal as a club is to reach out to any students interested or already invested in the Magic: The Gathering trading card game and wish to find other like-minded individuals that are interested in learning how to play. Something new we are trying to implement into our club are fun side events to get all of our student community involved. Join our Discord at https://discord.gg/HSPWVRHyCJ and stay connected with other magical people! 
We will be sharing our new meeting place and room for next quarter, so join the Discord to stay connected!
summary: Highlander MTG aims to create a safe and friendly environment for others to play Magic: The Gathering by hosting weekly meetings, drafts, and other events! Join our Discord at: https://discord.gg/HSPWVRHyCJ

name: Riverside X Dance
description: Riverside x Dance is a beginner-friendly K-pop dance team at UCR. With workshops, socials, and performances, RxD is dedicated to embracing a community that’s bringing back fun to kpop. With rolling auditions per quarter, we welcome all students to apply and join the team! Bringing back K-pop in style, The RxD Team ♡ ⋆.˚ ᡣ𐭩 .𖥔˚
summary: Riverside x Dance is a beginner-friendly K-pop dance team at UCR. With workshops, socials, and performances, RxD is dedicated to embracing a community that’s bringing back fun to 3rd gen kpop. Bringing back K-pop in style, The RxD Team ♡ ⋆.˚ ᡣ𐭩 .𖥔˚

name: Hands On Health Care
description:
Hands On Health Care (HOHC) aims to connect pre-health undergraduate students at UCR with opportunities and programs for hands-on clinical experience. Through workshops, panels, and guest speakers, members gain insight into various healthcare careers while developing practical skills. Such activities include suturing, IV clinics, CPR, and EMT skills practice, among others. HOHC also serves as an outreach platform, keeping members informed about health programs, career opportunities, and volunteering throughout the Inland Empire. By offering real-world experience, students can explore and confirm their passion for a career in healthcare through this club.

 

Follow us on our social media platforms below!

 

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/handsonhealthcare_ucr/ 

TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@ucr.hohc
summary: Hands On Health Care (HOHC) connects pre-health undergraduates at UCR with hands-on clinical experiences through workshops, panels, and skill-based activities. Outreach is also provided on various health programs, career opportunities, and volunteering.

name: Native American Honor Society
description:
The purpose of the Native American Honor Society shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of Native American, Pacific Islander, and Alaskan Native individual members in all fields. Members will be selected on the basis of GPA and must volunteer and be an active member.

You can find us here at; Costo Hall 229.
summary: “The purposes of Native American Honor Society shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of Native American individual members in all fields.”

name: Health Queers
description: Health Queers provides support for LGBTQ+ students and allies involved in healthcare and healthcare-adjacent fields. We provide a space for students to network with other queer pre-health students and to educate others on the health disparities that affect our community.
summary: Health Queers provides support for LGBTQ+ students and allies involved in healthcare fields. We provide a space for students to network with other queer pre-health students and to educate others on the health disparities that affect our community.

description:

If you're interested in joining, a previous experience in playing basketball is heavily recommended.
