name: UCR Healthy Campus
description:
UCR is taking an integrated and comprehensive approach to elevate health and well-being at UCR. UCR’s Healthy Campus has formed partnerships with staff, faculty, students and the surrounding community to develop, implement, and institutionalize policies and environments essential for sustainable behavior change, making the healthy choice the easy choice.

 

Student Org Competition Application:

http://ucriverside.az1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_8pGEOy9VhZzkqrP

 

STUDENT OPPORTUNITIES

  • Project Grants
  • Volunteering
  • Internships
  • Part Time Employment

 

SUBCOMMITTEES

  1. Built Environment
  2. Physical Activity
  3. Mental Health
  4. Healthy Eating/Nutrition
  5. Preventative Health
  6. Metrics
  7. Culture Change
  8. Substance Use & Addiction
  9. Marketing & Communication
summary: Healthy Campus invests in improving the health and quality of life of our UCR campus community.

name: First Generation Student Association
description:
Through reaching out to first generation students on campus, we hope to cultivate personal improvement through mentorship within our members. As first generation students, the effort to break the standard can be done through a higher education, thus the establishment of this organization. 

 

Whether you're a first generation college student, first generation immigrant, or the first generation in your family to be born in the States, FGSA is a open and safe space to make connections and share your story. It is common for first generation students to be independent and solve issues themselves. With this space, we hope to exchange knowledge and help one another succeed.
summary: FGSA hopes to empower first generation students by creating a community that promotes academic success and networking. We look to support and create unity with all of our members for success.

name: Highlander EMS
description: Highlander EMS is a student-run EMS organization dedicated to providing care during emergent and non-emergent situations for the UCR campus and surrounding communities. Each care-providing member is licensed by the California EMS Authority. In addition to providing care, we help students understand how to receive EMT licensure and guide them through the process.
summary: Highlander EMS is a student-run EMS organization dedicated to providing care during emergent and non-emergent situations for the UCR campus and surrounding communities. Each care-providing member is licensed by the California EMS Authority.

name: HOSA - Future Health Professionals
description: UCR HOSA is a pre-health organization that serves students in serving UCR and the surrounding community by volunteering, networking, and socializing with students alike. We compete in leadership conferences in order to better prepare for future careers.
summary: UCR HOSA is a pre-health organization that serves students in serving UCR and the surrounding community by volunteering, networking, and socializing with students alike. We compete in leadership conferences in order to better prepare for future careers.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America at UC Riverside
description:
Hi there! The Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA) at UCR are looking to make a change on campus for the betterment of our fellow UCR students as well as the Inland Empire as a whole. As part of the 'UC of the working class' and a YDSA chapter, we advocate and fight for:

Medicare for All, Universal Childcare and Pre-K, Religious, Gender, and Racial Equality, and Working Class Solidarity

 

That said, if you're looking to:

Get involved in organizing and unionizing student workers

Learn about progressive ideas that could benefit all working class citizens

Hang out and get to know some like-minded people

 

Then consider joining us for our general meetings! We'll be meeting every Thursday 6-7 at Watkins 2240 and feel free to invite your friends as our meetings are open to everyone.

 

Please join our discord and follow us on Instagram.

 

YDSA at UCR Discord
summary: YDSA is the youth and student section of the Democratic Socialists of America. We believe that working people should run both the economy and society democratically to meet human needs, not to make profits for a few.

name: Global Brigades - Legal/Human Rights
description:
The Global Legal Empowerment Brigades chapter at UCR is a non-profit organization that focuses on providing free legal services and education to rural, under-resourced communities that lack access to such. Our volunteers travel to communities located in Ghana, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Panama, while accompanied by lawyers and human rights activists. By assisting lawyers and activists in legal clinics and educational workshops, our volunteers learn about various ways to advocate for human rights and create a more empowered and secure community. When our organization is not on a brigade, we attend local volunteer events in Riverside county, while fundraising and preparing for the upcoming brigade.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=do1pqR8hLFc
summary: The Global Legal Empowerment Brigades chapter at UCR is a non-profit organization that focuses on providing free legal services and education to rural, under-resourced communities worldwide that lack access to such.

name: United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund @ UCR
description: UNICEF @ UCR is a club that will advocate for the protection of children's rights and will uphold these values through community service, fundraising to support UNICEF’s aid programs, and spreading awareness on a local and global level.
summary: UNICEF @ UCR is a club that will advocate for the protection of children's rights and will uphold these values through community service, fundraising to support UNICEF’s aid programs, and spreading awareness on a local and global level.

name: Maximizing Access to Research Careers at UCR
description:
https://youtu.be/fkhZB_8x0jg

The purpose of this organization is to increase the number of highly-trained underrepresented biomedical and behavioral scientists in leadership positions to significantly impact the health-related research needs of the nation.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to increase the number of highly-trained underrepresented biomedical and behavioral scientists in leadership positions to significantly impact the health-related research needs of the nation.

name: Raaga at UCR
description: We are UCR's premiere competitive South Asian fusion acappella team consisting of talented vocalists who come together to perform mashups and standalone songs, combining elements of South Asian and Western music in order to create engaging performances. We compete at top inter-collegiate acappella competitions within California and all over the nation. Follow our instagram to stay updated @raagaatucr
summary: Premiere competitive South Asian fusion acappella team at UCR consisting of talented vocalists who perform songs (mashups and standalone songs) at the top inter-collegiate acappella competitions nation and state wide. We hold team auditions every fall.

name: Black Scholars in Business
description: The purpose(s) of this organization, Black Scholars in Business, is to provide a community for students at UCR that drives academic, professional, and personal success through transformative actions in business, socioeconomic, and global education.  BSIB is intended to serve as a gateway in which students can enhance their skill sets, expand their network, and hone in on their passion with the goal of not only being leaders in today’s professional climate, but also laying the foundation for students to be progressive in business. Black Scholars in Business will help all students in all areas of study succeed in the professional world by providing them with numerous resources and opportunities.
summary: Black Scholars in Business (BSIB) aims to provide a community for students at UCR that drives academic, professional, and personal success through transformative actions in business, socioeconomic, and global education.

name: R'Brains and Behavior
description: R'Brains and Behavior is a student-led psychology club dedicated to creating an inclusive environment for anyone interested in psychology. We provide a space to explore diverse topics, engage in related activities, and connect with like-minded individuals. Our mission is to support members' educational, professional, and personal growth by offering networking opportunities, career development, and resources. Through events with UCR staff and alumni, we aim to foster a strong community and help members discover and expand their focus within the field of psychology.
summary: We are a Psychology Club! Our purpose is to establish resources, such as networking opportunities, educational advancement, and professional development, in addition creating a community for young professionals within the field of psychology.

name: Table Tennis Club at UCR
description:
Table Tennis Club at UCR is a community of table tennis players at all skill levels dedicated to fostering casual and competitive play.

Join our Discord for more information and to see when people are playing: https://discord.gg/7nswYnB5dS

Follow our Instagram @ucr_table_tennis

Buy Quality Equipment and Help Support our Club! : https://www.megaspin.net/default.asp?aid=ucr
summary: Table Tennis Club at UCR is sincerely built to help those who are interested in playing table tennis casually or to improve their skills and provide a friendly and welcoming environment in the sport of table tennis no matter the skill level

name: The Public Health Initiative
description: The purpose of this organization is to be focused in science and systems designed to protect and improve health at the community and population level. This includes efforts to improve health through disease prevention and promoting healthy behaviors as well as community service.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to be focused in science and systems designed to protect and improve health at the community and population level. This includes efforts to improve health through disease prevention and promoting healthy behaviors.

name: SciComm @ UCR
description: We practice creative and community-building science communication to the general public through events and multimedia projects. Additionally, we organize an annual workshops about the practice of science communication.
summary: We practice creative communication of science to the general public through events and multimedia projects. Additionally, we organize an annual workshop about the practice of science communication.

name: School of Business R'LEAD Program
description: The R’LEAD Program is an elective program that allows students to participate in various professional activities that demonstrate their career investment to employers. Activities include professional development workshops, field trips to various organizations, networking opportunities and more.
summary: The R’LEAD Program allows students to participate in various professional activities that demonstrate their career investment to employers. Activities include professional development workshops, field trips to various organizations, and networking.

name: Future Teachers of Color Committed to Racial Justice+
description:
The purpose of the FTOC Organization is:

  1. To welcome, showcase, and build a community of future teachers of Color (both undergraduate and graduate) who are committed to racial and social justice in educational settings. 
  2. To create a safe and welcoming space for FTOC members to discuss and share ideas on how to build racially transformative classrooms and schools.
  3. To provide opportunities for future educators of Color to meet current educators of Color who are currently doing racial justice work in their own schools/communities through guest speakers.
  4. To provide opportunities for community involvement and community service. 
  5. To support current teachers of Color committed to racial justice by helping in the planning and facilitation of the annual Institute for Teachers of Color (ITOC) Committed to Racial Justice conference at UCR (http://www.instituteforteachersofcolor.org/). 

 

Mission Statement:

Following the mission of the Institute for Teachers of Color (ITOC) Committed to Racial Justice (http://www.instituteforteachersofcolor.org/), the FTOC organization’s mission is to bring together racial justice-oriented students of Color from UCR who want to pursue teaching in a K-12 or higher education setting.  Our main goal is to empower future educators of Color to teach for racial and social justice in order to build racially transformative classrooms and schools. We hope to do this by creating a safe space for students to critically think and talk about educational issues involving race and racism, providing volunteer opportunities in educational settings, and inviting teacher guest speakers to share their racial justice work.
summary: FTOC brings together racial justice-oriented students of Color from UCR who want to pursue teaching in a K-12 or higher education setting. We are a group who wants to work towards building racially transformative classrooms and schools.

name: COPE Health Scholars
description: Our mission is to provide a gateway into the world of healthcare, where we offer invaluable insights into the diverse career paths while also equipping you with the knowledge and skills to succeed. But we’re more than a resource — this is a vibrant community where you can gain hands on experience through impactful service projects and building a meaningful vibrant community with likeminded peers.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide access to information about health care and the different careers in health care. Furthermore, the club is not only for the career, but to give students the opportunity to community service.

name: R'Cycle Co-op
description:
The R'Cycle Co-op is a student org focused on building up the cycling community at UCR. The goal of the Co-op is to promote Sustainability, Community, Safety Education and Training, and Advocacy for alternative transportation on campus.

Participation is open to all UCR affiliated individuals: Undergraduate students, Graduate students, Staff and Faculty. Please go to our Instagram to get started with the club!

All club participants are welcome to attend the general meetings. Section meetings will be held for each of the three communities of members: Undergrads, Graduate students, and Staff/Faculty. These meetings include group rides, lunch hours, and more.

One of the ultimate goals of the R'Cycle Co-op is to secure a physical facility on campus where the UCR community can have access to:

Inexpensive second-hand replacement parts

Affordable used bikes

Discounted new parts

A library of loaner tools

Technical assistance and training

The R'Cycle Co-op is also invested in promoting the use of alternative transportation beyond the boundaries of UCR through advocacy at the local, state and federal level.

We hope that you will join our community to help make our campus and world a better place, one bike-ride at a time!
summary: The R'Cycle Co-op is a student org focused on building up the cycling community at UCR. The goal of the Co-op is to promote Sustainability, Community, Safety Education and Training, and Advocacy for alternative transportation on campus.

name: Drag Club at UCR
description:
Here at Drag Club, we strive to share the experience of the performative art that is Drag. Our mission is to support and educate performers who are new to the art form or experience. As a club, we highlight Queerness, Transness, those questioning identity, and the expression of oneself through the art of Drag. Allowing a space for all forms of drag to flourish and grow. We aim to bring all types of Drag to the UCR Student Life and UCR. We fight for the right to exist, perform, educate, and slay.

Our Winter 2025 meetings are Mondays 2-3 pm. We meet in the LGBTQ resource center at the COSTO hall. 

Join our amazing club by attending our meetings and joining our Discord: https://discord.gg/Kseq5WQrQS
summary: To share the experience of the performative art that is Drag is our purpose, support and educate performers who are new to the art-form, or experienced. We value creativity, freedom of expression, “charisma, uniqueness, nerve and talent.”

name: Aag: Fusion Dance Team
description: Aag at UCR, is Riverside's Premier Bollywood Fusion Team! We specialize in various styles such as hip-hop, contemporary, jazz, bollywood, etc! We want to share our love and passion for dance with the community and allow for our dancers to flourish in a supportive environment.
summary: Aag is the Competitive Premier Fusion Bollywood Dance team. Aag is referred to as “fire” and we hope to show that passion on stage. We’re looking to establish our team by showing excellence in styles such as Bollywood, Hip Hop, and Bhangra.

name: Fight For Autism
description:
Fight For Autism (FFA) at UCR is a student-run organization that strives to advocate for those on the spectrum as well as represent and allow individuals on the spectrum to have a safe platform to freely express themselves. We host various events and activities that foster an inclusive environment for the neurodiverse community. Through our events, we hope to increase understanding and spread appreciation for autism. Our program Project L.E.A.D. grants members the opportunity to interact and bond with autistic children in local grade schools.

 

Fight For Autism: Tabling Video
summary: An action and awareness organization that hosts interactive events and activities to foster inclusivity for autistic individuals and peer advocates in the Riverside community.

name: Inland Empire Street Medicine
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to allow for more accessible healthcare to persons experiencing homelessness in the local community. Our goal is to build relationships and trust with persons experiencing homelessness by offering access to resources and support. By building rapport with this population, we hope to foster more long-term relationships between persons experiencing homelessness and primary care clinicians in the area. Through organizing a bi-monthly clinic with graduate professional schools through the UCR School of Medicine, local community organizations, and undergraduate volunteers, we can bridge the gap between the homeless population and the medical community
summary: At the Street Medicine Clinic at UCRSOM, we work to allow for more accessible healthcare, build relationships and trust, and provide resources and support for persons experiencing homelessness in our community.

name: Delta Alpha Pi International Honor Society
description:
Delta Alpha Pi International Honor Society recognizes the academic accomplishments of students with disabilities.

Delta Alpha Pi International Honor Society charter has officially been established at UCR: Zeta Psi. This Society will facilitate the development of leadership and advocacy skills, provide educational programming on disability-related topics, facilitate networking and leadership opportunities, and connect students with scholarships.
summary: The purpose of this Society is to recognize the academic accomplishments of UC Riverside students with disabilities, to facilitate the development of leadership and advocacy skills, and to provide opportunities to serve as peer mentors and role models.

name: Book Bears at UCR
description:
A book will be decided on and read by club members, who will then come together to discuss what they have read.

We want to provide a sense of community, comfort, and leisure to students throughout their academic journey by increasing the understanding and importance of literature. We also hope to understand and interpret the perspectives of our peers. Additionally, this organization will provide an outlet for creativity.
summary: A book will be decided on and then read by the members who will then come together to discuss the book. We hope to increase the understanding and importance of literature. We also hope to understand and interpret the perspectives of our peers.

name: Lawtinx Pre-Law Club
description:
Come join us at Lawtinx!

 

The Lawtinx Pre-Law Club was founded on the basis of offering representation and resources to the Hispanic/Latinx/Chicanx community at UC Riverside.

 

Despite making up 18% of the United States population, Latinxs makes up 4% of the legal field; our goal is to change that. We offer representation, guidance, and community to those interested in pursuing a career in the legal field by hosting speakers and offering LSAT resources amongst other cultural and professional development events. While the emphasis is on the Latinx community, all pre-law students are welcomed!

 

If interested feel free to contact us or visit our social media!

Instagram: @ucrlawtinx
summary: Lawtinx serves the large population of pre-law and Latinx students at UCR. Offering guidance and experience to students pursuing a career in the legal field. While the emphasis is on the Latinx community, everyone is welcomed.

name: Khmer Student Society
description:
Mission Statement:

Khmer Student Society is a student organization dedicated to creating a supportive and learning environment for Khmer students at UCR. The club serves as a place of representation for Khmer students and advocates for all relevant issues. Due to the lack of representation of Khmer students in higher education, the Khmer Student Society feels the need to be a necessary element within the UCR campus. We are here to be a culturally active and supportive club for our fellow students. We strive to cultivate traditions and culture for our students as well as provide a leadership opportunity for members to be involved in the community.

Club Value:

Khmer Student Society was created to foster a learning and supportive environment where all Khmer students and fellow peers can enjoy each other's company and all together learn more about Khmer culture and history. We demand respect from all members towards one another. As well as be a supportive friend. This club also provides an opportunity to network not only within UCR but within all Khmer student organizations as well as Khmer communities in various cities.
summary: We established Khmer Student Society at UCR to engage with Cambodian/Cambodian American students across the UCR campus with Cambodian culture, arts and traditions. We also provide a community where we provide mentorship and guidance to younger members.

name: Student Chapter of ISPE at UCR
description:
The International Society of Pharmaceutical Engineering (ISPE) aims to introduce its members to aspects of the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries, promote educational/career/internship opportunities, and support personal and professional growth.

Shape the Future of Pharma Through Your New Connections. Join ISPE Today
summary: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering (ISPE) aims to introduce its members to aspects of the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries, promote educational/career/internship opportunities, and support personal and professional growth

name: MannMukti at UCR
description:
To organize on behalf of MannMukti for the purposes of: 

  1. Advocating for those who suffer from any form of mental illness and to alleviate its harms, specifically as it relates to the South Asian community. 
  2. Encouraging healthy, open dialogue of mental health issues. 
  3. Improving awareness and promoting self-care, as it relates to mental health.
summary: MannMukti is an organization whose mission is encourage healthy, open dialogue of south Asian mental health issues.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc.
description: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C., January 9, 1914, by three young African-American male students. The Founders, Honorable A. Langston Taylor, Honorable Leonard F. Morse, and Honorable Charles I. Brown, wanted to organize a Greek letter fraternity that would truly exemplify the ideals of brotherhood, scholarship, and service. From its inception, the Founders also conceived Phi Beta Sigma as a mechanism to deliver services to the general community. Rather than gaining skills to be utilized exclusively for themselves and their immediate families, the founders of Phi Beta Sigma held a deep conviction that they should return their newly acquired skills to the communities from which they had come. This deep conviction was mirrored in the Fraternity's motto, "Culture For Service and Service For Humanity."
summary: From its inception, the Founders conceived Phi Beta Sigma as a mechanism to deliver services to the general community. Rather than gaining skills to be utilized exclusively for themselves and their immediate families, the founders of Phi Beta Sigma

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness
description: The purpose of “National Alliance on Mental Illness” is to break the stigma that is associated with mental illness and advocate for improving the lives of those who have suffered or are currently suffering from mental health issues specifically in the UCR community. Our goal is to give people the comfort and support that they need to overcome the set of challenges that they are facing. We want to increase empathy among people from all walks of life and backgrounds who are struggling and encourage them to seek help when needed.
summary: NAMI aims to break the stigma that is associated with mental illness and advocate for improving the lives of those who have suffered or are currently suffering from mental health issues specifically in the Inland Empire and UCR community.

name: The Basics Of Healthcare Initiative
description:
To educate individuals of the basics of healthcare, which is important to be aware of for every citizen regardless of health status and to stress the role of patient advocacy to combat potential lack of access, respect, or simple bedside manners. 

B: barrier-breaking (addressing language/cultural barriers and inequalities)

A: advocating (being a voice for those who do not have one; fighting stigmas and assumptions)

S: supporting (employing kindness and empathy in evaluation and treatment of patient condition)

I: informing (educating and learning about various diseases)

C: considering (acknowledging and respecting patient opinion)

S: spreading awareness (discussing importance of patient’s role as the member of the healthcare team)
summary: To educate individuals of the basics of healthcare, which is important to be aware of for every citizen regardless of health status and to stress the role of patient advocacy to combat potential lack of access, respect, or simple bedside manners.

name: Association for Information Systems
description:
The Association for Information Systems (AIS) at UCR is dedicated to enhancing the technical and professional skills of students of all majors interested in information systems, data analysis, and other related fields with weekly meetings and workshops.

 

The Association for Information Systems (AIS) at the University of California, Riverside is a student-run organization that aims to develop and promote professional and technical skills that are valuable to members interested in information systems, data analysis, and other related fields. Our vision is to spread the importance and impact of information systems to all majors. With weekly meetings, we welcome all majors with opportunities and workshops for networking, relevant certifications, and professional and personal development, and exposure among working professionals, guest speakers, and peers.

Check out our Instagram (@aisatucr) for information about our weekly meetings, and join our Slack for information on IS opportunities and updates!

All of our information can be found here!
summary: The Association for Information Systems (AIS) at UCR is dedicated to enhancing the technical and professional skills of students of all majors interested in information systems, data analysis, and other related fields with weekly meetings and workshops.

name: Partners in Health Engage at UC Riverside
description:
"Health care is a human right. At PIH, this core belief drives everything we do—from hospital bedsides to the streets. Injustice has a cure. Join us in our fight." - Partners in Health

At Partners In Health we hold fundraising events to raise money to help those in third world countries or who are struggling to obtain healthcare. We also hold Advocacy events, calls, and emails, where we speak to Members of Congress and House of Representative Members to pass more legislation, advocating for equal healthcare for all. And Lastly, we create Community Engagement events where we go to placed in the community, explain our mission, and pass out resources to those who need healthcare.
summary: "Health care is a human right. At PIH, this core belief drives everything we do—from hospital bedsides to the streets. Join us in our fight." - Partners in Health

name: The Women's Network
description:
The Women’s Network (TWN) was created for ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being uplifted by their community. TWN offers opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world, network with high-profile speakers and meet peers around their campus and  the country. TWN meets biweekly (twice a month) and holds speaker meetings, various networking events, resume, internship and LinkedIn workshops, alumni networking receptions  and interactive activity sessions to develop relationships with fellow members. Our workshops and guest speakers help prepare you for your next steps and may open your eyes to paths you haven't even considered.

 

The purpose of The Women's Network is to create a community of women that celebrates the desire to achieve, cultivating their ambition in order to prepare the next generation of leaders. TWN strives to provide excellent resources and networking opportunities for young women. To encourage women to be supportive of one another and build each other up. To support young women in any endeavors that they may pursue. To network with potent women in a wide array of different fields.

 

Fill out this form to become a member and join our network: https://www.thewomens.network/jointwn
summary: The Women's Network exists to create a community of women that celebrates the desire to achieve, cultivating their ambition in order to prepare the next generation of leaders. We encourage women to be supportive of one another and build each other up.

name: Hooks for Hope
description: Hooks for Hope is dedicated to making handmade crochet makes for the local community by gathering UCR students who love to crochet. Each crochet item is crafted with love and is one of a kind. Our mission is to bring individuals lots of joy and show them that each of them are unique and loved in different ways, just like their plushies. Part of our mission includes fostering a sense of community for crocheters on the UCR campus. Both new and experienced crocheters are welcome to club events.
summary: Hooks for Hope is an organization that aims to provide handmade crochet makes for the local community and to share the love of the craft with other students.

name: Creat'R Lab
description:
Creat'R Lab Makerspace

 

The Creat'R Lab Makerspace (Orbach Library, Room 140) at the University of California, Riverside is a dedicated maker space open to the students, faculty, staff, and alumni of UCR. Our lab offers a wide range of resources and equipment including 3D printers, a laser engraver, Cricut machines, sewing and embroidery machines, as well as button makers. We provide an inclusive environment where individuals can work on their projects and bring their creative ideas to life.

If you have any inquiries or require more information, we encourage you to visit our workspace or explore our 3D Printing LibGuide. For special events, please reach out to the innovative media librarian or maker services coordinator via phone or email. We look forward to welcoming you to the Creat'R Lab Makerspace and assisting you with your creative endeavors!

 

3D Printing

Educational/academic 3D prints are free. Non-academic/personal prints will be charged at a rate of $0.06 per gram via BearBucks. These charges enable us to maintain and improve our resources, ensuring a high-quality experience for all users.

To submit a 3D print request, please fill out the following form: Creat'R Lab 3D Print Request Form.

The estimated time to complete a print request is 7 - 10 business days.

All payments will be processed with BearBucks or Departmental Recharge Forms

3D Printing Info for Students, Faculty, and Staff

  • Students
  • Faculty and Staff

Pick Up Location and Instructions

  • Pick Up Location
  • Pick Up Instructions

Things to Remember When Submitting a Request

There are a few things to keep in mind when submitting a 3D print request. 

  • Keep These Things in Mind

Embroidery

Are you interested in embroidering but don't have the proper equipment?

Never fear, the Creat'R Lab is here! We have all the supplies you'll need in order to create embroidered objects for yourself, friends or family!

What do you need to do?

This is a free service, however, reservations are required. Reservations open 10 days in advance through this Google Calendar link. 

After securing your reservation, submit your design at our Submit Embroidery Designs Google Form.

Equipment

The Creat’R Lab has equipment for: sewing and textiles, paper cutting and zine-making, electronics, photography, videography, music, virtual reality, and more. 

  • Sewing & Textiles
  • Paper Crafting
  • Digital Fabrication & 3D Scanning
  • "Shop" equipment

Hours

To see the Creat'R Lab Makerspace's hours, visit library.ucr.edu/hours and select the Orbach Library or view our fall quarter hours below. 

  • Monday: 10:00am - 5:00pm
  • Tuesday: 9:00am - 5:00pm
  • Wednesday: 11:00am - 5:00pm
  • Thursday: 9:00am -5:00pm
  • Friday: 9:00am - 5:00pm

Please note: we are closed Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. 

Other Parts of the Creat'R Lab

Explore the other parts of the Creat'R Lab.

3DXP Lab
Robotics Lab

Creat'R Lab Projects

Check out projects that the Creat'R Lab brought to life on our Creat'R Lab Project Showcase website!

Have you used the Creat'R Lab for a project? Fill out our "Share Your Creat'R Lab Project" form and your project can be featured!
summary: The Creat'R Lab is an inclusive living state-of-the-art technology incubator for discovery, innovation, and entrepreneurship located in the Orbach Library

name: UCR Library
description:
WHO ARE WE:

The UCR Library is a place where students find information, do research, and study. There are two libraries on campus, the Tomás Rivera Library and the Orbach Science Library. 

 

WHAT YOU CAN DO AT (OR THROUGH) THE UCR LIBRARY:

Reserve a study room

Borrow laptops for free and use it for up to 24 hours

Access fun books and magazines at ucr.overdrive.com (or through the Libby app). 

Get research help (get ideas, resources on your topic, and more)

Find course reserves (an alternative to buying textbooks)

See more things you can do here: 10 Things to do at the UCR Library

 

EVENTS:

We host free workshops and meetups every quarter for UCR students, faculty, and staff on a variety of topics. See what current workshops we have on Highlander Link and RSVP on our Eventbrite page at ucrlibrary.eventbrite.com. We also hold exhibits you can view in Special Collections & University Archives.
summary: This summary appears when browsing.

name: Design at UCR
description:
If you're interested in learning more about design in the tech world, networking, professional development, tool workshops, design opportunities, and more, then join design at UCR! ✨

Everyone is welcome to join regardless of major and background! We're hoping to build a strong and supportive community of people who share an interest in design! Please feel free to reach out with any questions on our Instagram or our email!

📌 Be sure to follow us on Instagram and join our Slack to get the latest updates on our events including our first general meeting of the quarter, workshops, socials, and more!

Check out our important links here: Linktree
summary: University of California, Riverside’s first & only design club to support students interested in UI/UX, web design, and design thinking.

name: Friends of Médecins Sans Frontières at UCR
description:
Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) is an international medical humanitarian emergency aid and relief organization, founded in 1971, with national offices present in more than 20 countries including the United States. MSF's national offices contribute to the operation of medical humanitaria assistance projects in more than 65 countries worldwide. In 1999, MSF received the Nobel Peace Prize. More information about MSF is available here: https://www.msf.org/who-we-are

The overarching goals of Friends of MSF at UCR is as follows:
(1) To raise awareness for MSF.
Friends of MSF York strives to raise awareness for MSF, the work of MSF in the field, the populations that MSF serves, and the humanitarian issues MSF supports.

(2) To fundraise for MSF.
Friends of MSF at UCR raises money to support MSF, its projects and campaigns.

(3) To encourage others to consider work with MSF.
Although students cannot work with MSF field missions, Friends of MSF at UCR strives to make students aware of future opportunities with MSF in the hopes that they will consider working with MSF later in their professional careers. Friends of MSF at UCR provides information on MSF, the nature of fieldwork, and emphasize that professionals from a wide range of medical and non-medical professions are needed.

 

(4) Form deeper roots within the Inland Empire through community service projects and fundraisers for other local non-profit organizations.

Friends of MSF at UCR do not represent the MSF organization.
summary: We aim to raise awareness and knowledge about MSF’s work and about humanitarian issues taking place around the world, and support MSF in advocacy campaigns. We also aim to give back to the Inland Empire through clinical and non-clinical volunteering.

name: Audeamus
description:
Audeamus was started in 2007 at UC Riverside as a multidisciplinary honors journal for undergraduate students across the University of California (UC) system. Based at University Honors (UH) in UC Riverside, the journal started with the belief that students should be able to showcase any work that was of the highest merit, no matter what it was. There were many academic journals and literary magazines out there, but not as many places where anything could be published.

From 2007 to 2015, Audeamus was an annual journal put together by UCR University Honors students, but containing the work of undergraduate students across the nine University of California schools. In 2016, Audeamus published its 10th edition and decided that with its 11th edition, it would become a national journal to be able to accept submissions from beyond just the UC system.

The Audeamus journal publishes works of all mediums – from research papers to artwork and photography to musical scores – basically anything reproducible on paper. Audeamus is produced by UCR University Honors students of all disciplines and majors; the students do everything from research and advertisement, to journal design and submissions. Flipping through the pages of any issue, you can find an organic chemistry research paper right next to a literary analysis, and just down the table of contents, you might find some photography or a biology paper.

The world is a fairly eclectic place, so why not our journal? That’s what our name means in Latin: Let us dare. Let us dare to publish a journal that is as varied as the world is.
summary: Audeamus was started in 2007 at UC Riverside as a multidisciplinary honors journal for undergraduate students across the University of California (UC) system.

name: University Blood Initiative at UCR
description: As the UCR Chapter of the University Blood Initiative, our goal is to promote, encourage, and organize local blood drives, as well as educate our local community on the importance of blood donation.
summary: The University Blood Initiative at UCR seeks to empower local Riverside blood donor centers and encourage blood donation amongst the Riverside community.

name: United Taiwanese Student Association
description: United Taiwanese Student Association is a cultural and social organization that aims to promote Taiwanese culture and gather all the Taiwanese students at UCR. TSA not only provides various activities and opportunities for members to get engaged, but also allows members to share experiences and their own understanding of culture in order to build a strong connection with each other.
summary: United Taiwanese Student Association is a cultural and social organization that aims to promote Taiwanese culture and gather all the Taiwanese students at UCR.

name: Pacific Math Alliance
description: PMA at UCR is one of the first undergraduate chapters of the Pacific Math Alliance. We aim to give opportunities to students who have been long underrepresented in Mathematics, whether that be help on the road to graduate education, aiding in the transition to the private sector, or just creating a safe, fun environment for learning.
summary: Are you an aspiring mathematician, or just really love math? PMA aims to help underrepresented students learn and prepare for math-related post-graduation careers, make new math friends, and learn more about mathematics as a whole!

name: Music Enthusiasts Society
description:
The Music Enthusiast Society is a place for all music lovers, creators, and collectors to share their interest and appreciation for music and its many genres. 

Whether you're looking to find new music or make friends with similar taste, Music Enthusiast society is the place for you.

Join the discord!

We meet every Wednesday at Watkins 1111 from 8PM to 10PM
summary: The Music Enthusiasts Society is a club for appreciating and sharing of alternative music and its many underground genres.

name: Fitness Angels
description:
Have you always wanted to start hitting the gym but didn’t know where to start? Fitness Angels is open to anyone and everyone, regardless of gender, sexuality, and experience!

  Our purpose: 

At Fitness Angels, we strive to provide students with a safe space, encourage them to participate in various recreational events, and build meaningful relationships. We hope our members find gym buddies, learn proper form, and use machines in a private or small group setting to meet their fitness goals!

 

Throughout the quarter, our hardworking trainers host sessions to help you nail down your form so you don’t feel intimidated or hurt at the gym! We hold various events on and off campus to promote physical and social well-being and provide the physical, safe space athletes need to thrive.

Join our community, where we help everyone to prosper in their fitness journey!

Check out our IG @ucrfitnessangels + join our IG chat : https://ig.me/j/Aba-dVJ44Bs7Ackg/

Also, here is the link to our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/89460742/5M1yJpT5
summary:
Get social and fit with us! Anyone can join, regardless of gender, sexuality, and experience!
Psst... we have personal training at the SRC and online personal training with a certified personal trainer. $0 membership fee.

name: Programming Club at UCR
description: The Programming Club at UCR is a graduate student organization created to help fellow graduate students learn finance and information system industry-specific programs. We explore the fundamentals of multiple programs each year and use a student-teach student method to really bring connectivity within the club. The students decide what to learn as a group, as well as how fast they want to learn and who will teach it. By doing this we are bringing the positive experiences that every student has to offer, and if you are brand new to these programs you can start from the beginning at a good pace, and one day help teach others!
summary: The Programming Club at UCR is a graduate student organization created to help fellow graduate students learn finance and information system industry-specific programs.

name: Rides @ UCR
description: Rides@UCR is a car club & social organization that unites all those interested in the modification and passion for vehicles and driving. Everyone is welcome to our meets, even if you don't own a vehicle yourself. For the latest information on our events, check our Instagram @ridesucr.
summary: Rides @ UCR is a car club social organization that brings together all those interested in the modification, appreciation, and passion for vehicles. Everyone is welcome to our events whether you have a vehicle or not!

name: Highlander Duelists
description:
Highlander Duelists is a club that primarily brings the "Yu-Gi-Oh!" card game by Konami as well as a variety of other Trading Card Games to UCR. Our main goal is to introduce new players to the TCG scene, rekindle the spirit of old-time fans, and provide a place for players to socialize and play with others.

 

Join the Discord with this link!

https://discord.gg/4YvuQhUX6H
summary: Highlander Duelists is a club that brings the "Yugioh" card game by Konami to UCR. Our main goal is to introduce new players to the game, rekindle the spirit of old-time fans, and provide a place for current players to socialize and play with others.

name: Creative Writer's Guild at UCR
description:
Welcome to The Creative Writer's Guild!

 

We are an organization that provides a home for creative writers of any skill level, background or major! Whether you're just starting our or a seasoned veteran, we have something for everybody!

 

Each of our meetings will begin with an informational presentation about some aspect of writing, then we will offer weekly prompts and fun activities to get your creative juices flowing! You can collaborate with others or work on your own!

 

Every month, our club hosts monthly writing competitions based on certain themes, with the top voted works being published at the end of the year!

 

In addition to informational presentations, weekly prompts, and monthly writing competitions, we also offer workshopping, beta reading, editing, and publishing for member proposed works.

 

Our club is here to support all writers in their creative endeavors, whether that involves improving your craft or publishing your first novel! If you like what we have to offer, we would love to see you at one of our meetings! 

 

Come join us Wednesdays at 8pm in CHASS INT South Room 1130!

 

Join our Discord server here for more updates!
summary:
A place where creative writers of any skill, background or major can gather to harbor friendships, create professional careers, work on collaborative projects, get their work published, and have a good time! 

All types of creative writing are welcome!

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is a brotherhood founded on the Cardinal Principles of Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance, and Uplift.

name: Hmong Student Association at UCR
description: We offer social events, cultural workshops, academic support, and outreach to create meaningful connections and lifelong friendships. Whether you're Hmong or simply interested in learning more, HSA @ UCR is a place to grow, engage, and celebrate diversity. Join us as we continue to build a strong and vibrant community together.
summary: HSA @ UCR is dedicated to preserving Hmong culture and fostering a welcoming, inclusive community. We strive to be a "home away from home," providing a welcoming environment for individuals from all backgrounds and identities.

name: Highlander Business Society
description:
Highlander Business Society (HBS) is a professional student organization, established on 4 core values to prepare our members for the professional world: Professionalism, Leadership, Community, and Mentorship. HBS was founded in October 2021. We are open to undergraduate students and all majors. Our goal is to foster a thriving community of business professionals at UCR.

Keep up to date with our events on our Instagram: @hbsucr https://www.instagram.com/hbsucr/
summary: Highlander Business Society (HBS) is a professional student organization, established on 4 core values to prepare our members for the professional world: Professionalism, Leadership, Community, and Growth.

name: Creative Community of Writers
description:
The Creative Community of Writers, or C-COW, is an organization dedicated to building a community for writers of all creeds who wish to improve their craft. We welcome everyone from poets to novelists to playwrights, and hope to form a productive, enthusiastic culture of like-minded students who love the literary arts.

We host weekly workshops for peer critique & review, and have more events planned to help our members collaborate & hone their craft to become the best writer they can be.

Join us!
summary: The Creative Community of Writers, or C-COW, is an organization dedicated to building a community for writers of all creeds who wish to improve their craft. We host weekly workshops for peer critique & review, among other activities and discussions.

name: Club Swim at UCR
description:
Welcome to the UCR Club Swim. Our goal as a club is to bring students together through the sport of swimming and compete in competitions. While also expanding our club members' network/connections by competing against other Universities.

We practice every Monday/Wednesday/Thursday from 6:30 pm-8:00 pm. Follow us on Instagram and join our discord for news regarding practices, meets, and social events that we may have. 

Our website has all the information to sign up: https://swim.ucrhighlanders.org/ 

Practices start Week 2 for Fall 25. Can't wait to see you!
summary: Welcome to the UCR Swim Club. Our goal as a club is to bring students together through the sport of swimming and compete in competitions. While also taking the opportunity to meet with other swimmers from different schools.

name: Competitive Coding Club At UCR
description:
We are a community of students interested in learning and applying advanced algorithms and data structures to algorithmic programming contests. We compete in the ICPC SoCal regionals and we have been climbing the ranks steadily each year.

Discord: https://discord.gg/mBvvmuzue3
summary: We are a community of students interested in learning and applying advanced algorithms and data structures to algorithmic programming contests. We compete in the ICPC SoCal regionals and we have been climbing the ranks steadily each year.

name: Financial Aid Office
description: UCR's Financial Aid Office is dedicated to providing financial assistance to students, in accordance with Enrollment Services objectives and varying student needs; supporting the University’s enrollment and retention goals by providing financial aid from federal, state, University, and private sources and by providing related student support services; and ensuring that financial aid is delivered in a timely manner and in full compliance with all federal, state, University, and outside agency rules and regulations.
summary: Follow us to stay updated on the Financial Aid Office's events!

name: The Comedy Closet
description: An organization for student comedians! The purpose of this organization is to provide amateur comedians and performers the opportunities to express themselves, improve comedy skills, and collaborate with others in the field while having the opportunity to entertain an audience. Our organization focuses on three "pillars" of comedy, stand-up, improv, and sketch comedy.
summary: An organization calling all comedians. The goal is to perform, teach amateurs the craft of talking to an audience, giving feedback to members, and putting on showcases for the masses to see.

name: Biotechnology Club
description:
The Biotechnology Club @ UCR is a professional group at the University of California, Riverside, committed to supporting students interested in the broad field of Biotechnology. We function as a vital link between students' academic studies and the real-world applications in the industry.

 

Our main goal is to help students understand the wide variety of career opportunities in biotechnology. To do this, we form connections with experienced professionals and well-known organizations in the field, encouraging them to share their knowledge and experiences with our members through talks, seminars, and networking events.

 

We also are working towards offering practical workshops designed to improve important skills needed in the biotechnology industry. These workshops will cover topics like why biotechnology is important and what benefits it offers, the job prospects someone can have with a degree related to biotechnology, how to build a strong resume, using industry-specific software and hardware, how to perform well in job interviews, and hands-on experience with lab equipment.
summary: This organization serves as a conduit between UCR students and the biotechnology industry, cultivating professional connections, deepening field understanding, and fostering essential career skills through targeted workshops.

name: The Climbing Club at UCR
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to make climbing more accessible to the student body as well as provide climbers with a community in which they can meet their peers and challenge themselves. 

 

~Why Join Us?~

1. Thrilling Climbing Expeditions: Explore SoCal's stunning natural landscapes, from Bishop to Joshua Tree, we explore it all.

2. Inclusive Community: Connect with climbers of all skill levels in an inclusive, supportive environment.

3. Escape the Routine: Put away your homework, swap your textbooks and lab coats for crash pads and beanies on one of our outdoor climbing trips. Scale to new heights while making unforgettable memories with fellow climbers.

 

ALL SKILL LEVELS WELCOME!  Whether you're a seasoned crusher or have never touched a rock before, our club is open to all.
summary: Are you ready to break free from your studies and experience college life in a way like never before?  Look no further than The Climbing Club at UCR, where adventure meets camaraderie, and passion for climbing the great outdoors thrives!

name: Mujeres Activas en Letras y Cambio Social
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a comfortable space where women can empower each other, speak on the issues affecting women on campus, coordinate with on-campus centers such as the Chicano Student Program (CSP) and the Women’s Resource Center (WRC), engage with our communities through workshops and outreach, help Active Members prepare and navigate academia, and represent UC Riverside at the annual national MALCS’ Summer Institute.
summary: Creating a safe space for womxn to speak out on issues affecting them on campus and within the communities, navigate academia and prepare for graduate school, and represent UCR at the annual national MALCS Summer Institute (held during the Summer).

name: Dependents and Military Family
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a community where dependents and military loved ones can get together and support one another. Our mission is to create a welcoming organization where we can all get together, learn about the benefits we have, and provide support to one another. Our values are respect, integrity, teamwork, and communication.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create a community where dependents and military loved ones can get together and support one another. Our mission is to create a welcoming organization where we can all learn about the Veteran benefits together.

name: Business Strategy Club
description: The Business Strategy Club is a resource aimed at improving your knowledge and skills on strategy from an executive’s perspective. At BSC you will learn business strategy from the corporate to the competitive level while also improving soft skills including leadership, critical thinking and problem solving, and public speaking. Throughout the quarter, you will engage in strategic exercises and case studies where you will compete against fellow members in presenting the best solutions and alternatives to problems faced by today's most powerful executives. If you are unfamiliar with business strategy, feel free to check out our instagram page(@ucrbsc) and newsletter which includes posts that introduce helpful concepts equipping you for the upcoming challenge. Our goal is to provide cases that are easy to understand but challenging to work through.
summary: BSC is a new club focused on business strategy that provides a space for members to practice and grow as future business leaders. BSC will host strategic exercises and case studies throughout the quarter.

name: Loving our Emotions as Asians & Pacific Islanders
description:
We are an organization at UCR that promotes mental health awareness in the Asian & Pacific Islander community by doing stress relief activities and talk circles. Through creating a safe space to share, process, and acknowledge our emotions, we aim to encourage conversations and normalize aspects of working towards a healthy state of mind. 

Click here to go to our website, which has more information and upcoming events! Click here for our linktree, which has all of our socials and member applications!
summary: We encourage conversation within the Asian & Pacific Islander community of UCR. We aim to create a safe space to share, process, and acknowledge our emotions in order to normalize all aspects of working towards a healthy state of mind.

name: School of Business Leadership Council at UC Riverside
description: The School of Business Leadership Council is dedicated to establishing a council of UCR School of Business student leaders to increase collaboration and facilitate resources among School of Business student members, student organizations, and faculty to deliver excellence to the community.
summary: To increase collaboration among School of Business student organizations and the school administration to deliver excellence to the UCR School of Business community.

name: Womxn In the Law
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a supportive community and network for diverse womxn pursuing law school. Women in the Law is designed to create a safe space for minority womxn pre-law undergraduate students that aim to join the legal profession. We value teamwork, networking, and sharing resources to ensure our members are able to combat any social, sexist, or economic adversities they may face while applying to law school.
summary:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a supportive community and network for diverse womxn pursuing law school. A safe space for minority womxn pre-law undergraduate students that aim to join the legal profession.
Established 2022

name: Coachella Valley Free Clinic at UCR
description:
The goal of Coachella Valley Free Clinic is to provide language-appropriate primary care medical services and resources to educate and engage community members. CVFC will operate as a Student-Run Clinic, an environment where students are leading the care under the mentorship of licensed healthcare professionals.

Applications for the organization ARE CLOSED. Follow us on Instagram @coachellavalley.fc for updates on when our application is open.
summary: The Coachella Valley Free Clinic was created by a group of UCR School of Medicine students in 2019 under the supervision of Dr. Ann Cheney and other UCR SOM faculty. CVFC provides free primary care services to underserved and marginalized populations.

name: University Communications
description:
The University Communications team tells the story of America’s fastest rising university. The team works to enhance and protect the reputation of UC Riverside in support of the university’s mission to enhance the quality of life for the people of California, the nation, and the world.

Units include:
• Brand and Creative

• News and Content

• Enrollment Marketing

• Digital Marketing and Analytics

• Video and Photography

• Executive Communications
summary: The University Communications team tells the story of America’s fastest rising university.

name: End Overdose UCR
summary: End Overdose is committed to preventing drug-related death through education, medical intervention, and public awareness. We connect communities with the necessary resources to fight chemical dependency. United, we can End Overdose.

name: Radiant
description: Radiant is a skincare and self-care club that aims to focus on improving and educating the importance of self-care with college students. Our meetings include but are not limited to DIY events, skincare product reviews, and collaborations. Our last couple of collaborations included perfume samples from Valentino and a pop-up product giveaway from the Target and Walmart brand, Avatara. Our mission is to help spread awareness that everyone can feel radiant inside and out.
summary: The purpose of this club is to share the passion of skincare and self-care by encouraging relaxation and well-being in our pursuits to have radiant skin and a radiant soul.

name: Happy English Corner
description:
The Happy English Corner (HEC) 快乐英语角 is a club at the UCR campus that focuses primarily on students and scholars who are native Chinese speakers.

 

The HEC is lead by American Christians who help those originally from China to be introduced to American culture and to practice English with American partners from the community. The HEC desires to be a place for cross-cultural friendship development and sharing.

 

Sign up for the HEC through the HEC flyer WeChat QR codes in the photo gallery!

 

=================================
 
 
快乐英语角 (HEC) 将有志愿者为刚从中国新生免费提供接机服务 (从机场到UCR住处) 及安顿支持。
We are willing to pick you up from the airport when you arrive from China.
 
 
你可以飞往洛杉矶国际机场 (LAX) 或安大略国际机场 (ONT)。安大略离加州大学河滨分校 (UCR) 更近，也更方便。
You can fly to LAX or ONT (Ontario). ONT is much closer to UCR and much easier.
 
 
请使用以下方法之一把你的信息给 Scot (梅强): 电子邮件 "ScotMyhrSoCal@Gmail.com" 或者微信 "ScotMyhrMeiQiang".
Please contact Scot 梅强 by emailing "ScotMyhrSoCal@Gmail.com" or connecting through WeChat ID "ScotMyhrMeiQiang".
 
 
我们很高兴帮助你！
We are happy to help you!
 
 
您的信息仅用于机场接机及英语角与你的沟通。
The information is ONLY for airport pickup and communicating with you about the Happy English Corner.
 
 
=================================
summary: The Happy English Corner (HEC) 快乐英语角 is a club at the UCR campus that focuses primarily on students and scholars who are native Chinese speakers. We help those originally from China to be introduced to American culture and practice English speaking.

name: Beta Psi Omega
description:
Our overarching mission is to provide a supportive brotherhood for students pursuing the biological sciences professionally and academically and to further the advancement of biology as a science and as a profession. We are committed to serving the needs of students and thereby the community. Beta Psi Omega aims to provide passionate students with opportunities, insights, and guidance to success.

Our members have the benefit of having access to class resources, organic chemistry tutoring, exposure to labs on campus, professional development workshops, volunteer experiences, and an exceptional support system. We welcome all majors to get involved (biology, kinesiology, public health, psychology, pre-health, etc), as the skills one will gain are transferable to any field.
summary: University of California - Riverside's first and only co-ed professional biology fraternity,

name: Highlander Consulting Group
description: HCG is the premier student-run consulting group at the University of California, Riverside, committed to nurturing students' professional growth by offering hands-on opportunities to collaborate directly with diverse businesses. Our practical approach enriches our members with invaluable experience in consulting and strategic management, empowering them to excel in the business world.
summary: Highlander Consulting Group is committed to helping students develop their professional competencies, empowering them with the right opportunities to work directly with other Businesses, and giving them hands-on work experience in Business Consulting.

name: Girl Gains
description: Our mission is to promote female weight lifting and empower women to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment free community. Whether you’re a dedicated gym goer or a curious beginner, Girl Gains is a college campus-originated organization and an evolving online community here to support you in every stage of your fitness journey.
summary: Our mission is to promote female weight lifting and empower women to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment free community. Whether you’re a dedicated gym goer or a curious beginner, Girl Gains is a college campus-originated organization and an evolving

name: Muay Thai Club
description: Muay Thai at UCR  is an organization dedicated to the art of Muay Thai. Not only as a physical and mental endeavor but also an inclusive social setting where all are welcomed. This inclusivity also takes into account the diverse range of economic situations at UCR. Therefore, there are no dues or dress code requirements to join Muay Thai at UCR. Our other main priority is safety and personal individual needs. We all come from different backgrounds and different goals. Muay Thai at UCR will do our best to accommodate these differences.
summary: Our goal is to provide a space to train the martial art of Muay Thai. ALL are welcome regardless of skill level or athletic ability. Come train with us and stop by and enrich yourself in the culture of Thailand's national sport!

name: Club Sampling Club at UCR
description: Club Sampling Club offers members the opportunity to “sample” clubs by attending collaborative introductory events throughout the year. This allows students to experience the vast amount of diverse clubs at UCR without the commitment typically required. Members are able to broaden their skill set, network with other students, and enhance their overall Highlander experience!
summary: Club Sampling Club offers members the opportunity to “sample” clubs by attending collaborative introductory events throughout the academic year.

name: Hungry 4 Biscuits
description: Hungry 4 Biscuits is a student-led organization committed to addressing food insecurity across UCR and the Inland Empire. We raise awareness, provide access to resources like Basic Needs and CalFresh, and offer volunteer opportunities that empower students to make a meaningful impact. Through educational initiatives, tabling events, and service projects, we aim to support our community with compassion and purpose. Follow us on Instagram (@hungry4biscuits_ucr) for updates and join anytime to be part of the movement!
summary: We raise awareness about food insecurity, empower students to contribute meaningfully, and offer access to resources like Basic Needs and CalFresh. Through volunteer efforts, we aim to support both the UCR campus and the greater Inland Empire community.

name: Study of Religion Departmental GSA
summary: The Study of Religion Departmental GSA will act as a liaison between graduate students in the Study of Religion Department and the broader Graduate Student Association.

name: Vain Fashion Club @ UCR
description: VAIN at UCR is a space where people of all fashion interests can come together to converse on fashion topics and interest while also promoting a fashion culture here at UCR. With the creation of this club, a sparked interest in on campus fashion shows and runways promotes a safe environment for those who share this same interest in fashion. VAIN plans to promote on campus photoshoots, runways, debates, and conversation all surrounding fashion interests shared on and off campus. This club would work alongside the unofficial UCR fashion digital magazine set up by the creators of this club to help promote the upbringing of a fashion culture at UCR.
summary: Vain Fashion Club @ UCR

name: Riverside Paw Pals
description:
At R Paw Pals we strive to assist animals within the Riverside community. Currently, we primarily work  with the non-profit rescue Underdog Heroes and assist in the day to day care of the dogs residing there. If you love adorable animals and want to help the community this is the place for you! Feel free to message us on our Instagram or join us on discord if you're interested or have any questions!


Discord Link:https:// https://discord.gg/WdkqEadt
summary: Our organization is focused on informing students about the importance of animal shelters and providing aid and assistance through volunteering and fundraising for supplies and necessities.

name: Revive Thrift
description:
Hello everyone!

Revive Thrift is a thrifting club that hopes to provide a safe social outlet for all students and community members at UCR who love thrifting. We wish to promote sustainability and an increased knowledge of the cycle of how consumer waste and fast fashion harm the environment and local community. We also wish to educate members on how to thrift flip and earn extra income by reselling thrift finds online. Our biggest event is our thrift trips (dates posted on our Discord and Instagram) where opportunities to find new clothes, antiques, and friends are present. Whether you've never thrifted before or have much experience in this realm, everyone is welcome!
summary: This club hope to provide a safe social outlet for those in UCR community who share an affinity for secondhand shopping! We hope to promote sustainability and educate members about consumer waste and fast fashion.

name: Success in Science and Technology: Engagement with Role Models
description:
SISTERS stands for Success in Science and Technology: Engagement with Role Models. The goal of this organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school girls to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

Our meetings are solely led by female STEM undergraduate students to ensure that the girls have strong female role models in STEM. Our organization will host female leaders in various STEM disciplines to present about their careers, education, and hardships that they overcame to get to where they are now.

This year, we are planning to continue educating middle school students about careers in STEM through field trips, presentations, social issue seminars, and activities led by leaders in STEM. We will be hosting a hybrid format, and are excited to mentor and inspire middle school students to pursue STEM education/careers.
summary: The objective of our organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school girls to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

name: Goths and Alts of UCR
description: The purpose of this organization is to foster an inclusive community dedicated to the exploration, appreciation, and expression of alternative and goth cultures. Our mission is to provide a vibrant, supportive environment where enthusiasts of all backgrounds can connect through their shared passion for diverse music, art, fashion, and aesthetics within these subcultures. We aim to introduce and educate both newcomers and seasoned followers about the depth, diversity, and history of alternative and goth cultures. By hosting events, educational sessions, and creative showcases, we aspire to encourage artistic expression, cultural engagement, and meaningful connections. Our club welcomes everyone, regardless of prior knowledge or identification with these subcultures, to join us in celebrating and understanding the rich tapestry of individuality and community that defines our shared interests.
summary: Our club exists to create an inclusive haven for the exploration of alternative and goth culture, where members can connect through music, art, and aesthetics.

name: Little Explorers In N'terdisciplinary Sciences To Encourage INspiration
description: Little EINSTEINs is an outreach group aimed at K-5 classrooms within Riverside Unified School District. Our organization visits these classrooms, either virtually or in-person, and gives a short presentation on a STEM-related topic. After this presentation we break the classroom up into groups led by a Little EINSTEINs volunteer. Together they perform a fun experiment while learning the science behind it. Our overall structure consists of four components: warm-up, mini-lesson, experiment/demonstration, and review. Our goal is to create engaging activities in an effort to foster more positive attitudes toward STEM curriculum and encourage students to ultimately pursue these fields when entering higher education.
summary: Our mission is to expose elementary school students to the interdisciplinary nature of STEM, encourage them to enter higher education, and inspire them to pursue a STEM-related career in the future.

name: Citrus Free Care
description:
Striving for equity, inclusion, care, and education, the Citrus Free Care is a non-profit, interdisciplinary student-run initiative to serve the Jurupa Valley, Riverside, and Coachella Valley regions and the diverse needs of the community. A collaborative effort uniting students from different backgrounds, we aim to reduce health disparities, develop educational resources, and provide healthcare accessibility for underserved communities.

 

The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to promote equity through the diversity of every medical-aspiring student narrative. In cultivating an inclusive student base, we strive to empower patients in their health autonomy through our mission for representation. In addition, we wish to serve the Coachella Valley and Riverside communities educational resources for a multitude of topics. Through these educational initiatives, we also aim to empower health educators and make information accessible to the local community.

 

The Citrus Free Care volunteer application is now closed.
summary: The Citrus Free Care aims to provide essential wellness resources to underserved communities, to effectively educate the next generation of healthcare leaders, and partner with local organizations to address the health inequality.

name: BROTHERs
description:
The goal of this organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school boys to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

Our organization will host both male and female leaders in various STEM disciplines to present about their careers, education, and hardships that they overcame to get to where they are now.

This year, we are planning to continue educating middle school students about careers in STEM through field trips, presentations, social issue seminars, and activities led by leaders in STEM. We will be hosting a hybrid format, and are excited to mentor and inspire middle school students to pursue STEM education/careers. 

Despite the organization name, we encourage female and non-binary students to join the organization in order to inspire the younger generation!
summary: The objective of our organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school boys to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

name: Hindu YUVA at UCR
description: Hindu YUVA (Youth for Unity, Virtues, and Action) is one of the fastest-growing Hindu youth organizations across the country with over presence in over 60 different universities. At UCR, we hope to create a safe space for Hindus to freely practice their faith on campus and bring more awareness about what the Hindu faith is really about. We aim to preserve, practice, promote, and protect our Hindu dharma (way of life) by authentically celebrating festivals, hosting guest speakers, and having interactive general body meetings. We welcome everyone interested in our vision of creating awareness around the Hindu faith and a stronger presence of Hindus across UCR. We hope to see you soon!
summary: Hindu YUVA is a voluntary, non-profit, socio-cultural organization that aims to organize the Hindu students on campus in order to preserve, practice and promote Hindu culture, ideals and values.

name: High Performance Computing Club
description: Club to learn more about high performance computing and related computing infrastructure. Annual participation in the Student Cluster Competition. We will continually work towards becoming knowledgeable in the latest advances in high performance computing.
summary: Club to learn more about high performance computing and related computing infrastructure. Annual participation in the Student Cluster Competition.

name: Health Clinical & Administrative Program
description: Our mission is to educate our interns to embrace healthcare through a holistic lens and prepare aspiring healthcare professionals to be well-versed in both the clinical and administrative aspects of healthcare. We have a variety of opportunities for pre-health students at our UCR Health, UCPath and UCR School of Medicine sites.
summary: The Clinical & Administrative Program has been created to provide networking opportunities, clinical experiences, and an administrative background for Pre-Health Undergraduate Students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: Sustainable Developments
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to collaborate and work on projects that promote environmental sustainability and new green technologies. As students, we want to prove that we have the capability of developing new technologies and solving problems that work towards protecting our environment while learning and developing skills applicable to our future professions. In order to ensure that we are a diverse student organization that provides experience to all students of all industries, we will depend on the talents, knowledge, and skill sets of students across many different majors.

Our purpose is to have a platform for motivated students to obtain skills and knowledge in the multi-layered process of planning, design, and construction while utilizing methodology ranging from business, to scientific, and philosophical ideologies.

We want to prove to our community that we, as UCR students, are capable of working collaboratively across multiple disciplines and fields of study together for the betterment of the environment.

We value the environment and want our green projects to be an inspiring factor to raise awareness of creating an achievable sustainable future. Our guiding principles are to be inclusive, innovative, and inspirational as we use our research designs to encourage others to find more sustainable practices.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to collaborate and work on projects that promote environmental sustainability and new green technologies.

name: CNAS Peer Mentors
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide mentorship, resources, professional, personal and academic development to CNAS Students with an emphasis on first year students.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha
