description: Pi Sigma Alpha is the National Political Science Honor Society. It is the only honor society at UCR that recognizes and promotes the high academic achievement in those who are pursing in the field of political science. With specialized events, activities, and membership opportunities offered for those apart, we seek to help the future leaders in this field grow. Be apart for your hard work and get the recognition you deserve.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to stimulate scholarship and interest in Political Science by providing tangible recognition to students who have excelled in the field.

name: The EcoCAR EV Challenge Team at UC Riverside
description:
The latest AVTC series, the EcoCAR EV Challenge (2022-2026), will challenge 15 North American universities to engineer a next generation battery electric vehicle (BEV) that utilizes automation and vehicle-to-everything (V2X) connectivity to implement energy efficient customer-pleasing features, and meet the decarbonization needs of the automotive industry.

Learn more about the competition: https://avtcseries.org/about-the-ecocar-ev-challenge/

Read about our Year 1 achievements: https://www.highlandernews.org/87508/ucr-ecocar-team-wins-two-awards-at-ecocar-ev-challenge-y1-competition-in-orlando-florida/
summary: Home of UCR's Team for the EcoCAR EV Challenge, where we provide students with hands-on experience related to the development of electric vehicles, promoting electrification, STEM education and career opportunities in the automotive field.

name: Supporting and Mentoring Aspiring Students in Highschool
description: SMASH's project goal is to provide free and accessible tutoring for STEM related subjects at Ramona High School. We provide tutoring every Tuesday and Thursday after school and help students with homework problems, create practice problems, and review previous tests/quizzes that students may have taken. With our easily accessible tutoring service, we have been able to help students pass classes and gain a better understanding of the material they struggle with. For future work, we are currently planning on holding a college student panel where UCR CNAS Ambassadors can speak of their experience as college students and give the students of Ramona High School a run down of what to expect as college students. We plan to continue tutoring and help students gain a better understanding of STEM related concepts.
summary: The goal of SMASH is to increase college enrollment in STEM fields at Ramona High School by providing tutoring and mentoring services.

name: Adventure Club
description:
Adventure Club at UCR is a new hiking organization committed to fostering an inclusive community of diverse explorers. Together, we embark on expeditions that not only connect students but also celebrate the breathtaking beauty of Southern California.

Check Out Our Linktree
Check Out Our Instagram (@ucradventureclub)
summary: Adventure Club at UCR is a new hiking organization committed to fostering an inclusive community of diverse explorers. Together, we embark on expeditions that not only connect students but also celebrate the breathtaking beauty of Southern California.

name: Open Arms
description: This organization's purpose is to educate peers about pediatric chronic and terminal illness, as well as to fundraise for organizations helping these populations.
summary: This organization is to educate peers about pediatric chronic and terminal illness, as well as to fundraise for organizations helping these populations.

name: Highlander Economics Club
description: The Highlander Economics Club is a club founded to build a community of economics majors and any students interested in Economics as a whole. Our goal is to support students in creating study groups, finding necessary resources to succeed in UCR, and connecting students with professors and employers. In addition, we hope to educate members about the many facets of economics through speaker events by professors, career panels, workshops, and research opportunities.
summary: The Highlander Economics Club is a club founded to build a community of economics majors and any students interested in economics as a whole by providing access to professors, career panels, speaker events, and research opportunities.

name: SKY Campus
description: Our goal is to create a space where people can have fun and meet new people while experimenting with meditation and breathwork as forms of stress reduction.
summary: There are many different activities people engage in to relieve stress, like art, spending time in nature, etc. The purpose of SKY is to create a community on campus where people can experiment with meditation and breathwork as forms of stress reduction.

name: Art Factory at UCR
description:
Welcome to Art Factory at UCR! Our club's objective is to create a fun, inclusive, creative community on campus. We conduct a series of FREE artistic events throughout the quarter for students to come and enjoy. Please fill out our membership form, and after attending 2 meetings, you can join our Highlander link as an official member!

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGg6v_QL0OxA7koz_dzy3n7MJSqvJsxhOa3Mk23shyCCd2ng/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=109823290533721297721
summary: A safe place and creative place for artists in which they can take advantage of a set studio time to create multimedia art. We strive to give students the opportunity to connect with their artistic selves and disconnect from academic pressures.

name: Omicron Delta Epsilon Economics Honor Society at UC Riverside
description:
Omicron Delta Epsilon is one of the world's largest academic honor societies. The purpose of the Lambda Chapter of Omicron Delta Epsilon is to recognize academic achievement, foster community, and facilitate professional development for students majoring in Economics at UCR

-

We provide our members with opportunities to engage in research and have their works published in both the Lambda Chapter's publication and the National ODE journal, The American Economist. To help prepare our members for the job market and/or graduate school, we host workshops, discussions, networking events, and other various chapter activities.

-

For more information on the membership requirements, please see the constitution below or visit the Omicron Delta Epsilon page on the UCR Economics Department Website.

-

Follow our Instagram to stay up to date on our general meeting dates and locations, socials, and research events! @ODE_UCR 

-

Update: We are also looking for new members to join our org this year! Fill quick interest form on our Linktree on Instagram to apply to one of our many board positions!
summary: The Omicron Delta Epsilon(ODE) Economics Honor Society is a welcoming community committed to nurturing academic and professional excellence. We're open to more than just Economics and Business majors. ALL MAJORS are welcome and we can't wait to meet you!

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
description: Taiwanese American Student Association or TASA for short, is a social/cultural organization at UCR. The organization aims to promote a healthy community for Taiwanese Americans. Although the club is aimed towards Taiwanese Americans, people of all communities are welcomed.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to promote cultural awareness, friendship, inclusivity, family, mentorship, and socializing among students.

name: AGSM PRODUCT CLUB at UCR
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to empower aspiring product managers to learn, implement and showcase the skills required to be a product manager. By being part of the AGSM Product club members can build their network with fellow aspiring PMs.

name: Magician's Club
description:
Do you want to be like David Copperfield or Criss Angel?

We can teach you how to amaze people with magic just like your favorite magician. You can go from not knowing any magic at all to blowing the minds of anyone you meet. Currently, we mainly teach card tricks but stay tuned for new developments. We meet every Thursday from 3-4 pm. 

Don't hesitate to join our discord or follow us on instagram:

https://discord.gg/B8tvrHtA

magicianns_club
summary:
We enjoy showing other people magic. Feel free to stop by to see some tricks and maybe learn some of them. 
Meetings every Thursday from 3 to 4 pm.

name: Italian Club
description: Italian Club is a student org meant to explore, promote, and preserve the culture of Italy through various activities centered around art, cuisine, cinema, music, national holidays, traditions, and other aspects of Italian culture. Our mission is to promote knowledge and appreciation of Italy through engagement with the UCR community.
summary: Join Italian Club and learn more about Italian Culture!

name: College Corps
description:
#CaliforniansForAll College Corps is; a new statewide program uniting students from different backgrounds with a common purpose. This initiative is committed to helping college students graduate on time, with less debt, while addressing societal challenges in the community.

UC Riverside is among one of the many campuses participating in this initiative. College Corps Fellows are recruited to serve over the course of one academic year in local government agencies, schools, and not-for-profit organizations. Service placements focus on three priority areas: K-6 Education, Climate Action, and Food Insecurity. 

We are now recruiting for the 2023-2024 Cohort. Begin the application process at this link.
summary: The Application for the 2023 - 2024 UCR College Corps is officially open! You can apply today to earn up to $10,000 and serve your community.

name: The Shifa Society of Muslim Pre-Health Students
description: The Shifa Society of Muslim Pre-Health Students is a nonprofit organization dedicated to the pre-health professional development of members through the abridgment of deen (Islamic religion) with dunya (worldly life) to foster a community of successful health professionals and sustainable Muslims.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be the professional development of members through the abridgment of deen (religion) with dunya (worldly life) to build a community of successful health professionals and sustainable Muslims.

name: Highlander Combat Robotics at UCR
description:
Highlander Combat Robotics @ UC Riverside is dedicated towards fostering a love for robotics and STEM fields in a vibrant community of passionate engineers, innovators, and technology enthusiasts. Through immersive hands-on learning experiences, we strive to ignite curiousity, empower creativity, and inspire the next generation of leaders in the ever-evolving world of STEM fields.

Highlander Combat Robotics @ UC Riverside aims to offer aspiring engineers a platform to express creativity while acquiring crucial engineering and leadership skills, enhancing member interaction with professors, and preparing students for the engineering industry.

Currently, we are invested in nurturing and guiding students towards comprehending the intricacies of robotics, and teaching them how to go about creating them. In the near future, however, we hope to design, build, and program the robots in order to compete against teams from around the world.

Please Join Our Discord if you are interested in joining the organization!
summary: Highlander Combat Robotics at UCR is dedicated to fostering a love for robotics and STEM fields in a community of passionate engineers, innovators, and technology enthusiasts. We strive to ignite curiosity and inspire the next generation of engineers!

name: Ahlul-Bayt Student Association
description: ABSA is a religious organization that caters to the spiritual and educational needs of Muslim students, with all aspects aligning with the teachings of the Qur’an and sunnah of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and his Ahlul-Bayt (AS). Although we mainly cater to the needs of Shia Muslim students on campus, we are dedicated to providing a safe space and environment welcoming all backgrounds to our events and meetings.
summary: ABSA is a religious organization that caters to the spiritual and educational needs of Muslim students, with all aspects aligning with the teachings of the Qur’an and sunnah of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and his Ahlul-Bayt (AS).

name: Anti Warfare Warfare Club
description:
We aim to study warfare and chase the many rabbit-hole connections to psychology, sociology, economics, political science, and more. We believe in a simple truth. You cannot prevent war, if you do not know how it works.

Our organization helps its members create tabletop exercises that reflect the reality of warfare in order to study the strategy, tactics, and decision-making that happens in command posts, situation rooms, and battlefields across space and time

Join us and let's explore one of the most complex, fascinating, and sobering parts of human nature.
summary: The Anti Warfare Warfare Club seeks to conduct analytical and educational wargaming to study conflict, tactics, international relations, military doctrine, and any other subject that connects to warfare.

name: Star Wars Club at UCR
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to spread the Star Wars fan base across campus and to provide a school-affiliated Star Wars community through related activities.

name: VEX Robotics
description:
Who We Are:

At Ursa Mechanica, we are more than just a team; we are a family united by our love for robotics. Our diverse group of students brings together a wealth of knowledge, skills, and creativity, forming the perfect blend for tackling the challenges posed by VEX Robotics competitions. From programming wizards to mechanical maestros, we have it all, and together, we forge ahead into the realm of cutting-edge robotics.

 

Our Passion:

Our passion for robotics goes beyond mere wires and circuits. We believe in the power of technology to transform lives and solve real-world problems. Through VEX Robotics, we channel this passion into designing robots that not only excel in competitions but also inspire others to explore the wonders of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics). We aim to leave a lasting impact on the world, one robot at a time.

 

What Sets Us Apart:

What sets Ursa Mechanica apart is not just our technical expertise but also our spirit of collaboration and sportsmanship. We understand that the journey is as important as the destination. Every challenge we face, every obstacle we overcome, and every success we achieve, is a testament to our teamwork, determination, and resilience.

 

Our Mission:

Our mission is clear: to innovate, to inspire, and to make a difference. Through VEX Robotics, we hone our skills, learn from each other, and grow as individuals and as a team. We strive for excellence, aiming to push the boundaries of what is possible in the world of robotics.

 

Join Us on Our Journey:

We invite you to join us on this exhilarating adventure into the future of robotics. Follow our progress, cheer for us in competitions, and witness the magic of innovation unfold before your eyes. Together, let’s explore the limitless possibilities of robotics and inspire a new generation of inventors, engineers, and dreamers.

Feel Free To Add Our Socials!

Instagram: @ucr_vex.robotics

Discord: https://discord.gg/CsWsfR7wJQ

 

interested? Join the discord!
summary: We are a robotics club who competes in VEX U. We strive to create an equal opportunity for everyone to learn and apply their knowledge in real world situations. Every year we tackle a new challenge that is presented to by using the engineering process.

name: Highlander Jiu Jitsu at UCR
description:
The purpose of Highlander Jiu Jitsu is to improve the lives of its members by fostering fitness, a sense of community, and continuous personal growth. Highlander Jiu Jitsu will provide regular classes and training opportunities for anyone interested in participating. We will provide a safe and welcoming environment which respects and accounts for the diverse needs and goals of those who come to train Jiu Jitsu.

More information here: https://linktr.ee/ucrbjj

 

Discord link https://discord.com/invite/KUPC4wgMu7
summary: The purpose of Highlander Jiu Jitsu is to improve the lives of its members through the practice of Jiu Jitsu by fostering fitness, a sense of community, and continuous personal growth.

name: Tokusatsu Crisis Community
description: Tokusatsu Crisis Community strives to create a community welcome to both new and old fans of the Tokusatsu film genre/style. Tokusatsu is a Japanese film genre/style that usually features a costumed superhero fighting monsters that are filmed primarily using practical effects. We hold regular meetings where we watch shows/movies/clips and play games to give out Toku merchandise.
summary: Tokusatsu is a Japanese film genre/style that usually features a costumed superhero fighting the evils that are filmed primarily using practical effects. The Tokusatsu Crisis Community strives to create an environment to welcome both new &old fans.

name: KADENCE UCR Cover Team
description: https://youtube.com/shorts/L86wCr1CrJs?si=MZNeoXKfs9XRXZUS
summary: A cover team based around live singing and dancing that is open to anyone who has a love for kpop, jpop, p-pop and more! While this is a cover team everyone is welcome! Officer positions are available! You don't need to sing or dance to join!

name: Avant-Garde A Cappella Group
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to foster a community of driven singers, providing performance opportunities, and experimenting with various musical styles. We aim to bring more attention to the arts and music scene of Riverside.

name: Highlander Riders
description: Motorcycles social and informational club. This will be a safe place for the people who love two or three wheeled bikes
summary: Motorcycles social and informational club.

name: Tea and Go
description: Learn how to play the games of Go and Mahjong, while enjoying a new traditional tea every meeting!
summary:
Learn how to play the games of Go and Mahjong, while enjoying a new traditional tea every meeting! 
Fall 2024 meetings Wednesdays 3-5 pm, follow ucr_tag on Instagram for updates on location.

name: Highlander Skunk Works
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide an environment where real world design and manufacturing experience can be acquired in a variety of topics ranging from computer science, electrical engineering, as well as mechanical engineering. This will be achieved by creating an atmosphere in which fast paced design iteration and prototyping is encouraged. This organization gives opportunities to apply skills learned in class through hands-on experience. Members will decide the projects/competitions that the club undertakes based on a vote from active members that will occur every time the previous project is completed.
summary: We are a small team of dedicated engineers from various backgrounds focused on fast paced innovation and iteration. Our club holds votes at least once a year and sometimes more than once a year on what engineering project/competition to join.

name: Nepalese Student Association
description: The Nepalese Student Association (NSA) is a vibrant and culturally diverse student organization established at UCR. Our mission is to create a welcoming and inclusive community for Nepalese students and those interested in Nepalese culture. NSA serves as a hub for cultural exchange, academic support, and social interaction, fostering a sense of belonging for our members. Our activities and initiatives promote a deeper understanding of Nepal, its rich traditions, and its global significance. With a commitment to diversity, friendship, and knowledge sharing, NSA is dedicated to ensuring Nepalese culture and its values thrive within the university community.
summary: "Nepalese Students Association at UCR: Celebrating Nepalese culture, fostering unity, and providing academic support for graduate students."

name: Society of Student Biophysicists
description: The Society of Student Biophysicists will serve as a hybrid Student Chapter / mini-GSA. As a Student Chapter of the Biophysical Society (BPS), its goal will be to promote academic and professional growth of students, with a particular focus on graduate students, in biophysics and related fields. This would be done through events such as poster competitions, talks, conferences, and seminars / symposia. As a mini-GSA for the Biophysics program here at UCR, it will serve as a hub of information for current and incoming biophysics graduate students and will host events to foster a stronger social connection amongst the biophysics student body. Both 'fronts' of the club will work synergistically to elevate the community of aspiring biophysicists in the UCR campus.
summary: Our mission at SSB is to provide students with the opportunities for academic and professional growth in biophysics and related fields, while also being a hub for all things biophysics here at UCR!

name: Cell, Molecular & Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association
description: Graduate Student Association (Mini-GSA) for members of the CMDB program to interact and network with other members of the CMDB program or other related programs (GGB, MCBL, MCSB, BCH, etc). The GSA is open to all current graduate students in CNAS and has a goal to plan joint events with GSAs of other related programs.
summary: Graduate Student Association for members of the CMDB program to interact and network.

name: The Komorebi Project Student Organization
summary: Komorebi is a term that directly translates to the dappled sunlight filtering through the trees, and in keeping with its name, this organization is dedicated to offering aid and care to the homeless population and individuals in need in Riverside.

name: AI at UCR
description:
The "AI at UCR" club is an academic organization committed to introducing AI and ML enthusiasts to the wider horizons of this evolving field. Recognizing the challenges of diving into the complex world of AI, our goal is to aid beginners in navigating this domain, ensuring they have the right guidance and resources to start their journey. We aim to keep our members informed of the latest news in AI and to host expert guest speakers from the AI realm. As we grow, our vision includes organizing workshops on contemporary AI trends, initiating AI-driven competitions, and providing valuable networking opportunities with industry professionals. Understanding the intricacies of AI/ML and its potential barriers for newcomers, we emphasize our commitment to assist every novice. We extend a warm invitation to students of all majors, encouraging them to join our journey and participate in our diverse range of activities and events.

Join our discord: https://discord.gg/Xgc8h4TX85
summary: We aim to educate students on AI through immersive workshops, interactive seminars with industry experts, and hands-on projects that bridge theory and real-world applications.

name: HanWave
summary: HanWave is a Korean community for Korean students at UCR. Our goal is to provide many opportunities for smooth exchanges between Korean students on campus, and to provide various events and useful information related to campus for Koreans in the school.

name: Chabad on Campus at UCR
description: Chabad on Campus at UCR attempts to provide students (both Jewish and non-Jewish) with the opportunity to experience and engage with Jewish culture as well as promote Jewish values in fun and engaging events.
summary: Chabad on Campus at UCR attempts to provide students (both Jewish and non-Jewish) the opportunity to experience and engage with Jewish culture as well as promote Jewish values in fun and engaging events

name: Pickleball Club at UCR
description:
Welcome to the Pickleball Club at the University of California, Riverside! We offer recreational pickleball for students, staff, and faculty. Join us to improve your pickleball skills, enjoy social events, and make new connections. Open to all skill levels!

How to register:

Step 1: Sign up on the SRC's Do Sports Easy Website - https://recreation.ucr.edu/competitive-sports/sport-clubs

Click on the link above and scroll down to the Pickleball Club, then click "Registration". Follow the steps on the website to officially register through the club (this is a separate process from Highlander Link because we are an SRC-affiliated organization).

Step 2: Bring your Student ID card whenever you attend one of our open plays. THIS IS IMPORTANT. Without your Student ID, the SRC employees cannot check you into the club.

Step 3: Have Fun! See you out there Highlanders!
summary: Welcome to the Pickleball Club at UCR!  We invite you to join us to learn, improve, and enjoy the game of pickleball.  Open to all, beginner friendly!

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at UCR
description: We aspire to mobilize advocacy efforts at UCR surrounding sexual and reproductive health and justice, educate student body, and create lasting change on and off campus.
summary: We aspire to mobilize advocacy efforts at UR surrounding sexual and reproductive health and justice, educate student body, and create lasting change on and off campus.

name: ActiveChild at University of California Riverside
description:
ActiveChild at UCR is the official UCR Student Chapter of ActiveChild.

In partnership with our parent charity, our goal is to improve student health in impoverished schools by providing resources that promote physical activity and healthy habits. We believe that by empowering students to live healthy lives, we can help them achieve their full potential. 

ActiveChild at UCR contributes to these goals in two critical ways: fundraising and awareness. Fundraisers are held to aid ActiveChild in completing its mission in the IE, and weekly chapter meetings are held to help the student body develop a framework to understand health disparities, nutrition habits, and lifestyle choices that influence our pediatric population.
summary:
Hello there! We're UCR’s Student Chapter of activechild.org

Over here we're dedicated to fighting pediatric health inequities by promoting nutrition and healthy habits. We hope you'll consider joining us! ⛹️‍♂️

name: Artificial Intelligence Student Collective
description: The AI Student Collective is a national network of student organizations dedicated to providing accessible AI literacy through pre-professional programs and events. Powered by the Humans for AI nonprofit, we focus on elevating the student experience by providing professional development opportunities, from creating education initiatives to orchestrating industry readiness.
summary: The AI Student Collective is a national network of student organizations dedicated to providing accessible AI literacy through pre-professional programs and events.

name: The Surgical Specialties Club
description: The Surgery Club is for aspiring pre-medical students who are interested in a surgical profession and for students who wish to gain knowledge and experience in the surgical field of medicine. Our goal is to educate pre-med students on the wide range of surgical specialties and subspecialties. Additionally, we wish to guide and educate students on the different pathways to a surgical profession.
summary: The Surgical Specialties Club is for aspiring pre-medical students who are interested in a surgical profession and for students who wish to gain knowledge and experience in the surgical field of medicine.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority Incorporated
description: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority is committed to establishing and maintaining high standards in both academics and ethics in order to promote the ideals of scholarship, service, sisterhood, and finer womanhood. Our goal  is to enhance the societal standing of women by addressing and overcoming the challenges they encounter, nurturing a sense of camaraderie and solidarity among college women, actively engaging in and contributing to the collegiate experience, and delivering dedicated service to the broader community. These values, which represent our dedication to quality, unity, support, and the epitome of refined womanhood, act as our cornerstones.
summary: Zeta Phi Beta was founded on the campus of Howard University on January 16, 1920. The Sorority's international programs such as Z-HOPE (Zetas Helping Other People Excel) through Mind, Body and Spirit serve to empower people from all walks of life.

name: Project Lux
description:
Project Lux is an organization committed to health equity.

We work toward achieving this goal through enrolling individuals experiencing houselessness in health insurance programs, such as Medical, and helping these individuals with other basic health care needs.

Additionally, we work with student volunteers whom we hope to inspire to continue to create change in their communities. We aim to help these volunteers recognize this broad issue and give them a platform through which they can make a tangible impact. We envision a world in which no individual is discouraged from receiving medical aid due to unaffordable services.
summary: Project Lux is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit student-run organization dedicated to bridging the gap between healthcare and houselessness. Our chapter is primarily focused on health insurance enrollment, community service and advocacy.

name: Overall Consultation for All the Pre-med at UCR
description: OCAPUCR was founded to help all the pre-medical, dental, pharmacy, and many more pre and already healthcare students in as many ways as possible. The organization’s mission is to provide our students with as many resources as possible for free to help them successfully continue their educations at each of their professional schools. We believe by being role models and guidance to our students, we can help our students not only succeed in their careers but also become the next generational healthcare leaders who can do the same thing for their patients and students. We also believe these students can grow up to become healthcare workers in Riverside County and the Inland Empire area therefore we are also preparing the next generational physicians and other healthcare professionals for our community too. OCAPUCR is a name given for its meaning “Overall Consultation for All the Pre-med at UCR” to represent our passion for helping and taking care of all the pre-health students at UCR, even though it was named “all the pre-med” We are not limited to only medicine but opened to help other fields of healthcare like dental and pharmacy too. But, for now, we are mainly focused on medicine since we have mentors from that field of healthcare. Also, we are not limited to helping only UCR students, we believe free resources should be available as abundantly as possible to many students, therefore we are striving to help students who are outside of our UCR community too.
summary: “We work to provide the absolute best to our students and we will never stop”

name: Supply Chain Society at UC Riverside
description:
Mission: Inspire, educate and develop our members to be the future leaders in supply chain management, providing opportunities for hands-on training, networking, collaborations, and career advancement to bridge the skill and knowledge gap between academia and industry.

 

Vision: Develop UC Riverside as a center for Supply Chain development, bringing more job opportunities and exposure in the field to the university and its surrounding communities.
summary: Developing leaders in supply chain management at UCR by providing opportunities for hands-on training, networking, collaborations and career advancement.

name: STEMivate
description: This organization aims to inspire students in Riverside and the greater Inland Empire to pursue interests in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) and attain excellence in these fields. The organization facilitates engagement with college-level peers through peer mentorship, integrating STEM learning into classrooms. STEMivate focuses on promoting academic excellence and outreach by delivering college-level material in an accessible and enjoyable manner through college student mentors with expertise in STEM subjects. The primary goal is to help students succeed and excel in STEM-related subjects through a faster and more engaging learning experience.
summary: STEMivate is an organization founded in 2023 by students at the University of California, Riverside. This organization was built on the foundation of motivating students across Riverside county to pursue a passion in STEM.

name: She Means Business
description: The purpose of this Organization is to provide an effective, cohesive forum to educate, promote, and uphold the principles, ideals, and precepts of She Means Business, an undergraduate-focused women in business organization. It shall promote the principles for the professional advancement of its members; stress the principles of equality and respectability for all members; provide a forum for the interchange between members of this organization and the other members of other UCR organizations; and develop an understanding of professional work ethic, critical thinking, general professionalism among its members.
summary: The purpose is to principles for the professional advancement of its members, stress the principles of equality and respectability for all members, and provide a forum for the interchange between members of this organization and other UCR students.

name: Academic Resource Center
summary: The ARC offers a wide variety of programs & services to support students in being successful here at UC Riverside. Please come visit us on the 1st floor of Skye Hall and see how we can best support you in your academic and personal pursuits here at UCR!

name: Pre-Therapy Student Organization
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to inform students about career path options in therapy and learn more about how to get into a therapy program. This organization aims to provide resources for students who have interests in a therapy career.

name: Scotty's Final Call
description:
Stay in the loop about free well-being events on campus! You'll get updates about programs that offer free food and health-promoting supplies. Plus, we’ll let you know when there's leftover food available for pickup

Easy Steps: “SIGN IN” and select “JOIN” to subscribe.

  • Live text/email alerts when available
  • Campus-wide locations
summary:
Stay in the loop about free well-being events on campus! You'll get updates about programs that offer free food and health-promoting supplies. Plus, we’ll let you know when there's leftover food available for pickup

Easy Steps: “SIGN IN” and select “

name: Pi Sigma Epsilon
description: PSE is a dynamic organization dedicated to fostering professional development, particularly in the realms of sales, marketing, and management. It offers a unique platform for students to enhance their skills, engage in real-world business scenarios, and build a supportive network with an emphasis on leadership, ethical conduct, and teamwork.
summary: An organization dedicated to preparing the emerging workforce with the core competencies necessary for success.

name: R'TimeinaFlash
description: The purpose of our club is to provide a welcoming environment for photographers, ranging from any skill type or medium.  Film, Digital, and Phone photography is welcome.  If you simply like photos, this is the club for you.  We will learn not only the technical aspects needed to achieve certain looks but participate in biweekly challenges to practice the  techniques.  We hope to learn and grow together, and most importantly, have fun doing so!
summary: A club for any photographer, of any skill level.  Whether you're a beginner or expert, whether you shoot film or digital.  We are dedicated to helping photographers grasp the technical aspects of photography through the use of biweekly challenges and fun!

name: AAAA - Furries at Riverside
description:
The furry fandom is a subculture interested in anthropomorphic animals. Its origins date back to at least the 1970s in science fiction and comic conventions. The Anthropomorphic Animal Appreciation Association hopes to serve and portray the furry population on campus positively, as well as foster community collaboration between furries at Riverside with other interest-based or academic groups.

We are known best for our passion for the arts, character building, and the costumes we call fursuits! Our website link will direct you to our Discord server. Come say hi!
summary: The Furries at Riverside, a chapter of the Anthropomorphic Animal Appreciation Association, aims to educate members of UCR about the furry fandom and serve as a hub for fans of anthropomorphic animals to interact inside UCR.

name: Outdoor Collective at UCR
description: Outdoor Collective at UCR is an organization that welcomes people of all backgrounds who love nature and are interested in getting involved exploring new outdoor activities.
summary: Outdoor Collective at UCR is an organization that welcomes people of all backgrounds who love nature and are interested in getting involved exploring new outdoor activities.

name: Legal Minds Society
description: We're a group of UCR students who want to expand the pre-law culture on campus. Our goal is to create a platform for pre-law students to connect and grow. We understand the challenges and opportunities in the field, and we want to make it easier for everyone to access the resources and support they need to succeed. By bringing together like-minded individuals, we can create a strong community that maximizes our resources and helps us all reach our full potential. Join us in building a powerful network of pre-law students at UCR!
summary: A group of UCR students whose goal is to expand the pre-law culture on campus. We aim to create a platform for pre-law students to connect and grow. We understand the challenges and opportunities in the field, and we want to make it easier for you

name: Hispanic Student Association at UCR
description:
At HISA we aim to create an environment inclusive of all Hispanic students. By sharing our respective cultures we strive to encourage diversity and create an environment that makes everyone feel welcome. Through various culturally themed events we motivate students to engage in social life and build on campus community.

Follow us on Instagram @hisa.ucr for updates on future events and meetings.
summary: HISA is a cultural association dedicated to the appreciation of the Hispanic community at UCR.

name: 0 Med Coalition
description: We are a student-led nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting the medical needs of people across the globe. Our goals mainly focus on increasing accessibility to healthcare products depending on each community’s need, from medicine to isopropyl alcohol or anything else. Through education and awareness, we aim to break down barriers to medical education, inspire future healthcare professionals, and bring attention to the pressing health needs of people.
summary:
Nonprofit to help spread awareness of medical issues outside of the US. 
Connecting you to practicing Doctors, helping eliminate medical supply shortages abroad: Med Coalition

name: Health Exploration and Rotational Training Society at UCR
description: The Health Exploration and Rotational Training Society (HEARTS) at UCR is devoted to assisting undergraduates in discovering their interests through exposure to various healthcare professions. Our organization serves as a forum for enhancing student knowledge of health careers. This includes our unique rotational training practice, which helps students connect with direct physician and patient care experience, professional development skills, and opportunities for educating populations of interest. We serve to connect our members to programs and opportunities in various healthcare fields. Most importantly, we aim to create a supportive environment for future physicians and scientists while supporting our members in their journey to accomplish their future endeavors.
summary: HEARTS at UCR fosters undergraduates' healthcare exploration through diverse experiences. Our unique rotational training program offers direct exposure to healthcare opportunities. We create a supportive environment for aspiring physicians and scientists

name: LEO's Club at UCR
summary: The purposes of this organization shall be representing qualities of leadership, excellence, and opportunity while serving the Riverside community, alongside our student body.

name: Tartan Seoul
description: Tartan Seoul is an all-inclusive K-pop dance team for students to come together with a passion for dance and Korean Culture! All UCR students are free to attend events to socialize and take part in projects with their peers who share similar interests in a safe, enjoyable environment in our team.
summary: Tartan Seoul is an all-inclusive K-pop dance team for students to come together with a passion for dance and Korean Culture!

name: The Young Americans for Liberty at UCR
description:
We are Young Americans for Liberty (YAL). As Americans we recognize the naturally-given rights of life, liberty, and pursuit of happiness as well those set forth by our Founding Fathers within the constitution.  We recognize that while the unalienable rights of life, liberty and pursuit of happiness are not granted by the government, but inherent of every human being and necessary for the existence of a human being, other rights such as. While not natural human rights, our country was created to protect the freedoms of the individual and directed by we the people.

We recognize that freedom deserves responsibility and therefore we hold ourselves to a high moral character and conduct. Integrity emphasizes our stance towards action. Principle defines our outlook towards government. Peace and prosperity drives our ambitions towards our countrymen.

We inherit a corrupt, coercive world that has lost respect for voluntary action. Our government has failed and dragged our country into moral decay. The political class dominates the agenda with a violent, callous, controlling grip. And, for this we do not stand.
summary: In YAL at UCR, we champion liberty and freedom for all, committed to combating injustices both on campus and globally. Through weekly meetings, we tackle social, political, and economic issues, and advocate against policies that contradict our beliefs.

name: Powerlifting at UCR
summary: The purpose of our organization is to create a diverse community of powerlifters and to send our athletes to national and world level competitions. We encourage new and veteran lifters to join our club so we can share our passion for this sport.

name: Sikh Collegiate Federation
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to forge a Gurmat-inspired generation through creative leadership development, community organizing, and social activism. Our mission is to build a Sikh political campus voice by strengthening the power of Sikhs.

name: The Play Project
description: The Play Project is an initiative aimed to connect to the Riverside community through creating fun and playful opportunities for children to exercise, whilst educating children on the importance of a playful lifestyle.
summary: The Play Project strives to serve the community of Riverside, creating playful and engaging activities with the youth.

name: Black Psych Society
summary: B’Psych will connect students with similar backgrounds and create more accessible opportunities to advance their careers through mentorship, networking, and more. B’Psych will strive to unite and welcome Black scholars to the broad field of psychology.

name: MEDDL Club
description: The MEDDL Club is dedicated to fostering creativity and innovation among students in the field of Mechanical Engineering by encouraging them to make full use of the Mechanical Engineering Design and Development Laboratory (MEDDL). Our mission is to provide a platform for students to collaborate, experiment, and apply their theoretical knowledge in practical, hands-on projects. Our makerspace is home to various 3D printers, laser cutters, CNC milling & routing machines and more! All of which is available to UCR students.
summary: The MEDDL Club is dedicated to fostering creativity and innovation among students in the field of Mechanical Engineering by encouraging them to make full use of the Mechanical Engineering Design and Development Laboratory (MEDDL)

name: Spectra at UCR
summary: Spectra is a national professional association whose goal is to empower and support queer and trans individuals in mathematics.

name: Gotilo Gujarat
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a community that is welcoming to both Gujarati and non-Gujarati people. Our primary objective is to celebrate the rich Gujarati culture and learn about each other's values. As well as bringing awareness of the Gujarati culture on campus with traditional events. At UCR, we strive to create a safe and welcoming environment for everyone who wishes to be a part of our community.
summary: Our organization aims to create a welcoming community for both Gujarati and non-Gujarati people. We celebrate the rich Gujarati culture, learn about each other's values, and raise awareness on campus through traditional events.

name: Real Estate Club
description:
• Our mission is to provide real estate knowledge and resources to those interested in pursuing a potential career in real estate. We serve as a networking hub for all individuals interested in real estate. No real estate background is necessary to be a part of the Real Estate Club community as we are open to all. 
  • We aim to become a trusted and influential group, helping both individuals and the industry grow by focusing on the concepts and practical methods of real estate.
summary: Embodies reliability in the real estate industry, integrating proven business models and leveraging experienced leaders. We foster growth through continuous education within the community, empowering informed decision-making.

name: History Enthusiasts at UCR
description: To assist students interested in history(regardless of major) by promoting interactions within the History department, cultivating a vibrant historical community, and mobilizing on-campus and off-campus participation in history-related events.
summary: To assist students with an interest in history by promoting interactions within the History department, cultivating a vibrant historical community, and mobilizing participation both on-campus and off-campus in history-related events.

name: Darbar
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to have the youth experience the mysticism embodied in Indian and world heritage, which has been in decline over the last several years. The Chapter serves as a platform to showcase the rich Indian culture by facilitating performances of top classical artists at UC Riverside, putting together workshops, and bringing together a community of students who appreciate cultural heritage. Through these initiatives, we also aim to uplift the overall wellness of those associated.
summary: Darbar serves as a platform to showcase the rich Indian culture by facilitating performances of top classical artists at UC Riverside, putting together workshops, and bringing together a community of students who appreciate cultural heritage.

name: Biochemistry & Molecular Biology Graduate Student Association
description:
The Biochemistry and Molecular Biology Graduate Student Association (BcMBGSA) at UCR is a student-run organization dedicated to creating supportive environments that sustain graduate students by promoting professional development, academic excellence, networking opportunities, mental wellness, and a strong scientific community through social, academic, and community programs. 

 

Also, Through our Sustainability and Outreach Program, we support undergraduate sustainability by assisting with research, building bridges between undergraduates and graduate students, and creating platforms for graduate students to present their research to undergraduates and local high schoolers, inspiring the next generation of scientists.

 

BcMBGSA serves as a vital link between BCMB graduate students and the broader campus community. We are dedicated to enhancing the overall experience of BCMB and other graduate students within the College of CNAS by fostering collaboration and networking.
summary: BcMBGSA strengthens the scientific community, professional growth, wellness, and collaboration among UCR graduate students and faculty. We promote outreach & sustainability by connecting grads, undergrads, and local students to inspire future scientists.

name: Mindbody at UCR
description:
Our mission is to research, share, and apply evidence-based strategies to enhance physical and mental well-being.

  • Journal Club – We analyze research articles, discuss key findings, and explore real-world applications. Each session, we present 1–2 articles from psychology, biology, or medicine (or any article related to physical or mental health). We also help students get involved in research labs.
  • Socials – We host fun activities like board game night, giving members a chance to connect and unwind.
  • Education Committee – Every other week, we create a slideshow for our Instagram page on a condition or disease related to sports or nutrition. We also plan to visit high schools in the Riverside Unified School District (RUSD) to present current research.
summary: The primary purpose of Mindbody is to promote awareness of mental and physical health strategies, such as the effects of sleep, nutrition, and exercise.

name: Egyptian  Student Association
description: The Egyptian Student Association (ESA) at UCR is a student-led organization committed to fostering a welcoming space for Egyptian students and anyone interested in Egyptian culture. Through a variety of cultural, social, and educational events, ESA celebrates Egypt’s rich heritage while creating opportunities for students to connect, build community, and engage in meaningful dialogue. Our mission is to promote cultural awareness, support one another academically and socially, and share the beauty of Egyptian traditions with the broader campus community.
summary: The Egyptian Student Association is an organization that focuses on creating a safe space for Egyptian Students and those interested in Egyptian culture. ESA’s goal is to allow members to engage in educational, cultural, and social activities.

name: Racquetball Club at UCR
description: Our club’s all about building a welcoming racquetball community at UCR, where students can play, learn, and talk about the sport—whether you’re brand new or competitive. We run regular practices focused on improving skills, having fun, and enjoying friendly matches. Plus, we’re passionate about introducing racquetball to more people around campus and our community through events and outreach that invite everyone to discover and enjoy the game. Currently we boast about 30 members, and have players of all skill levels involved. Join our discord to learn more about when we hold our practices as our events may not up to date: https://discord.gg/wrSedXB.
summary:
UCR's first dedicated Racquetball Club!

Open to everyone, whether you’re brand new to the sport, experienced on the court, or just curious about racquetball. Join us to play, learn, and connect—no experience needed!

name: Body, Hair and Self Care Collective at UCR
description: Our mission is to empower individuals to embrace their unique beauty through community, education, and access to high-quality products, fostering confidence and self-expression in a supportive and inclusive environment.
summary: Our mission is to empower individuals to embrace their unique beauty

name: Highlanderettes
summary: UCR Spirit Team enhancing the fan experience through cheer and dance.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi
description: The purpose of the Kappa Alpha Pi Fraternity at the University of California, Riverside shall be to provide students with the opportunity to learn about the Law & Graduate School application process, Law School, and the profession of law. This goal will be achieved by providing the membership with the opportunity to participate in events focused on law and service designed to help the legal community. This fraternity will also provide its members access to people in the area of law and government. Above all, this fraternity and its membership will strive to create a fellowship in the form of a fraternal bond between members of the organization, thereby providing the members with personal experiences to aid their search for information about law and law-related areas.
summary:
The purpose of the Kappa Alpha Pi Fraternity at the University of California, Riverside shall be
to provide students with the opportunity to learn about the Law School application process, Law
School, and the profession of law.

name: Health Professions Association
summary: HPA is an organization that aims to connect the undergraduate students of UC Riverside who are interested in pursuing a career in healthcare through mentorship, social events, professional development events, and community service events.

name: Bright Smiles Medical Association at UCR
description: At Bright Smiles Medical Association, we aim to empower the local community, especially underserved communities, by promoting dental health awareness and addressing oral healthcare disparities. We plan to equip undergraduate pre-dental students with the tools and knowledge necessary to make a meaningful impact. Through interactive workshops, volunteerism, and guest speakers, we seek to provide comprehensive education on oral health. Our initiatives include oral hygiene workshops, hygiene kit creation, community health fairs, and developing a health hub with educational materials on topics like the link between oral health and diseases, dietary impacts on oral health, and preventive practices. We also plan on organizing trips to local schools to teach proper dental care. Through these efforts, we strive to foster a commitment to preventive care, education, and community service while promoting oral health equity for all.
summary: Bright Smiles Medical Association serves underserved communities by promoting dental health awareness and addressing oral healthcare disparities. We offer volunteerism, workshops, and other events to promote oral health equity, and preventive care.

name: LabLink
description:
Our goal is to promote equitable research access by creating online research training modules that will better prepare students for lab research.

This club is a part of an effort to help connect students with research faculty in order to expose willing candidates to scientific (life/physical/engineering) research, while connecting faculty with UG assistants. To do this, we have partnered with Gladis Herrera-Berkowitz and the UG research office to improve the research portal and explore future collaborations to reach more students. 

We aim to provide scientific literacy and improve professional skills through communication workshops, guest speakers, and resume/CV builders.

LabLink Introduction
summary: Our goal is to promote equitable research access by creating online research training modules that will better prepare students for lab research as well as improving scientific literacy and professional skills.

name: Ethics Bowl at UCR
description:
We meet on Tuesdays at 8pm until 10 pm.

Fixed discord link:

https://discord.gg/Nj5hQ3Rhhf
summary: At this club we prepare for the annual Ethics Bowl by analyzing cases, developing arguments, and researching. During the off-season we house our own tournaments and continue discussing ethical dilemmas.

name: R' Karters
description: The Mario Kart Club is a space for Mario Kart lovers to come together and play Mario Kart. Meetings involve playing on one or two large servers as a group with the rest of the club. There is also the opportunity to win prizes via monthly and quarterly tournaments.
summary: A safe space for Mario Kart lovers to come together and play Mario Kart.

name: Fostering Loving Hands
description: Fostering Loving Hands aims to foster community engagement, service, and compassion. FLH strives to empower UCR students to serve and advocate for underserved populations through education about local issues and disparities and creating and connecting students to opportunities to directly help these communities.
summary: Fostering Loving Hands aims to foster community engagement, service, and compassion. FLH aims to empower UCR students to serve and advocate for underserved populations through education and creating opportunities to directly help these communities.

name: FirstGen Leaders and Innovators
description: The purpose of this organization is to guide and help first-generation students who find an interest in the business world. This program is focused on prioritizing the network connections students can obtain from similar first-generation students with diverse ethnicities and viewpoints in business. The fundamental purpose behind the organization is to support and guide students without knowledge of the business world and teach them the importance that unity holds to a successful future. Overall, the most important value within this organization is to prove and demonstrate that together working with the same bright end goal anything is possible. In this organization, members are to gain trust, confidence, essential skills, and professionalism in the real world. With time and experience, self-promoting your business utilizing the internet as a key to success will be implemented into each member's set of skills, and will have a basic understanding of how to promote a business.
summary: Gathering a community of first-generation students who seek advice or guidance on the fundamentals of the business world or for those who find a passion for business. Being united as one to strive for the best outcome possible and learn together.

name: Finance and Investment Banking Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to bring together like-minded individuals who are keen on growing their knowledge of investment banking. Our mission is to provide members with comprehensive professional development through presentations, workshops on financial modeling, valuation techniques, and investment analysis. We aim to facilitate valuable networking opportunities with industry professionals by inviting them to give presentations on their paths to success, insights into breaking into the industry, and other relevant experiences. Additionally, FIBA is committed to preparing members for successful careers by offering resume review sessions, strategic career guidance, and real-life experience. FIBA also manages an investment portfolio, giving members hands-on experience with investment strategies and portfolio management. Furthermore, FIBA competes in the National Investment Banking Competition (NIBC), which offers members industry-level experience. Through these initiatives, FIBA seeks to equip its members with the knowledge, skills, and connections necessary to excel in the competitive world of investment banking.
summary: FIBA brings together individuals passionate about investment banking, offering professional development through workshops, industry networking, hands-on experience with an investment portfolio, and competes in the National Investment Banking Competition.

name: Queer Leadership Council
summary: The Queer Leadership Council (QLC) at UCR is dedicated to supporting and empowering LGBTQ+ organizations and individuals on campus. We provide essential resources, emergency relief, and advocacy

name: We R’ Dancers
summary: Student-led organization dedicated to celebrating and fostering a vibrant community of performers.Open to dancers of all backgrounds, providing a supportive space to express yourself, grow your skills, and make connections."Led by students, for students"

name: BAPS Campus Fellowship
description: The purpose of the organization is to unite students on UC Riverside’s campus and provide opportunities to learn about the Swaminarayan way of life within Hinduism. It will address issues affecting Hindu and Swaminarayan youths through group discussions and campus events. Students will be able to discuss topics that affect them in college and will be able to learn methods on balancing being a college student and practicing the Swaminarayan faith.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to unite students on UC Riverside’s campus and provide opportunities to learn about the Swaminarayan way of life within Hinduism.

name: Cricket Club
description: Our cricket club is a casual and fun group where members come together to enjoy playing cricket. We focus on fostering teamwork, improving skills, and having a great time on the field. While most of our activities involve friendly matches among ourselves, we also occasionally compete in games against other University Colleges (UCs), giving members the chance to experience competitive play in a relaxed environment. Whether you're looking to sharpen your cricket abilities or just enjoy the sport with fellow enthusiasts, our club welcomes players of all levels.
summary: Our cricket club is a casual, fun group where members enjoy playing together, focusing on teamwork, skill improvement, and having a great time. We welcome players of all levels, whether you're looking to sharpen your skills or just enjoy the sport.

name: Enlight Education
description:
Enlight Education has been dedicated to cultivating passion and academic excellence in STEM to empower the next generation of innovators and leaders through high-quality education. The school's general science curriculum lacks the wide range of scientific knowledge many kids are curious about, and traditional science camps are out of budget for some. We at Enlight hoped to spread knowledge to the underserved youth and create a community that would allow kids to fully explore the world of STEM.

At Enlight Education, we consider every child's passions and talents and aim to grow their knowledge through our various activities. We focus on a range of fields, including Science, Biotech, Engineering, and Computer Science. Our academic mentors create unique lesson plans, experiments, and activities to ensure not only academic improvement but also a lifelong passion for learning and STEM.
summary: Enlight Education has been dedicated to cultivating passion and academic excellence in STEM to empower the next generation of innovators and leaders through high-quality education.

name: FIT - Fashion in Theory
description: We are a fashion and health Magazine exploring how they intersect. We do monthly issues with coordinating shoots. We write about different topics like superfoods, exercise fads, and even skincare. We also sew, thrift, and craft.
summary:
We are a fashion and health Mag exploring how they intersect. We do monthly issues with coordinating shoots. We write about different topics like superfoods, exercise fads, and even skincare. We also sew, thrift, and craft. 
#thoughtdaughter
#hmm

name: HerEssentials
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be committed to addressing hygiene needs and menstrual care by providing bras, menstrual products, and essential hygiene items to individuals in need. We are dedicated to supporting underserved communities by supplying undergarments such as underwear and socks to unhoused individuals. Additionally, we place a special focus on children, partnering with local elementary schools to provide undergarments. We strive to address and reduce healthcare disparities in our local underserved communities, caused by limited access to these essentials, bridging the gap through neighborhoods HerEssentials.
summary: The organization's mission is committed to addressing hygiene needs and menstrual care by providing bras, menstrual products, and essential hygiene items to individuals in our local underprivileged community.

name: Highlander Sports Business Association
description: HSBA is a student-run organization dedicated to empowering a network of individuals who aspire to work in the field of sports business, connecting students to the dynamic world of sports management, marketing, and entrepreneurship. Through workshops, speaker series, and hands-on experiences, we bridge the gap between academic learning and industry practice, empowering our members to shape the future of the sports business.
summary: Highlander Sports Business Association (HSBA) is an organization that aims to educate and empower UCR students who plan to pursue a career in sports business through providing students with opportunities for networking and professional development.

name: Melody Maker at UCR
description: As students, we understand how school can be stressful, but we also recognize the healing and stress-relieving power of music. Our goal is to offer an avenue for students to embrace music as a way to unwind and connect. Through learning and playing together, students will not only develop musical skills but also create lasting memories and build a supportive community.
summary: We will provide instruments, such as ocarina and ukulele, and learn how to play it as a club. Our goal is to create a community that focuses on sharing and embracing music for anyone who is looking for a place to both practice and have fun at UCR.

name: Heal the People
description:
At Heal the People, we are dedicated to addressing health disparities and the social determinants of health affecting Riverside County’s most vulnerable populations, particularly unhoused residents. Our mission focuses on creating lasting impact through action and advocacy.

As members, we will volunteer to gather and distribute social services and resources to unhoused individuals at local shelters. We’ll collaborate with social workers to understand community needs, health disparities, and the factors that influence the well-being of our community members.

Additionally, we will lead initiatives to protect the rights of unhoused residents by advocating for stronger laws and increased funding from local government. Through outreach, partnerships, and advocacy, we aim to empower individuals experiencing homelessness, enhance access to resources, and promote dignity and equity for all.
summary: At Heal the People, we focus on addressing social determinants of health impacting Riverside County’s unhoused population. Through advocacy and action, we aim to protect their rights, increase access to resources, and push for stronger laws and funding.

name: Data Science Society
description: Data Science Society (DSS) is the leading student-run Data Science organization on campus. We welcome all students passionate about any aspect of Data Science, as well as those curious to learn more about the field. Our mission is to foster a strong student community while providing valuable resources and opportunities for career development. Throughout the year, we will host technical workshops, social events, and other exciting activities to help members grow both personally and professionally in Data Science.
summary: The leading student-run Data Science organization on campus. We welcome all students passionate Data Science, as well as those curious to learn more about the field!

name: AscendUCR
description:
AscendUCR is the official student chapter of the overlaying organization Ascend. Ascend is the largest nonprofit organization filled with a global network advancing Pan-Asian professionals in the workplace. It’s our mission to build community and ignite change by developing, elevating, and empowering the API leaders of tomorrow. 

🔸Connect. Inspire. Ascend.
🔸🔸Pan-Asian Leadership Org for Professional Networking
🙌 Open to ALL majors/cultural backgrounds
summary: Ascend is the largest global network advancing Pan-Asian professionals in the workplace. It’s our mission to build community and ignite change by developing, elevating, and empowering the API leaders of tomorrow.

name: Classical Studies Society at UCR
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to build community through discussion of the ancient world, its history, its art, and its culture, as well as being a positive force in the goings-on of members’ academic and social lives.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to build community through discussion of the ancient world, its history, its art, and its culture, as well as being a positive force in the goings-on of members’ academic and social lives.

name: Warhammer Club
description:
The Official Warhammer Club! If you are a new player wanting to get into Warhammer, or a veteran looking for someone to play some games we got it all! Don't care about playing and want to nerd about the lore, or show off your painting skills? Join us!

https://discord.gg/VTvHCqB3B8
summary: The Official Warhammer Club! If you are a new player wanting to get into Warhammer, or a veteran looking for someone to play some games we got it all! Don't care about playing and want to nerd about the lore, or show off your painting skills? Join us!

name: Calisthenics Club
description: The Calisthenics Club is a student-led organization focused on enhancing physical fitness and personal growth through bodyweight training. The club provides a welcoming space for students of all fitness levels to work on strength, flexibility, and endurance. Through weekly skill sessions, you will learn to master: front lever, handstand, muscle up, L sit, Plance, etc.
summary: The Calisthenics Club is a student-led organization focused on enhancing physical fitness and personal growth through bodyweight training. Through exercises such as pull-ups, push-ups, squats, and core members will gain new flexibility and skills.

name: The Care Equity Lens
description: Marginalized groups are not treated equally in healthcare and healthcare research. They often face additional discrimination that hinders their ability for proper treatment. Our organization gives a voice to those in need and helps bring awareness to healthcare professionals. Events include fundraising, panels, volunteering, presentations and collaborations with other groups, and spreading information through social media.
summary: This organization's goal to raise awareness to the inequalities in healthcare and research that affect many individuals. We aim to inspire current and future generations of doctors and researchers to take action towards improving the healthcare.

name: Russian-Speaking Student Association
summary: The Russian-Speaking Student Association is founded on a commitment to celebrating, understanding, and sharing the Russian Language among the members of the post-Soviet Diaspora and language learners and enthusiasts.

name: Venture Capital Association
description:
The Venture Capital Association (VCA) at UC Riverside is a dynamic student organization focused on educating and empowering students in venture capital, entrepreneurial finance, and the startup ecosystem. VCA provides members with valuable hands-on experience in evaluating investment opportunities, performing due diligence, and understanding the intricacies of venture capital funding.

Through a wide range of events, including workshops, guest speaker sessions, and networking opportunities, VCA connects students with industry leaders such as venture capitalists, entrepreneurs, and investors. The organization also actively promotes entrepreneurship, offering resources and guidance for students interested in developing and launching their own business ventures.

Whether students aim to explore careers in venture capital, start their own companies, or simply expand their knowledge of the startup world, VCA serves as a valuable platform for growth, learning, and professional development.
summary: The Venture Capital Association (VCA) at UC Riverside educates students on venture capital, finance and entrepreneurship, offering hands-on experience, networking opportunities, and resources to develop their own ventures.

name: Designing Dreams
description:
Designing Dreams is UCR's sewing club!

 

We hold follow along step-by-step workshops which are designed for COMPLETE beginners so you can learn to design your sewing dreams.

You can also design other’s dreams by participating in our volunteer opportunities avaliable only for active members. This group of sewists will volunteer at shelters/hospitals to make the kids there the outfits of their dreams. 

We also hold very fun events from time to time for our members to show off their sewing creations!
summary: Learn to sew from UCR’s very own sewing club! Join our step-by-step workshops, our volunteer opportunities, and take part in our fun events to show off your sewing creations!

name: Abide College Ministry
summary: A Christian campus ministry from the Church of Southland, Chino that looks to create a space for all students to build a strong community with other Christians and have meaningful conversations to foster personal and spiritual growth.

name: Real Estate Careers and Horizons
summary: REACH is dedicated to helping students get educated about the different career opportunities in the real estate industry through mentorship & training to acquire the skills necessary to break into the industry, whether an internship or full-time job.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at UCR
summary: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers aims to propel those of Asian descent through opportunities in both their career and resume building aspect, filling a hole needed in the UCR community.

name: Aviat'R
summary: Aviat'R empowers students with skills and resources to fuel their passion for aeronautics. We pioneer advanced Unmanned Aerial Systems to compete at the highest levels, driven by excellence, innovation, and leadership.

name: Turning Point USA at University of California Riverside
description: Turning Point USA has embarked on a mission to build the most organized, active, and powerful conservative grassroots activist network on college campuses across the country. With a presence on over 3,500 campuses, Turning Point USA is the largest and fastest-growing youth organization in America.
summary: WHAT WE BELIEVE: -The United States of America is the greatest country in the history of the world -The US Constitution is the most exceptional political document ever written -Capitalism is the most moral and proven economic system ever discovered

name: Students For Iraq
description: The purpose of Students for Iraq (SFI) at UC Riverside is to provide a welcoming space for students to connect and celebrate the rich cultural heritage of Iraq. We aim to create a united, inclusive community highlighting Iraqi culture’s beauty and diversity. By sharing Iraq’s history and commemorating significant national events, SFI seeks to foster understanding and a genuine, positive portrayal of Iraq to the UC Riverside community.
summary: The purpose of Students for Iraq (SFI) at UC Riverside is to provide a welcoming space for students to connect and celebrate Iraq’s rich cultural heritage. We aim to create an inclusive community that highlights the beauty and diversity of Iraqi culture.

name: Labor Council for Latin American Advancement
description: The Labor Council for Latin American Advancement (LCLAA) Student Alliance is dedicated to promoting social justice and economic equality for the Latine community in the United States. By establishing student chapters on college campuses nationwide, we empower students to become advocates in their community, fostering leadership, education, and political engagement. Our mission is to provide college students with opportunities for professional development, access to critical educational resources, and the skills needed to champion policies that benefit Latine workers and their families. Through our affiliated chapters, we aim to cultivate a new generation of informed, motivated, and socially conscious leaders committed to the principles of equality and justice.
summary: The LCLAA Student Alliance empowers college students nationwide to advocate for social justice and economic equality for the Latine community. Through campus chapters, students gain leadership, education, and advocacy skills to support Latine workers.

name: National Arab American Medical Association NextGen Chapter at University of California, Riverside
description:
The NAAMA NextGen chapter at UCR is dedicated to empowering students of all backgrounds who are passionate about pursuing careers in healthcare. Our chapter’s mission centers on fostering a supportive and inclusive community where students can access vital resources that promote healthcare advocacy, professional growth, and meaningful community service. Through a combination of mentorship programs, networking events, skill-building workshops, and leadership opportunities, we provide our members with the essential tools they need for career development and a smooth transition into the healthcare field.

One of the distinctive aspects of NAAMA NextGen at UCR is our focus on connecting students with renowned Arab American healthcare professionals from across various specialties. These accomplished professionals, who have made significant contributions to healthcare, bring a wealth of knowledge, experience, and inspiration to our community. We invite these leaders to campus for exclusive guest lectures, panel discussions, and interactive networking events where students can gain valuable insights, ask questions, and engage in meaningful conversations. This direct access to industry leaders offers students the chance to learn not only about the challenges and successes these professionals have encountered but also about the unique perspectives and resilience they bring to their work.

Our chapter also emphasizes the importance of understanding diverse backgrounds in healthcare and encourages all students to consider how social, cultural, and historical factors shape patient care and community health outcomes. Through our events and workshops, we aim to cultivate cultural competence and empathy in healthcare, recognizing the vital role these skills play in effectively serving a diverse population.

NAAMA NextGen at UCR is more than just a student organization; it is a network of future healthcare leaders committed to excellence, inclusivity, and the advancement of health and well-being for all communities. By bringing together students and seasoned healthcare professionals in a supportive environment, we are laying the groundwork for a new generation of healthcare providers who are prepared to meet the challenges of tomorrow with compassion, knowledge, and an unwavering commitment to making a positive impact in their communities.
summary: NAAMA NextGen at UCR empowers students from all backgrounds pursuing healthcare careers. Through mentorship, networking, and events with renowned Arab American professionals, we foster leadership, inclusivity, and a commitment to advancing health for all

name: Highlander Data Consulting
description: Highlander Data Consulting provides pro-bono, data-driven strategy consulting across diverse industries.
