Our team leverages advanced data analytics and insights to help businesses optimize operations, refine strategies, and enhance decision-making processes. Equipped with strong analytical skills, our consultants are committed to elevating each aspect of our clients' business by translating complex data into actionable strategies suited for today’s data-driven landscape.
summary: Highlander Data Consulting offers pro-bono, data-driven strategy consulting that empowers businesses across industries to optimize operations and refine strategies through actionable, analytics-based insights tailored for today’s data-centric world.

name: Mycology and Uncommon Botanicals
description: This organization is focused on educating about fungi and unique pants with hands-on and research-based learning. The club is meant to change perceptions about fungi and to allow exploration of fungi and plants that are rare or unknown. Another part of the club will be to understand the medicinal properties of fungi or plants.
summary:
The club is about exploring the aspects and characteristics of fungi (mushroom) and rare or unique plants.
As an extension, we will be discussing the multidisciplinary applications of fungi and uncommon plants.

name: Business Analytics Society
description: We aim to build a community of students interested in the field of business analytics. By providing members with opportunities to develop their skills, they will gain practical experience, and enhance their understanding of data-driven decision-making through hands-on projects, workshops, and networking events.
summary: To be a leading student organization that bridges the gap between academia and industry, inspiring future business analysts to excel in a data-driven world.

name: Operation Smile at UCR
description:
Operation Smile's student programs at universities aim to empower young people to actively engage in the organization's mission of bringing free, high-quality surgical care to children with cleft lip and cleft palate worldwide. These programs encourage students to raise awareness about the challenges faced by children born with facial deformities, as well as the impact of life-changing surgeries. Through fundraising events, students directly contribute to funding surgeries and related medical services, helping to bridge financial and geographical gaps in access to care. Additionally, by supporting local medical training and infrastructure, students contribute to Operation Smile's goal of creating sustainable health solutions in communities around the world.

Beyond fundraising and awareness, Operation Smile’s university programs foster a deep sense of social responsibility and global citizenship among students. Participants gain valuable experience in organizing events, working in teams, and connecting with a network of like-minded individuals passionate about humanitarian work. The programs often offer volunteer opportunities, where students can assist with medical missions or community outreach. These hands-on experiences provide students with a firsthand look at healthcare challenges in low-resource settings, building empathy and understanding while nurturing a new generation of advocates committed to making a difference in global health.
summary: Volunteering and leadership in Operation Smile provides students with a firsthand look at healthcare challenges in low-resource settings, building empathy and understanding while nurturing a new generation of advocates.

name: Students for Exploration and Development of Space
description: SEDS (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space) is an international student organization dedicated to advancing space exploration, science, and technology. It aims to empower students with hands-on experience, educational resources, and professional networking opportunities in the space industry. SEDS fosters a collaborative environment where students can lead projects, attend conferences, and connect with industry experts, promoting innovation and a deeper understanding of space-related fields. Through workshops, events, and advocacy, SEDS inspires future leaders in space exploration and development.
summary: SEDS (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space) is an international student organization dedicated to advancing space exploration, science, and technology.

name: Product Management Club
description: The Product Management Consulting (PMC) Club is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering students with hands-on experience in product management and technology entrepreneurship. Our mission is to provide students with the knowledge, tools, and resources needed to bring tech startup ideas to life. Through collaboration, mentorship, and practical experience, we aim to foster a supportive environment where students can develop and test innovative ideas, ultimately equipping them with the skills to lead or participate in tech-driven projects or startups.
summary: The Product Management Club (PMC) is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering students with hands-on experience in product management and technology entrepreneurship through projects, lessons, and competitive events.

name: UCR Brain Game Center
description:
At the UCR Brain Game Center, we strive to improve your brain’s fitness. We research, test, and disseminate evidence-based, scientifically optimized brain fitness games that assist you in real life with memory, vision, hearing, learning, and more.

Located at Olmsted Hall Room 127 & 130
summary: At the UCR Brain Game Center,we strive to improve your brain’s fitness. We research, test, and disseminate evidence-based, scientifically optimized brain fitness games that assist you in real life with memory, vision, hearing, learning, and more!

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers at University of California, Riverside
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to foster an engaged community of future transportation professionals at the University of California, Riverside, a leading institution in sustainable and intelligent transportation systems (SITS). The chapter promotes a diverse array of research projects and engineering applications, leveraging the extensive resources available within the College of Engineering’s Center for Environmental Research & Technology (CE-CERT). We will also organize workshops, guest lectures, and project activities to equip members with the skills to tackle complex transportation, air quality, energy, and climate change challenges. Additionally, we will collaborate with other ITE chapters in Southern California to provide networking opportunities for transportation engineering students across campuses. Our partnerships with CE-CERT and the regional transportation agencies will enhance the understanding of advanced mobility technologies, such as connected and automated vehicles, and transportation electrification. Ultimately, our goal is to educate and inspire the next generation of transportation engineers to create sustainable solutions focused on safety, mobility, environmental resilience, accessibility, and equity.
summary: To build a community of future transportation professionals at UC Riverside, advancing sustainable solutions with CE-CERT resources. Through research, networking, and partnerships, we equip members to tackle mobility, safety, and environmental challenges

name: Muslim Tech Collaborative
summary: MTC UCR is dedicated to creating a space for Muslims in tech to come together as a professional and spiritual community, showcasing Islamic excellence by leveraging our talents and creating positive tangible impacts on the world.

name: Astronomy Union for Reading, Outreach and Research Activities
description: AURORA (Astronomy Union for Reading, Outreach, and Research Activities) aims to build a vibrant community for fostering collaboration, learning, and innovation in astronomy. Through journal clubs, workshops, outreach, and engaging discussions, we connect students and researchers passionate about the cosmos.
summary: Promote research culture through journal clubs, talks, workshops, and outreach. Foster meaningful discussions among graduate students, postdocs, and motivated undergraduates to inspire collaboration and innovation in research.

name: Code Compassion at UCR
description:
• Host interactive events and programs that foster inclusivity, providing opportunities for students, healthcare professionals/guest speakers, and community members to engage with real life patient stories and scenarios to understand their experiences on a deeper, more human level
  • Improve the quality of patient care by encouraging healthcare providers and future professionals to incorporate empathy and advocate for marginalized voices in healthcare 
  • Foster collaboration and dialogue among students, healthcare professionals, and patients to bridge gaps between medicine and advocacy.
summary: To bring awareness and increase understanding about the importance of compassion, empathy, and narrative in healthcare among the Inland Empire/Riverside community– particularly for marginalized and underserved populations

name: Sisters Affirming our Social-cultural Identities
description:
Sisters Affirming our Social-Cultural Identities (S.A.S.I.) was established as a cultural enrichment and advocacy group driven towards the advancement of Black women everywhere. S.A.S.I. is intended to center those who identify as Black women and address their unique intersectional oppressions. We are here to empower, support and advocate on behalf of Black women across the African diaspora, spiritually and emotionally, mentally and physically.

On the campus of University of California, Riverside, S.A.S.I. will serve as an organization that seeks to encourage women to work towards their own personal self-development by providing a healthy space that promotes vulnerability and growth. S.A.S.I. will use its platform to discuss, dissect issues that are relevant and directly affect women of color, black women and the black community overall. 

S.A.S.I. is an organization that is intended for the growth of women of color. We will stand in alliance and solidarity with our male counterparts. We will work to strengthen and foster a healthy relationship within our community amongst the men and women on this campus.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a safe space for black women. S.A.S.I. aspires to do this through programming, outreach, meetings, events, and any other means to ensure the protection of black women on campus.

name: Moot Court Club
description:
The Moot Court Club is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering skills in legal analysis, oral advocacy, and critical thinking through the simulation of appellate court proceedings. Our members engage in thought-provoking debates, analyze real-world legal issues, and compete in local and national Moot Court competitions.

Through workshops, mock trials, and mentoring, we create a supportive environment where students can develop essential skills for law school, careers in law, and beyond. Open to students of all backgrounds, the club aims to empower members to advocate confidently, think critically, and lead effectively.
summary: The Moot Court Club simulates appellate court advocacy, helping students develop skills in legal analysis, oral argument, and critical thinking. We foster teamwork, leadership, and confidence through workshops, mock trials, and competitions.

name: Women in STEM and the Arts
summary: WISAA aims to create a most relaxing space for individuals that identify as women/femmes who are majoring in STEM by providing an artistic outlet.

name: Alight Campus Ministry at UCR
summary: Settle in and find rest in Christ, alight in His presence with us! We host weekly bible studies and other exciting activities to help us grow together as we follow Jesus. Founded on Acts 2:38 and John 8:12.

name: Dental Global Brigades
description:
Dental Global Brigades is a student-led organization dedicated to improving oral health and providing access to dental care for underserved communities worldwide. As part of the larger Global Brigades network, we collaborate with passionate students and dental professionals to address global health disparities through sustainable, community-driven initiatives.

Our mission is to empower communities to achieve long-term dental health by providing education, preventative care, and treatment during service trips. At UC Riverside, we aim to foster a community of students committed to making a positive impact through outreach, advocacy, and hands-on volunteer opportunities.

By joining Dental Global Brigades, members will gain invaluable experiences in global health, cultural exchange, and teamwork, all while contributing to a meaningful cause. Together, we can create healthier smiles and brighter futures for communities in need.
summary: Dental Global Brigades at UCR is a student-led organization focused on improving oral health in underserved communities. Through education, preventative care, and service trips, we empower communities while offering members hands-on experience.

name: Theft Protection and Locksport Club at UCR
description:
The Theft Prevention and Locksport Club at UCR (TP&LC@UCR) is dedicated to educating students about personal property security and promoting awareness of theft prevention strategies on campus. In response to rising theft rates, the club aims to empower members, particularly first-year students, with the knowledge and tools to safeguard their belongings, including bikes and scooters often left unattended due to building restrictions. Through hands-on engagement with locksport, members gain insights into how security systems work and their vulnerabilities, fostering an ethical understanding of security practices. The club also supports professional development in security fields, collaborating with campus authorities like UCPD to create a safer university environment. 
Discord
summary: The Theft Prevention and Locksport Club at UCR educates students on securing belongings, promotes theft prevention, and offers hands-on locksport training to understand systems. The club fosters safety, awareness, and growth in ethical practices.

name: Black Graduate Student Association at UCR
description: The mission of the Black GSA is to foster a vibrant and inclusive space that promotes the academic, professional, social, and cultural well-being of Black graduate students at UCR. We are committed to honoring the intersectional identities within our community, addressing systemic inequities, and empowering members to lead and advocate for positive change. Additionally, the Black GSA strives to mentor and inspire future generations of Black students, ensuring a legacy of excellence and representation in graduate education and beyond.
summary: The mission of the BLack GSA is to foster a vibrant and inclusive space that promotes the academic, professional, social, and cultural well-being of Black graduate students at UCR.

name: Club Quetzal
description: This organization shall be to strive in providing representation and teaching others about Guatemalan culture. We want to foster a sense of family here at UCR to cultivate a strong community where it is plain to see members can depend on one another. This club is open to everyone as we want to build a rich and diverse community within the club. Club Quetzal aims to teach others about diversity and show that, through engaging in activities focused around acceptance and tolerance, we will get a better understanding of others while learning about ourselves. Club Quetzal will give students an open floor to speak freely in a respectful and positive manner. It has been established to give Guatemalan students at UCR  the opportunity to feel a sense of inclusion and solve the lack of representation for Guatemala. Along with an opportunity for those tied to their culture to share cultural aspects and teachings to those of descent who may not be so aware. Whilst simultaneously also contributing to multiculturalism and teaching non Guatemalan students about the significance of the culture as well.
summary: Club Quetzal is a Guatemalan heritage club open to all Guatemalans and students interested in the culture. We strive to create a supportive community and family here at UCR whilst also providing representation and showing others our culture.

name: Cheese Club
description: The Cheese Club at UCR invites cheese lovers to come together and discuss the variety of cheeses along with their histories. Our goal is to create a community where those who are connoisseurs of cheese and those who just enjoy cheese can discuss their opinions on cheese and cheese products in a respectful and fun learning environment!
summary: A friendly environment where people can socialize and eat cheese. New cheese is brought every meeting. We take requests!

name: Elite Healthcare Hospice
description: The Elite Healthcare Hospice at UC Riverside is dedicated to providing compassionate support to individuals and families facing end-of-life challenges. Through volunteer service, advocacy, and education, we aim to foster a community of care, understanding, and respect. Our mission is to raise awareness about the importance of hospice care while equipping members with the skills to make a meaningful impact in the lives of those in need.
summary: Elite Healthcare Hospice at UC Riverside fosters compassion and support for individuals and families facing end-of-life challenges through service, advocacy, and education.

name: Medics in Armenia at UC Riverside
summary: Medics in Armenia is an organization dedicated to educating and providing valuable experiences for incoming medical professionals. Students participate in shadowing opportunities, volunteer work, and local services applicable to the medical field.

name: Joshua Medical Group Food Recovery Program
description: In alignment with our core values, we are committed to addressing food insecurity within our community. Through our food donation initiatives, we strive to ensure that everyone, regardless of circumstance, has access to nutritious meals.
summary: We are dedicated to combating food insecurity by providing nutritious meals to everyone in need through our food donation initiatives.

name: Smile Society
description: Smile Society is a student-led organization that brings together individuals passionate about health equity, with a specific focus on oral health. Our activities are centered around education, advocacy, and fundraising. We educate members and the broader community about the significance of oral health, advocate for increased awareness and better hygiene practices, and organize fundraising events to support non-profits and NGOs that provide dental supplies and educational programs to underprivileged communities.
summary: Smile Society promotes oral health through education, advocacy, and fundraising, supporting underprivileged communities with dental supplies and hygiene awareness.

name: MAES Student Chapter at UC Riverside
description:
This organization intends to cultivate and advance representation of Latinos in STEM. By hosting workshops, events, and meetings we will build a community that encourages and aids Latino leadership in STEM. We aim to help guide you in your academic and professional STEM careers by offering scholarships and internships through mymaes.org. We also plan on building a fun and welcoming social experience within stem majors! We will also serve as an outreach program to local public schools for grades K-12 by exposing students in local public schools to science, STEM, and career fairs.

Spring quarter meetings: 

Thursday's at the Bears Den

Doors open at 6:30pm 

Meeting starts at 7:00pm
summary: The purpose of this social and academic organization shall be to promote, cultivate, and advance representation of Latino leadership and education in STEM. Our club will serve as a resource for outreach programs toward K-12.

name: Quranic Studies UCR
description: The Quran Club is a learning group focused on fostering a deeper connection with the Quran. It offers participants the opportunity to learn proper Quranic recitation (Tajweed), understand the Tafsir (interpretation) of verses, explore translation for better comprehension, and work on memorization of Quranic passages. This supportive environment is designed to empower women in their spiritual growth and enhance their knowledge of Quranic teachings
summary: A Quranic Studies Club focused on teaching Quranic recitation (Tajweed), Tafsir (interpretation), translation, and memorization in a supportive environment, fostering spiritual growth and deepening understanding of Quranic teachings.

name: Geography Club
description: The Geography Club will host weekly discussions and activities on a wide range of topics in the field of geography. This includes discussions about currents events, history, sociology, and more. All students of any major are welcome!
summary: The Geography Club is established to engage all students at the University of California at Riverside in the  study of social issues, political and human geography, and geopolitics through discussion and map creation activities.

name: Hylander Billiards Association
description: Hylander Billiards is a place to have fun and learn about pool in general. Please do not be intimidated that this is a pool club, all people are welcome and we can teach you all how to play.
summary: Hylander Billiards is a place to have fun and learn about pool in general. Please do not be intimidated that this is a pool club, all people are welcome and we can teach you all how to play.

name: Lacrosse Club at UCR
summary: We provide students at the University of California Riverside the opportunity to play lacrosse at the collegiate level and cultivate the sport of lacrosse within our community by providing a safe, inclusive, and competitive environment for players.

name: The Garden Project at UCR
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to work with local schools to build student-run gardens, provide nutritious fresh food, and educate youth about nutrition.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to work with local schools to build student-run gardens, provide nutritious fresh food, and educate youth about nutrition.

name: Highlander Cloud Computing
summary: The goal of Highlander Cloud Computing is to host workshops based on cloud computing as well as host resources for cloud computing on campus.

name: R'LING
description: R'LING is a student led linguistics-focused organization that strives to offer and create meaningful opportunities for students interested in linguistics or its related fields. Through various panels, guest speakers and workshops, the organization provides academic guidance and community structure to foster students' interests in the linguistics field. We hope to inform and educate students on the diversity within the study of linguistics and the various career opportunities it offers.
summary: R'LING is a linguistics-focused club organization that offers various opportunities to network and collaborate with related departments, as well as formally discuss the studies of language and other related fields.

name: Alzheimers Foundation of America at UCR
description: To raise awareness, provide education, and foster a supportive community for those affected by Alzheimer's disease as well as inspire individuals to learn about Alzheimer’s disease. Through advocacy, volunteer efforts, and scientific discussions, we aim to promote understanding, contribute to research efforts, and support individuals and families impacted by neurodegenerative conditions
summary: To raise awareness, provide education, and foster a supportive community for those affected by Alzheimer's disease as well as inspire individuals to learn about Alzheimer’s disease through advocacy, volunteer efforts, and scientific discussions.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description: Students will have a safe place where they feel comfortable to support Israel and its right to exist. Along with this there will be many events held to teach about the life and culture of Israel and its people. Another main part of our organization will be to be a strong
summary: Supporting Israel in a safe area as well as learning about its culture and daily life

name: French and Friends: A Francophone Club
summary: We promote French language and Francophone culture. We're inclusive of the global French diaspora and seek to spread understanding of the complexities of the Francophone world. Join us for French themed games, activities, entertainment, food and more!

name: The Math Mission
description: The Math Mission is a student-led organization at UCR dedicated to improving math education for elementary school students. Our mission is to provide free, high-quality tutoring and mentorship to young learners, helping them build confidence and foundational skills in mathematics. Through structured tutoring sessions, engaging activities, and community outreach, we aim to make math more accessible, enjoyable, and impactful. By connecting college students with local schools, we foster leadership, service, and a commitment to educational equity. Our club is open to all students passionate about teaching, mentoring, and making a difference in the community.
summary: To provide mentorship and tutoring that strengthens K-12 students' math skills, builds confidence, and fosters a love for learning with schools in the Inland Empire.

name: The Joker's Lounge
description: The club is all about creating an environment where card lovers can hang out and socialize through card games. Events will be in the form of social events, tournaments, etc. Card games include anything such as Uno.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to bond with people over card games. Our mission is to bring people together of all skill levels to enjoy a social environment through card games.

name: Kindred Volunteer Collective
description: Join us in building a more inclusive and equitable future! Our organization is dedicated to bringing more attention to various causes and uplifting student groups through tangible acts of philanthropy and service. We believe in the power of collective action to create real change. We're driven by our core values of inclusion, integrity, innovation, and passion, and we invite you to be a part of our mission to foster a supportive community where everyone thrives. Become a member and help us make a lasting impact! Please follow our IG for more info!
summary: Make an impact! We raise awareness among various causes & uplift student groups through philanthropy & service. Driven by inclusion, integrity, innovation, & passion, we foster a supportive community for positive change.

name: Virtual Reality X
description:
The VRx at UCR Club, initiated by undergraduate students at our university, seeks to utilize virtual reality technology to enhance the lives of seniors suffering from Alzheimer's Disease in Riverside County. Our mission is to provide innovative VR-based dementia screening, educate the community about Alzheimer's, and offer immersive VR experiences that benefit the cognitive and emotional well-being of the elderly. This club represents a unique intersection of technology, healthcare, and volunteerism, aiming to bridge the gap between younger and older generations through meaningful interactions and support.

Our vision is to establish a compassionate and innovative community where the challenges of Alzheimer's Disease are met with cutting-edge technology and empathetic care. We envision a future where virtual reality is a key component in the management and care of Alzheimer's, offering new avenues for early detection, therapeutic intervention, and quality of life improvements. By empowering students and engaging seniors, the VRx at UCR Club aspires to be a leader in promoting mental health and social connection, ultimately transforming the way our society supports its aging population.
summary: We are a service-oriented organization that uses cutting-edge VR technology to improve the quality of life of seniors in our community and provide invaluable VR resources to at-risk populations through educational content and early dementia screening.

name: Let's Be The Change
description: To Promote Health Equity And Access To Public Health Resources For People In The Inland Empire And Globally. Through Strategic Partnerships, We Strive To Close The Gap In Health Inequity And Provide Resources That Support The Development And Expansion Of Medical Infrastructure And Public Health Initiatives. Through Calculated Allocation, And Putting The Interest Of The Individuals In Need Above Ours, Health Equity Could Be Achieved. To Achieve Our Mission, We Will Work Closely With Local Organizations And Community Leaders To Identify The Most Pressing Needs And To Develop Strategies For Addressing Them. We Will Also Be Committed To Transparency And Accountability In All Of Our Activities, Ensuring That Our Partners Can Trust That Their Contributions Are Being Used Effectively And Efficiently. Together, We Can Make A Meaningful Difference In The Lives Of Those In Need, And Ultimately Create Change In The World.
summary: Our mission is to promote health equity in the Inland Empire and globally by expanding access to public health resources. We work with a larger organization that has paper trails to prove direct contributions internationally and in the community.

name: Highlander Health Informatics
description: We at Highlander Health Informatics are dedicated to providing students with the knowledge and opportunities to explore the intersection of healthcare and technology. Through events, discussions, and hands-on experiences, we aim to equip members with the skills and insights necessary for success in the dynamic field of health informatics.
summary: The Highlander Health Informatics Club introduces and connects students to the ever-growing field of health technology through various events such as workshops, networking events, presentations, and hands on experience.

name: Neurodivergent Collective
description: Welcome to the Neurodivergent Collective! If you are a neurodivergent student, this may be the club for you! Each week we will host a fun activity or workshop aimed at building community, finding commonalities and making peer connections. Though we are not a therapeutic organization, we do hope to provide social, emotional and academic support, and, above all, to combat the isolation many of us feel navigating college life. Weekly meeting time/place TBD.
summary: The Neurodivergent Collective aims to create a community space at UCR where neurodivergent students (autistic students, students with ADHD, etc.) can coexist as our most authentic selves. Come for the fun activities, stay for the warm support.

name: Sparklepop Nail Club
description: Sparklepop Nail club will provide members with nail stands, nail plates, nail polishes, and everything that is needed to make the nail design. The nails created are strictly press-on nails, meaning there will be no skin and nail contact, eliminating the risk of infections.
summary: Sparklepop’s mission is to create a DIY nail art community for the students of UC Riverside, sharing bold and unique designs together! Imagining glittery intricate designs onto press-on nails, just like an artist painting a canvas.

name: Belong
description: We acknowledge that Gay, Trans and Queer people have been systematically marginalized from the Christian community and so together we dreamed up Belong as an answer to our community's isolation. This organization exists to show students on campus that the love of Jesus is for everyone. We joyously celebrate the fullness of everyone's complexity, nuances, and layered individuality. We believe that you don't have to change a thing about yourself to be loved by God. This is a queer centered space that practices and teaches Queer affirming theology. Allies and non-spiritual/religious people are warmly welcomed and encouraged to join if they are reflecting on these issues, recognizing that this is a queer centered space. If a student has ever felt left out, excluded or isolated from their faith for whatever reason we hope and earnestly work together to create a space of belonging, friendship, and community here at Belong.
summary: Belong is a Christian club that is a welcoming, safe, Queer affirming and intersectional community for students at UCR. We exist to support students on campus who are interested in exploring the life of Jesus from a Queer and love-centered point of view.

name: Brothers Leading African Americans through Consciousness and Knowledge
description:
the members of Brothers Leading African Americans
through Consciousness and Knowledge at UCR, established to provide develop leaders, promote
brotherhood and unity among UCR students, and support the uplift and development of UCR, Riverside
and surrounding communities
summary: the members of Brothers Leading African Americans through Consciousness and Knowledge at UCR, established to provide develop leaders, brotherhood and unity.

name: The 3E Study
description: The 3E Club seeks to generate research to improve health and wellbeing for marginalized emerging adults. We aim to spread awareness about cardiometabolic and mental health, mentoring in college, research opportunities, and social determinants of health. We hope to create a community for all students to feel supported and have a space safe on campus.
summary: The 3E Study seeks to generate research to improve health and wellbeing for marginalized emerging adults. We aim to spread awareness about cardiometabolic and mental health, mentoring in college, research opportunities, and social determinants of health.

name: Filipino American Medical Association
description: Through our passion for personal and professional growth, FAMA is dedicated to connect and empower our members by fostering a supportive community that values culture, career advancement, and community engagement.
summary: Through our passion for personal and professional growth, FAMA is dedicated to connect and empower our members by fostering a supportive community that values culture, career advancement, and community engagement.

name: PROsthetics Club
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to promote student exposure to the varying applications of prosthetics, engaging students from all engineering and CNAS backgrounds with an emphasis on medicinal applications I.E. creating mock prosthetics.

name: Cru - Real Life
description:
For Fall 2025

We traditionally meet every Thursday @ 8pm 

Check us our on Instagram! @cru.ucr for updates and events AND/OR contact jgonz706@ucr.edu for more information!

Our Mission
To journey with students and faculty to follow Jesus and fulfill their God-given destiny.

Our Vision
We’re a Christ-centered, caring community passionate about seeing students, faculty, and communities transformed by the gospel. We long for a world where everyone knows someone who truly follows Jesus.

Our Values

We value faith, growth and fruitfulness in a compassionate community committed to being

-Culturally connected.
-Academically achieving.
-Financially responsible.
-Empowered spiritually.

Our Roots 

Cru - Real Life is a ministry of Cru, and so our roots stretch back to the founding of Campus Crusade for Christ in 1951.
summary: Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ. With Cru, you'll have local and global opportunities to learn, connect and go.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description:
Our Mission :

To create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to others for the betterment of our communities.

Our Vision: 

A future where every Pi Kappa Phi embraces his role as a leader, puts service before self and improves the world around him. 

Our Public Values: 

Common Loyalty, Personal Responsibility, Achievement, Accountability, Campus Involvement, Responsible Citizenship, Lifelong Commitment.
summary: Pi Kappa Phi was founded by exceptional men who chose to lead. For more than a century, we have stayed true to our roots, developing the next generation of servant leaders who put service before self and work to improve the world around them.

name: Sidequests
description:
Our Mission - Redefine college experience.

Our Culture - Work hard, play hard. Everybody is a co-creator of the experience “Sidequests”. We believe the solution to overcome any problem is simple. Treat people equally, let the best idea win. Remain curious.
summary: Leave the city, go on that trip. The night is ours. Experience a sidequest.

name: Oaxaqueños de UCR
description: Our mission is to provide representation of the Oaxacan community at UC Riverside.
summary: Our mission is to provide representation of the Oaxacan community at UC Riverside.

name: Healthy Heart Nation UCR
description: Healthy Heart Nation UCR is a student chapter of Healthy Heart Nation, a national nonprofit dedicated to improving cardiovascular health in underserved populations. Through this partnership, we participate in and host events organized by Healthy Heart Nation, including clinical outreach, health screenings, and educational workshops. These events are overseen by licensed healthcare professionals to ensure safe and effective delivery of care, while also providing UCR students with meaningful volunteer opportunities and professional development. These efforts allow us to bridge gaps in care and strengthen connections between the community and future healthcare professionals.
summary: Our mission is to advance cardiovascular health awareness and promote health equity on campus and in the local community by uniting students from diverse fields to engage in clinical outreach, education, and service with underserved populations.

name: Exotic Animal Care Club
description: The Exotic Animal Care Club aims to provide educational opportunities focused on the health, behavior, and care of exotic animals. By creating a supportive learning environment, members will gain insight into species-specific needs and common health risks. The club will also maintain a weekly blog spotlighting unique exotic animals to deepen public awareness and spark curiosity. This could be a beneficial experience for those going into careers as zookeepers, wildlife researchers, activists, and veterinarians planning on specializing in exotic animals or wildlife.
summary: The Exotic Animal Care Club aims to spread awareness on issues concerning exotic animals and their care, diseases affecting them and their treatments. We aim to support future zoo keepers, veterinarians specializing in exotics or wildlife, and more!

name: Our Protectors of Animal Wellbeing and Safety
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide hands-on animal volunteer experience, spread animal care awareness, create a space for animal lovers to gather, and overall contribute to the well-being of animals.

name: Med X Clinical Research Initiative at UCR
description: Students in UCR Med X will collaborate in research teams to explore clinically relevant topics of their choice. The organization provides guidance, instruction, and resources to support every step of their research journey, from idea to execution.
summary: UCR Med X empowers undergraduates to bring their clinical research ideas to life by providing resources, mentorship, and hands-on instruction in research design, ethics, and implementation.

name: Project Development Club
description: The Project Development Club's mission is to help students learn proper techniques for creating research projects and analyzing data. We allow our members to form their own student-led projects, which will sharpen their analytical and professional leadership skills while exploring topics they are passionate about. Throughout the year, we will host social events and workshops to encourage collaboration and foster career readiness.
summary: Our organization is dedicated to helping students develop and strengthen their analytical and professional leadership skills through student-led projects, R coding workshops, and social events that foster networking and career readiness.

name: Creatives In Business
description:
Creatives in Business is a graduate student organization that empowers students interested in blending creativity with business. Whether your passions lie in marketing, branding, fashion, entertainment, media, or design, creatives is a space for innovative thinkers who want to explore how creativity drives industry success.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between creative expression and strategic business practices. We offer networking opportunities, guest speaker panels, workshops, and hands-on projects that prepare students for careers in creative industries.

Whether you’re looking to break into creative marketing, start your own brand, or simply find your people, creatives is here to support you.
summary: A graduate student organization that brings together students interested in the creative side of business whether it’s marketing, branding, media, fashion, or entertainment. We create space for collaboration, industry exploration and skill-building.

name: Live Action at UCR
description: The purpose of this organization is to connect, educate, and empower students to stand together in defense of human life. As a unified body, we seek to raise awareness about the injustice of abortion and respond through informed dialogue, compassionate outreach, and active engagement. Our members are equipped to engage respectfully with those of differing views and to support local families in need—offering tangible help and hope in order to save lives.
summary: Our organization exists to unite and equip students to defend the dignity of every human life. Through education, respectful dialogue, and community outreach, we work to confront the reality of abortion, and support local families in need.

name: Formula One Club
description: A community of students who are interested or love Formula One and want a space to share or learn more about the sport, its drivers, and history. We hold meetings either before or after race weekends, and discuss other items like driver drama or tech talks. We are dedicated to making a fun and positive community for F1 fans on campus and getting others into the sport!
summary: A community of students who love Formula One and want a space to meet others who love the sport or even those who want to learn more about it.

name: Beta Sigma
summary: Dedicated to connecting UCR's mobile gamers. We foster an inclusive environment of sportsmanship and community through events, with an emphasis on Supercell titles. Beta Sigma stands for Brawl Stars, and no one does it like Beta Sigma.

name: Punjabi's at UCR
description:
I want to establish a vibrant and inclusive space at UCR where Punjabi culture is not only celebrated but uplifted through meaningful events, cultural education, and student support. Punjabi students make up a growing part of the UCR community, and it’s important that our heritage, language, and experiences are recognized and shared with the wider campus.

My goal is to create a club that brings students together through a variety of events—such as cultural nights, dance performances, holiday celebrations like Vaisakhi, language workshops, guest speaker panels, and social mixers. These events will offer both Punjabi and non-Punjabi students a chance to engage with and learn about Punjabi traditions in a fun, respectful, and educational way.

Beyond hosting events, this club will also serve as a platform for Punjabi-identifying students to connect, find community, and express themselves freely. Whether someone is deeply rooted in Punjabi culture or just beginning to explore their identity, I want every member to feel seen, supported, and welcomed.
summary: Punjabi and Sikh students at UCR are a campus sub-community that blends social, cultural, and academic influence. From organizing cultural shows and bolstering South Asian representation to advancing university-level scholarship on Punjabi identity, they

name: Careers in Scrubs
description:
Careers in Scrubs is a pre-health organization at UC Riverside dedicated to helping students prepare for and explore careers in medicine, dentistry, nursing, pharmacy, public health, and other allied health fields. We provide opportunities for professional development, career exploration, and building a strong pre-health network.

Our events include skill-building workshops, panels with healthcare professionals and graduate students, and networking sessions to help members learn about different career paths and what it takes to succeed in them. We also host mentorship programs that connect members with peers and professionals who can offer academic guidance, application tips, and career advice.

Beyond career preparation, we engage in community service projects that give members a chance to contribute to local health initiatives and develop leadership skills. Active members can also run for officer positions, plan events, and take on meaningful roles in shaping the organization’s direction.

Open to all UCR students, Careers in Scrubs is the place to gain knowledge, build connections, and strengthen your professional skills while being part of a supportive pre-health community.
summary: Careers in Scrubs provides pre-health students with hands-on experience, mentorship, and networking to explore careers in medicine, dentistry, nursing, pharmacy, public health, and allied health through workshops, events, and community engagement.

name: Aspire, Xplore, Ignite, and Succeed.
description: AXIS (Empowering Students to Aspire, Xplore, Ignite, and Succeed) is a multidisciplinary student organization at UC Riverside committed to helping students unlock their full potential. The club provides a supportive community where members connect with peers of similar interests, gain mentorship, and access professional development opportunities across fields such as medicine, law, and computer science. Through workshops, networking events, speaker panels, and hands-on initiatives, AXIS equips students with the skills and connections to excel academically and professionally. With a strong focus on inclusivity, leadership development, and collaboration, AXIS empowers its members to turn ambition into achievement and build a lasting impact within the UCR community and beyond
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to empower students from every background and culture in UC Riverside to help students achieve success by aligning with like-minded individuals, resources, and opportunities.

name: Association of Environmental Professionals at UCR
description: The Association of Environmental Professionals (AEP) Student Chapter at UCR is dedicated to building strong connections between students and environmental professionals, fostering mutual growth and collaboration. Our mission is to support students preparing for careers in the environmental field by providing opportunities to learn from experts, explore diverse environmental professions, and develop valuable professional networks. Through events, mentorship, and community engagement, AEP empowers students to expand their knowledge, gain real-world insights, and take meaningful steps toward impactful environmental careers.
summary: The AEP Student Chapter at UCR connects students with environmental professionals to foster mutual growth. We support future environmental leaders through networking, mentorship, and events that build knowledge, skills, and career readiness.

name: Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
description:
The Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association (MSME GSA) is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering a strong academic, professional, and social community among graduate students within the fields of Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering. Our mission is to support the personal and professional development of our members through a variety of initiatives, including research symposia, career development workshops, networking events, and social gatherings.

MSME GSA serves as a platform for graduate students to connect with peers, faculty, alumni, and industry professionals, while also acting as a collective voice to advocate for student interests within the department and the broader university community. By promoting interdisciplinary collaboration, leadership opportunities, and community engagement, MSME GSA strives to enrich the graduate experience and empower students to succeed in their academic and professional pursuits.
summary: The MSMEGSA supports graduate students in Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering through professional development, networking, and social events, fostering a strong, collaborative community and advocating for student interests within the department

name: Highlander Mixed Martial Arts Club
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to promote a healthy environment for people to learn, share knowledge, and grow as martial artists, regardless of background.

name: Pokemon Club
description:
We aim to create a space where fans and newcomers alike can enjoy Pokémon together. Whether a longtime fan or someone who is just getting to know Pokémon we welcome all to join. We hope to see you there! Feel free to join the discord for updates and to talk with fellow club members!

Discord: discord.gg/yW7ChCedES
Instagram: instagram.com/pokemonatucr/
summary: A club for people who cherish and want to learn more about Pokémon. We cover everything from the trading card game, to the manga, to the video games.

name: Stratos Associates
description: Stratos Associates is a student-run business organization that empowers students to explore business strategy, marketing, and innovation. We provide professional development through workshops, speaker events, networking, and real-world projects with small businesses and nonprofits to help students gain experience that turns into tangible job skills and prepares them for success.
summary: Stratos Associates is a student business club focused on leadership, entrepreneurship, and career growth through events and real-world projects.

name: South Asian InterVarsity
description: SAIV is about building a joyful and authentic community for students of South Asian background to encounter Jesus, and empowering them to boldly take risks for the Gospel on every corner of campus and beyond.
summary: SAIV is about building a joyful and authentic community for students of South Asian background to encounter Jesus, and empowering them to boldly take risks for the Gospel on every corner of campus and beyond.

name: CampusCycle at UCR
description: CampusCycle aims to reduce waste on campus by promoting the reuse and redistribution of non-perishable, pre-packaged food, dorm supplies, and additional everyday items. Our mission is to build a more sustainable, mindful community by giving unwanted goods a second life and connecting students and community members with the resources they need. Through awareness events, educational meetings, and collaboration with other organizations, CampusCycle shall reduce waste while fostering community cooperation.
summary: CampusCycle reduces campus waste by promoting the reuse of non-perishable food, dorm supplies, and everyday items. We build a mindful, sustainable community through education, events, and partnerships that give unwanted goods a second life.

name: Council for Global Health Scholars Chapter @ UC Riverside
description:
The mission of this Chapter is to provide students with leadership development, hands-on healthcare project opportunities, and access to a global professional network through CGHS programs such as the Honors Member Program and Global Impact Fellowship. This Chapter is part of the Council for Global Health Scholars, a cooperative initiative that has spent the past twenty years helping top students navigate and succeed in demanding medical professions while contributing to healthcare infrastructure and volunteer networks worldwide, impacting over 1,000,000 patients.

As a distinguished Chapter, our focus is to bring together like-minded scholars to develop healthcare innovations that support underserved communities globally, in partnership with Global Medical Brigades®. Through these efforts, students will gain real-world experience in implementing solutions that address healthcare challenges in various communities.

Additionally, members will benefit from leadership training and project management development, along with immediate access to exclusive resources such as certification programs, free graduate school preparation materials, scholarship opportunities, and global networking connections that will help advance their careers in healthcare leadership.
summary: Our Chapter empowers students through leadership training, global healthcare projects, and networking, partnering with Global Medical Brigades to innovate for underserved communities while offering resources, certifications, and career support.

name: Quant Club at UCR
summary: Our mission is to equip UCR students with the knowledge, skills, and network needed to succeed in competitive careers at top firms like SIG, 2Sigma, and Jane Street. We bridge the gap between academic theory and real-world application.

name: Women's Business Association
summary: A supportive and empowering space for college students, particularly women and students who identify with femininity, to explore personal growth, professional development, and creative expression within the field of Business.

name: Mobile Gaming Academy
description:
This org is a space for students who share a passion for mobile games of all kinds. We provide a structured environment where members can engage in casual and competitive play. Members can take part in specific game divisions, join community events, and contribute ideas to keep this club growing. In leadership, we are supported by a long term growth for years to come.

Join our Discord!:

 https://discord.gg/epU2yAY5RV
summary: Our club is dedicated to playing any gams on the phone and/or on a tablet. We cater to beginners, casual, and pros.

name: Barbecue Club
summary: Our goal is to create a community where students can learn the art and science of barbecue, from smoking meats to mastering techniques.

name: Canna-Business Club
description: The purpose of the Canna-Business Club is to cultivate a well-informed community by promoting awareness and education about cannabinoid meta-studies, related research, and market data through safety and responsible decision-making. Our focus is on providing accurate information on the medicinal properties, potential risks, Market trends, Labor practices and legal considerations associated with cannabis use through a communalist lens [Communalist: Meaning communally mindful engagement] for proper ethics engagement (used collectively to filter economic lens).  The name “Canna-Business Club” and our pillars “ Safety, education and, awareness” reflects our commitment to proper academic rigor to discuss trends, projections, policy ordinance and research discussions while unraveling the stigmas around cannabis through education, cultural awareness, and responsible dialogue, explicitly not promoting its consumption while reclaiming the narrative from bad actors.
summary: a communally cultural, and analytically academic organization dedicated to fostering stigma-free discussions through evidence-based education about cannabis research, analysis, safety, history, trends, projections and cultural significance

name: redefineD at UCR
description: The purpose(s) of this organization is to educate students about street medicine and the needs of underserved communities. By working closely with our parent organization, we provide members not only with knowledge but also with the opportunity to participate in hands-on fieldwork opportunities. Through service, learning, and real-world experience, we aim to help marginalized populations redefine their lives while inspiring students.
summary: Through service, learning, and real-world experience, we aim to help marginalized populations redefine their lives while inspiring students.

name: Undergraduate Research Club
description:
The Undergraduate Research Club (URC) at the University of California, Riverside is dedicated to fostering a culture of inquiry, discovery, and innovation among undergraduate students across all academic disciplines. URC provides a platform for students to explore research opportunities, develop professional skills, and engage with faculty mentors through workshops, collaborative projects, and educational events.

Our mission is to make research more accessible by connecting students to the tools, resources, and networks they need to succeed whether they are new to research or already involved in a lab or project. Through partnerships with departments, student organizations, and professional institutions, URC promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and helps students share their findings through presentations, publications, and conferences.

URC strives to uphold integrity, inclusivity, and ethical conduct in all scholarly activities while inspiring a new generation of researchers to apply their knowledge for the betterment of the academic and local community.
summary: The Undergraduate Research Club (URC) at UCR promotes student research, collaboration, and innovation across all disciplines through mentorship, workshops, and hands-on projects that connect students with faculty and professional opportunities.

name: Women's Club Basketball at UCR
summary: Women's club basketball team at UCR. Full roster, away and home games, official jerseys.

name: Anime Diva Club
summary: This club centers women and femme-presenting anime fans to connect, create, and celebrate our love for anime without judgment. Most anime spaces feel male-dominated, so we figured, why not make our own space where we can just nerd out, hype each other up

name: Track Club at UCR
description: Track Club at UCR is an organization of dedicated runners united by a passion for training, improving, and competing together at a competitive level. Our mission is to provide a structured, supportive, and organized environment for athletes aspiring to continue their cross country and track careers competitively.
summary: Track Club at UCR is a community of runners who share a passion for training, improving, and competition together at a competitive level. Our mission is to provide a supportive environment for those who wish to pursue cross country and track.

name: Business Entertainment Association
description: The Business Entertainment Association connects students with opportunities in Business Entertainment, fostering meaningful relationships and networks with industry professionals and students alike. Through workshops, visits, guest speakers, and networking events, students in the Business Entertainment Association will be able to immerse themselves in the field and take a next step in their careers.
summary: Connecting students with opportunities in Business Entertainment and sharing insights into how things operate behind companies focused on Entertainment.

name: Faces of Afro-Muslims
description: FAM (Faces of Afro-Muslims) is a community dedicated to celebrating, uplifting, and amplifying the voices of Afro-Muslim students. We exist to create a safe space where culture and faith intersect — where we can embrace our roots, express our identities freely, and connect through shared experiences.
summary: FAM is a community dedicated to celebrating, uplifting, and amplifying the voices of Afro-Muslim students. We exist to create a safe space where culture and faith intersect — where we can embrace our roots an express our identities.

name: Carribbean Student Union
description: All Students are welcome, you do not have to be of Caribbean descent to learn and take part in Caribbean Culture! Our instagram is @ucrcsu for current updates!
summary: The Caribbean Student Union (CSU) aims to celebrate and share Caribbean culture by fostering unity, inclusion, and education within the UCR community. The organization supports students of Caribbean descent and allies and promotes cultural awareness.

name: BlaqOUT
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a space specifically tailored for Black Queer scholars at UC Riverside (UCR) to connect students with similar backgrounds, enhance retention, and foster connections within the greater community. BlaqOUT will do so by creating equitable opportunities for students to engage their community and advance their careers through mentorship, networking, and other initiatives. BlaqOUT will strive to unite and welcome Black Queer scholars to the greater queer community.
summary: blaqOUT (UCR) is a social club designed to provide community, opportunities, and foster retention and involvement for Black Queer (LGBTQIA+) students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: Athletes In Action
summary: Our purpose is to create a safe and comfortable environment for both believers and non-believers of Christ as well as individuals seeking to know God — to connect through faith, sport, and community. Athletes In Action ex

name: Highlander Builders Initiative
description:
Highlander Builders Initiative (HBI) is a student organization at UC Riverside that admits technically skilled and creative students to collaborate on ambitious real-world projects. We bring together builders to grow as innovators, while forming lasting relationships through our community.

highlander.builders
summary: Where engineers and creatives at UCR collaborate on ambitious real-world projects

name: Voicing Inclusion Thriving Awareness and Leadership
description:
VITAL is a student-led organization at the University of California, Riverside dedicated to promoting wellness, awareness, and leadership development within the community. Our mission is to educate, empower, and engage students and community members through workshops, outreach initiatives, and collaborative events that foster inclusion, personal growth, and meaningful impact.

VITAL provides opportunities for students to develop leadership skills, participate in community service, and collaborate with campus organizations and local partners to address topics such as mental health, social awareness, and personal development. We prioritize consistency, education, and sustainable partnerships to create lasting positive change for our members and the communities we serve.
summary: VITAL is a student led initiative for wellness and inclusion in the inland empire. Advocating awareness, leadership, and equitable access to knowledge and resources—ensuring that our efforts promote lasting empowerment and strong actions.

name: Scotty's Tutors
description: Scotty's Tutors' mission is to bring a diverse set of experiences and specialties by UCR students to UCR students, who may feel more limited in other tutoring spaces on campus and may see more academic success in a more collaborative environment. Join our discord here: https://discord.com/invite/sx3vTMGyW.
summary: Scotty's Tutors' mission is to bring a diverse set of experiences and specialties by UCR students to UCR students, who may feel more limited in other tutoring spaces on campus and may see more academic success in a more collaborative environment.

name: Figure Skating Club
description: Join us for some fun on the ice! The Figure Skating Club at UCR meets twice a month at Icetown Riverside for recreational skating sessions and social meetups. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced skater, our club is all about enjoying the ice, learning new skills, and connecting with others who love to skate in a relaxed and supportive environment.
summary: Recreational skating at Icetown Riverside — join us twice a month for fun on the ice!

name: Neo-Noir Creatives
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide a space that is specifically structured for students to collaborate and produce their own work for both academic and professional development of Black scholars at UCR interested in the arts ( Theater, Film, Dance, Music, Writing, Spoken Word, etc.). NNC will connect students with similar backgrounds and create more accessible opportunities to advance their careers through networking and more. NNC will strive to unite and welcome Black scholars to the broad field of the arts.
summary: a space that is specifically structured for students to collaborate and produce their own work for both academic and professional development of Black scholars at UCR interested in the arts ( Theater, Film, Dance, Music, Writing, Spoken Word, etc.).

name: Black Students in Education
description:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide a supportive and empowering space specifically structured for the academic, professional, and personal development of Black scholars at UCR who are interested in the field of education. Black Scholars in Education (BSE) seeks to cultivate a community where students can connect with others who share similar backgrounds, experiences, and aspirations. Through mentorship, networking, leadership development, and educational workshops, BSE aims to create more accessible and equitable pathways for Black students pursuing careers in teaching, educational policy, counseling, administration, research, and related fields.

In addition to promoting professional growth, BSE will serve as a platform for dialogue and collaboration on issues that impact Black educators and students within the education system. The organization will encourage members to engage critically with topics such as representation, equity, cultural responsiveness, and systemic barriers in education. By fostering a sense of belonging and purpose, BSE strives to unite, inspire, and prepare the next generation of Black educators and advocates who will transform classrooms, institutions, and communities for the better.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide a space that is specifically structured for the academic and professional development of Black scholars at UCR interested in education. BSE will connect students with similar backgrounds and create

name: Anderson Fintech Club
description:
The Anderson Fintech Club is a student organization at the University of California, Riverside (UCR) dedicated to preparing students for the dynamic intersection of finance, technology, and compliance.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between students and the fintech industry by connecting them with regulators, founders, and financial institutions. We are committed to promoting responsible innovation in digital finance, upholding integrity and respect for regulatory institutions, and advocating for a just and accessible banking experience for all.

The club is open to all UCR students, staff, and faculty. We equip our members through a variety of events, including guest lectures, professional workshops, networking sessions, and case competitions. Our activities are led by an executive board of elected student officers and supported by a dedicated faculty co-founder.
summary: Empowering UCR students to navigate the future of finance & tech. We connect members with industry leaders to foster responsible innovation, integrity, and accessible banking.

name: Caring 4 Children
description: Caring 4 Children (C4C) at UC Riverside is a student-led organization dedicated to making a positive impact in the lives of children throughout Riverside and the Inland Empire. By partnering with local organizations, C4C provides education on child safety, distributes community resources, and raises awareness about preventable forms of child abuse. Chiefly, the club strives to create a safe, supportive, and empathetic network where students, families, and community members can come together to ensure every child grows up in a safe and nurturing environment. Our mission is to spread awareness on the complexities, causes, and impact of child abuse while also providing an environment for students, faculty, and staff to further prevent it.
summary: Caring 4 Children (C4C) at UC Riverside is a student-led organization dedicated to making a positive impact in the lives of children throughout Riverside and the Inland Empire.

name: Women In Neuroendocrinology
description: Our organization is dedicated to advancing research and healthcare in women’s health, with a focus on neuroendocrinology and neurology. We aim to educate students about the unique aspects of the female neuroendocrine system and the neurological mechanisms underlying disorders such as epilepsy. In addition, we strive to raise awareness of the longstanding gaps in women’s neurological care. Through these efforts, we hope to contribute to the development of more comprehensive and effective treatments for all women, including both cisgender and transgender patients. Our three pillars of advancement are Research, Clinical Practice, and Public Health. We will potentially be bringing in guest speakers, having interactive workshops, as well as having volunteering/fundraising events!
summary: An organization dedicated to advocating for women's neuroendocrinological health through discussion of research, clinical practice, and participation in civic engagement.

name: Next Generation Graduates
description: Next Generation Graduates is to empower aspiring professionals to achieve their post-undergraduate goals by providing accessible, interdisciplinary workshops, mentorship, and resources that prepare students for success in graduate and professional programs. from medicine and healthcare to business and beyond. Our mission is to bridge knowledge gaps, foster leadership, and build a supportive community that guides students through every step of the graduate school journey.
summary: Next Generation Graduates empowers students to achieve post-undergraduate goals through accessible workshops, mentorship, and resources that prepare them for success in graduate and professional programs across healthcare, business, and beyond.

name: Y Squared at UCR
description:
We bring yoyoing into the UCR community! Every week we host trick circles and meetups to play with yoyos and learn new tricks on campus. No experience required, be there or be squared! 

https://discord.gg/54Ty4ekFEU
summary: We bring yoyoing into the UCR community! Every week we host trick circles and meetups to play with yoyos and learn new tricks on campus. No experience required, be there or be squared!

name: Creator Club
description: We are currently meeting Thursday's 6:00-7:00 PM in the Rivera Library 209! See you there <3
summary:
Open Minds, Creative Minds, Free Minds

Come to Creator Club to learn how to go viral on social media, build a life long audience, make money through UGC and more! Come to Creator Club to meet some of your favorite online influencers!

name: Highlander Sports Analytics
description: Highlander Sports Analytics (HSA) is a student organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of sports, data science, and statistics. The organization aims to foster a collaborative and inclusive community where students can engage in hands-on research and projects using real-world sports data. Whether interested in athletics, analytics, or both, students can find a community to grow their skills and share their interests.
summary: Highlander Sports Analytics (HSA) is a student organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of sports, data science, and statistics.

name: CoalitionPAC at UCR
summary: CaPAC is a Political Action Committee that seeks to advocate for policies that benefit younger generations and to uplift the standard of living, Civil Liberties, and stop pay gaps, while continuing to pursue policies at the local, state, and national

name: School on Wheels at UCR
summary: School on Wheels is a nonprofit organization that provides free one-on-one tutoring, mentorship, and academic support to children experiencing homelessness with the mission of helping these students succeed despite the challenges of housing insecurity.

name: Rollout@UCR
summary: The gathering of individuals in the shared purpose of enjoying the transformers franchise as well as additional franchises including but not limited to mechs & robots of any type. To discuss the impacts of fictional conflicts that reflect modern society.

name: Somethin' Sweet
summary: Somethin' Sweet at UCR offers current students to be able to learn how to bake while creating connections with other members that will be cherished. It focuses on spreading the love and joy that comes with baking.

name: Desi Chai Society
description: Our purpose is to give South Asian professionals a platform to enlighten us with their stories while being inclusive about the role their identity played. The goal of this club is for students to gain a strong skillset that they are not only able to use within social settings but also professional environments.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to learn about important historical contexts surrounding South Asian culture while including professionalism.

name: Sweet Society at UCR
description: Sweet Society at UCR, is focused on teaching students how to create mind-blowing desserts with the simple ingredients found in their kitchens. We provide educational content focused on proper practices when preparing and serving different baked goods, such as cookies, brownies, cinnamon rolls, etc. Sweet Society at UCR is focused on making all of our recipes available for public use, so that anyone can learn to bake. We also hold baking competitions where students can show off some of their own recipes.
summary: Sweet Society at UCR is a student-run organization focused on increasing students' proficiency at baking by sharing recipes and educational baking content, holding baking competitions, and holding fundraisers. The primary focus of the club is on desserts

name: Bridging Voices in Healthcare
description:
The Bridging Voices in Healthcare club aims to bridge the language barriers prevalent in our healthcare system. Although there have been many improvements, such as translators and more diverse staff, communication is still an issue that needs to be addressed. Patients that do not speak English acknowledge the effort of physicians who try to speak their language. Patients who see their future medical team trying their best to understand them and make a difference will allow them to have more trust in the healthcare system and more likely want to take care of their own health.

We aim to accomplish this mission by teaching our members common and important medical terminology in different languages. We also aim to educate premeds how to pursue medicine by bringing in physician and medical student guest speakers to talk about their journey.
summary: We aim to bridge the language barriers prevalent in our healthcare system by teaching our members important medical terminology. We also aim to educate our members how to pursue medicine by bringing in physician and medical student guest speakers.

name: Reboot @ UCR
description: We provide support and enhance the wellness of students on the University of California, Riverside campus by providing resources, workshops, and activities to help students de-stress and avoid burnout while spreading awareness.
summary: We provide support and enhance the wellness of students on the University of California, Riverside campus by providing resources, workshops, and activities to help students de-stress and avoid burnout while spreading awareness!

name: DKMS at UCR
description: We are an organization dedicated to fighting blood cancer and disorders by creating awareness and recruiting stem cell donors to provide a second chance at life to those in need. We do this by providing registration forms and swab kits so volunteers can sign themselves up to join the potential donor list. We provide all the materials including swab kits and registration materials, and postage as well as mailing/submitting for potential donors, so to sign up is cost free and as simple as possible.
summary: We are an organization dedicated to fighting blood cancer and disorders by creating awareness and recruiting stem cell donors to provide a second chance at life to those in need.

name: Math Club
summary: The goal of the club is to give undergraduates a community with others who enjoy math and to participate in mathematical discussion in an environment that is more relaxed than other mathematical settings.

name: Believer's Loveworld UCR
description: Giving your life a meaning.
summary: BLW UCR is a campus ministry under Christ Embassy. We're dedicated to raising a generation of young men and women who will fulfil God's dream for their lives. Our mission is to take God's divine presence to the University of California, Riverside.

name: Music Undergraduate Student Association
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be for potential and enrolled Music undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in Music and beyond to network and support each other's goals and interests. We’ll strive on being the bridge for career readiness incorporating all our Music Undergraduate scholars pursuing the depths of Music Industry, Music Education and more. Our purpose is to also advocate for all Music Undergraduates by making this organization a safe place where they can bring their concerns and have a voice in their education.
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be for potential and enrolled Music undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in Music and beyond to network and support each other's goals and interests. We’ll strive on being the bridge for career

name: HiddenIEats
summary: HiddenIEats is dedicated to supporting small, family-owned establishments from the Inland Empire to educate and spread awareness to UCR students their impact.

name: Sport Medicine Club at UCR
summary: Our mission is to educate students on the fundamentals of sports medicine, injury prevention, rehabilitation, and performance optimization through workshops, guest speakers, and volunteer opportunities.

name: Karing Kits
description: Our mission is to increase access to feminine hygiene products in nearby areas to homeless women. While these kits are not made exclusively of feminine hygiene products, they are directed at maintaining adequate access to these cleaning products in order to help reduce the amount of avoidable illnesses caused by lack of resources and accessibility. These illnesses include, but are not limited to, toxic shock syndrome, UTIs/RTIs, fertility issues, yeast infections, increased risk of cervical cancer, and bacterial vaginosis. While many of these illnesses are not deadly, they are avoidable when good feminine hygiene is practiced. Most local homeless shelters do not provide basic cleaning supplies for women since these are seen as “nonessential” supplies, in comparison to clothes and food. This is a precarious misconception that endangers the health, comfort, and safety of the women living in these unfavorable conditions. Our goal is to provide the items and support needed to keep these women healthy, clean, and happy while expanding and serving our community.
