summary: Our mission is to increase access to feminine hygiene products in kits to homeless women, which are directed at maintaining adequate access to cleaning products in order to help reduce the amount of avoidable illnesses caused by lack of resources.

name: Royale ESports
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to bring together students who enjoy Clash Royale for friendly competition, strategy sharing, and community events that promote teamwork, fun, and gaming excellence.).

name: Matcha Club
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to create a welcoming space for students to de-stress, study, and connect with others over a shared love of matcha. The club aims to educate members about the origins and cultural significance of matcha, explore

name: Vietnamese International Student Association
summary: A student-run cultural organization dedicated to celebrating heritage and traditions, fostering unity among members, and sharing our culture with the broader campus community through events, performances, and outreach activities.

name: Young Peoples Alliance at UC Riverside
description: While entrenched interests cling onto power, our generation is left with high costs, manipulative corporate practices, and barriers to building a career. We're fighting to give young people a voice. As young people, we've seen firsthand how our generation's interests are sidelined by our political system. Our mission is to empower young people to shape their future, engage with the political sphere, and reclaim the American Dream they were promised. We develop bipartisan policy on the most important yet overlooked youth issues, organize our generation, and advocate for policy change.
summary: As young people, we've seen firsthand how our generation's interests are sidelined by our political system. Our mission is to empower young people to shape their future, engage with the political sphere, and reclaim the American Dream they were promised.

name: Free Enterprise, Entrepreneurship and Networking club
description: FEEN (Future Entrepreneurs and Executives Network) is a student-led organization at the University of California, Riverside, dedicated to cultivating the next generation of innovative entrepreneurs, business leaders, and creative thinkers. The club provides hands-on experiences that bridge product development, design thinking, and business strategy through workshops, guest speakers, pitch sessions, and collaborative projects. FEEN’s goal is to empower students to think critically, act creatively, and build inclusively while learning how to understand product-market fit, user-centered design, and strategic business planning. Rooted in the belief of being “men and women for and with others,” FEEN strives to prepare its members to turn ideas into impactful ventures that drive growth, innovation, and positive change.
summary: Understand product-market fit and how to validate ideas. Learn the principles of user-centered design and prototyping Combine product, design, and strategy into a cohesive business concept.

name: Neurotechnology Society at UC Riverside
summary: Highlanders exploring and innovating at the intersection of technology, neuroscience, medicine, and bioethics.

name: Aprendiendo Juntos
description: Our organization focuses on helping and educating children mostly in underserved communities to teach them the English language. We travel to Mexico and other places, also including the United States, to volunteer in many schools, orphanages, homes, etc. We make sure the children we teach feel that we care for them and are there for them whenever they need and not just view us as an "extra class" but as a place where they feel comfortable and distract themselves for a while. We value humility, respect, compassion and commitment on helping others.
summary: The organization is about focusing on teaching children/teens/adults across the United States and mostly Mexico the English language. Mostly focusing on children in underserved communities. We focus on volunteering and influence others to help.

name: Wallflower Magazine
description: Wallflower is an indie life and style magazine redefining quiet confidence. Each issue blends refined aesthetics with expressive experimentation exploring fashion, culture, and personal expression through the eyes of the community learning to stand out. Wallflower reimagines individuality, challenges norms, and defines what it means to be an icon.
summary: Wallflower is an indie life and style magazine redefining quiet confidence. Each issue blends refined aesthetics with expressive experimentation exploring fashion, culture, and personal expression through the eyes of the community learning to stand out.

name: Mosaic Jewelry Studio
description: Mosaic aims to inform and educate students on the processes used to design and create rings, bracelets, necklaces, and other jewelry pieces. In addition, the organization shall cover proper safety protocols in the jewelry studio. The organization will serve as a supportive platform where jewelry students can connect, share skills, and collaboratively assemble their individual talents into a vibrant mosaic of creativity and design.
summary: The organization will serve as a supportive platform where jewelry students can connect, share skills, and collaboratively assemble their individual talents into a vibrant mosaic of creativity and design.

name: Business Brilliance
description: Business Brilliance is dedicated to connecting ambitious student leaders with peers and industry professionals across diverse fields. Our mission is to create a collaborative community that fosters growth, leadership development, and global networking.
summary: Business Brilliance (BB) connects student leaders with peers and professionals to promote growth, leadership development, and international networking.

name: Rhythm Gaming Club @ UCR
description:
We are the Rhythm Gaming Club @ UCR, a community for students, by students, who love rhythm games of all kinds! From classics such as Dance Dance Revolution and Pump It Up, to modern titles such as maimai, Sound Voltex and Chunithm, to non-arcade titles like osu! and Project Sekai, and many many more, there is something for you here.

 

Our goal here at RGC@UCR is to create a welcoming community where players of all skill levels can connect, improve, and share their passion for rhythm gaming. We host casual meetups, tournaments, and arcade outings! Whether you’re starting out or chasing full combos, our club is the right place to hit buttons to the beat, make new rhythm gamer friends, and enjoy rhythm gaming together! 

 

Make sure to join our discord to talk amongst fellow rhythm gamers, share your scores, and keep up with club updates such as tabling, arcade meetups, meetings, and game nights! 

Discord: https://discord.gg/9AWWcMVnB9
summary: Here at the RGC@UCR, our clubs purpose is to bring together and foster a community of students that enjoy rhythm games. Whether you’re a seasoned pro or a total beginner, our club welcomes everyone regardless of their rhythm game background.

name: Highlander Cards
description: Highlander Cards is here at UCR to create a community within the UCR campus for students to enjoy and learn more about their favorite card games. Games include (but are not limited to) Uno, Texas Hold'em, Pot Limit Omaha, etc. We will teach and discuss in-depth on game theory (including mathematical/statistical optimal way to play the games) as well as host an environment where gaming will be taught to be played responsibly.
summary: Highlander Cards is an organization at the UCR campus focused on building a community amongst students who are interested in learning about card games such as (but not limited to) Uno, Texas Hold'em, and Pot limit Omaha.

name: Wayward Academy: World of Nintendo
description:
This club brings together people who are interested in WAWON in an engaging and welcoming community. Our mission is to create a space where players can connect, share strategies, and grow in both Nintendo games and a community. We believe in teamwork, passion, and supporting each other so we can build a thriving community that continues to grow.

Discord: https://discord.gg/N2XRZZsKTr
summary: A group of people that love Nintendo Games and welcome those who want to play!

name: Competitive Pickleball at UCR
summary: Our club fosters an inclusive pickleball community for health, skill, and lasting friendships. We host competitive events with nearby schools and our mission is guided by inclusivity, sportsmanship, growth & fun—everyone plays, respects, and improves.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Chapter at UCR
summary: The Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) at UCR fosters a supportive and inclusive community, promoting equity, professional growth, and engagement for women and underrepresented genders in mathematics at UC Riverside.

name: Echo
description: Echo – HiFi & Music Listening Club is dedicated to exploring the art and science of sound. We bring together students who appreciate high-fidelity audio and music culture to learn about topics such as frequency response, acoustic design, and headphone or speaker technology. Through regular listening sessions, demos, and open discussions, Echo offers a non-commercial space to experience music and connect with others who share a passion for sound.
summary: Echo is a student-led HiFi and music listening club that explores sound, technology, and culture through shared listening sessions and discussions.

name: Highlander Golf Club at UCR
description: Members of the club, depending on membership status, will golf at the range once a week and play on a course once a week. There will also be special events and meetings that will occur throughout each quarter. Members will be able to rent equipment or bring their own.
summary: Golf Club at UC Riverside. Learn how to golf and have fun doing it!

name: Circle K International
description:
Circle K International (CKI) is a global student lead service organization sponsor by the Kiwanis Interational. It focuses on three main pillars: community service, leadership development, and fellowship. CKI empowers college and university students to serve their communities through various projects, develop leadership skills through their programs, and build friendships with peers locally and internationally. 

 
  • Service: CKI engages in a variety of service projects, both on their campuses and in their local and global communities.
  • Leadership: The organization provides opportunities for leadership development through its self-governing structure, where students elect their own officers to run club activities.
  • Fellowship: CKI aims to create a fun and inclusive environment for students to connect and build lifelong friendships.
  • Affiliation: CKI is the collegiate branch of the Kiwanis International family, which also includes Key Club for high school students and Kiwanis clubs for adults.
  • Motto: CKI's motto is "Live to Serve, Love to Serve," and its mission is to develop college students into a global network of responsible citizens and leaders.
summary: Circle K International (CKI) is a global, student-led collegiate service organization sponsored by Kiwanis International. It focuses on three main pillars: community service, leadership development, and fellowship.

name: Hearts in Harmony
summary: Hello we are Hearts in Harmony. We hope to create harmony in people’s lives through our passion for music, inspiring connection and joy throughout the community in Riverside. We plan to play our instruments and use the power of music to unite everyone.

name: College Working Families Party at UC Riverside
summary: WFP at UCR works to address the increasing disenfranchisement of the United States of America’s emerging working class. We aim to provide a platform that gives young voices the power to advocate for representation in the contemporary political landscape.

name: In Her Words Book Club at UC Riverside
description: In Her Words Book Club believes in education as a medium to shift the cultural atmosphere of UC Riverside by highlighting personal narratives in writing workshops and organizing initiatives to get involved in the community.
summary: A book club that encourages mental wellness and promotes literacy for the youth and impacted communities.

name: Line Dancing Club at UCR
summary: The Line Dancing Club at UCR is a student organization that teaches line dancing, hosts weekly practices, and builds a welcoming community through social events and collaborative activities. All skill levels are encouraged to participate and get involved

name: Highlander Cubing
summary: Our aim to provide a space for people to solve Rubik's cubes and build community.

name: Syrian American Medical Society
description: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) was founded in 1998 in the United States as a professional society to provide networking, educational, cultural, and professional services to medical professionals of Syrian descent. SAMS Foundation, the charitable arm of SAMS, was launched in 2007. Since then, SAMS has become one of the most active and leading medical relief organizations working on the front lines of crisis relief in Syria, neighboring countries, and beyond. SAMS has since implemented an integrated model of essential services that emphasizes priority needs, including the delivery of medical education, training, and financial support for physicians and other healthcare workers inside Syria and in neighboring countries. SAMS has established field hospitals, intensive care units, dental and primary care clinics, birth and newborn care facilities, mobile medical units, and dialysis centers.
summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) started in 1998 as a professional network in the U.S., originally focused on connecting Syrian-descent medical professionals and offering educational and cultural resources. SAMS was founded in 2007.

name: Green Thumb Association
summary:
A social club focused on gardening and botany as a hobby. 
We have opportunities for leadership, learning how to garden, starting a project, and finding other people that love gardening.

name: Mixed Heritage Club
description: There is a club for every ethnicity at Riverside - except those of mixed-race origin. I am seeking to found a club for those of mixed-ethnicity, so that none of us feel excluded from any ethnic clubs that we seek to join. We will host networking events, volunteer for charitable organizations, and create a welcoming environment for all mixed-race people who may feel excluded from other organizations.
summary: A club for those of mixed-race ethnicity, to help us feel like we belong. We will run volunteering events and fundraise for charity, as well as host networking events.

name: American Cancer Society at UCR
summary: Our organization fulfills its mission by spreading awareness about cancer and engaging the UCR community through service, fundraising, and educational events aligned with ACS’s three pillars of advocacy, discovery, and patient support.

name: Students for a National Health Program at UCR
description: Students for a National Health Program (SNaHP) at UCR is dedicated to advancing health equity through education, advocacy, and scholarship in support of a universal, single-payer health care system. Grounded in the belief that health care is a human right, our chapter works to promote patient-centered care, physician autonomy, and health policy that prioritizes public good over profit. We seek to promote a healthcare system committed to improving access, quality, and aﬀordability of care for the medically underserved communities of Inland Southern California and beyond, regardless of patients' socioeconomic status, race, gender, sexual orientation, age, residency status, or disability. Our eﬀorts are aimed at achieving our ultimate goal of repairing our broken health system through the implementation of a single payer healthcare system for all.
summary: The efforts of Students for a National Health Program (SNaHP) at UCR are aimed at achieving our ultimate goal of repairing our broken health system through the implementation of a single payer healthcare system for all.

name: Legally Queer
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be a club specifically designed and catering to queer students at UC Riverside who have an interest in law or in studying queer law and history. We host a variety of events including academic, social, and mock trials to further enhance the experience of our queer pre-law community at UCR. We also connect queer pre-law students to advising resources through guest speaker events and campus partner relationships.
summary: Legally Queer is a space for queer students & allies at UCR who have an interest in law or in studying queer law and history. We host a variety of events including academic, social, and mock trials for students to learn about their legal journey.

name: The Actuarial Science Club
description: The Actuarial Science Club is to create a supportive and collaborative environment for students that are in the major or interested in actuarial science for either a career or just further interest. Our mission is to explore students' knowledge on the subject and explore the profession. We wish to help them through a strong community, group studying, workshops, speaker sessions, and develop them with career-ready skills. We wish to grow our major and build a strong network with certified actuaries that are in the profession.
summary: The Actuarial Science Club is a student organization in the University of California Riverside. This club is aimed for students who are in the major or just interested in Actuarial Science.

name: The Community Initiative
summary: Our club focuses on educating underserved communities about common and growing medical conditions while promoting overall health and wellness. We collaborate with the Riverside Community Center to host biweekly educational sessions led by UCR students an

name: Sports Therapy Society
summary: Sports Therapy Society is a UCR student organization focused on injury prevention, rehabilitation education, and physical wellness through workshops, guest speakers, and hands-on learning for students interested in sports therapy and health fields.

name: El Amor Del Señor
description: El Amor del Señor is a college focused, one week mission trip aimed at empowering and mobilizing students to be a part of the Great Commission on the mission field and on their college campuses.
summary: El Amor del Señor is a college focused, one week mission trip aimed at empowering and mobilizing students to be a part of the Great Commission on the mission field and on their college campuses.

name: Riverside Daikoku
summary: A new student organization centered around the JDM (Japanese) car culture, a prominent subculture especially among younger auto enthusiasts. Sharing the stories behind our cars, and seeing others unique tastes in builds.

name: Joining Undergraduates in Civic Enagement
description: JUICE Club aims to explore how participation in community service and civic engagement not only benefits the community at large, but also strengthens community, builds connections, and promotes a sense of fulfillment and belonging among student volunteers. In alignment with its community values, JUICE Club encourages and supports student members to engage with civic issues they are passionate about.
summary: Create a community at UCR that supports and empowers students to address civic issues they are passionate about through community building and civic engagement.

name: The Sports Club
description:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a welcoming community for students who enjoy

sports of all kinds, including both playing and watching sports. The organization aims to foster

connection and friendship among sports enthusiasts through social events, recreational activities, and

opportunities to bond over professional sports such as the NFL, NBA, MLB, College Sports and other

leagues. The club provides members with a fun and inclusive environment to engage with sports and

build lasting relationships.
summary:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a welcoming community for students who enjoy

sports of all kinds, including both playing and watching sports.

name: Beating in Sync
description:
Beating in Sync is a student-led organization at UC Riverside dedicated to advancing cardiovascular health awareness and education. We strive to empower students and community members through workshops, guest speaker events, and outreach initiatives focused on prevention, lifestyle modification, and early recognition of heart disease.

We collaborate with healthcare professionals and student groups to organize events focused on lifestyle modification, early detection, and long-term prevention. By creating opportunities for students to learn about cardiovascular health and engage in meaningful outreach, BIS fosters a culture of education, advocacy, and compassion for heart wellness across the Inland Empire.
summary: Beating in Sync (BIS) promotes heart health awareness through campus events, education, and outreach. We aim to inspire early prevention and equitable cardiovascular care across the Inland Empire.

name: Women in Film
description: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to establish an open space for female and gender nonconforming voices who are interested in the film industry. We aim to bring support, education, and a collective voice to women and gender minority filmmakers, through workshops, discussions of women in the industry, movie screenings, and offering production opportunities. We welcome anyone regardless of experience. Throughout our time working on student films at UCR, we have noticed a significant lack of women on film sets. This absence is not unique to UCR however, there is a systematic lack of representation and opportunity for women in film and television. Through sharing our experiences and knowledge we strive to create a safe space for individuals to feel heard and welcome in meetings, events, and productions. Our goals are to not only show our resilience in a male-dominated industry, but to achieve our ideas and establish a community of like-minded individuals to support each other throughout our careers. We want everyone to feel inspired and motivated to develop their projects and reach goals they haven’t dreamed of yet.
summary: WIF aims to bring support, education, and a collective voice to women and gender minority filmmakers, through workshops, discussions of women in the industry, movie screenings, and offering production opportunities.

name: Together in Grief
description: Together in Grief is a club dedicated to creating an open dialogue about grief at UCR, providing a supportive space for grieving students to connect and support each other, and educating others on how to support students who are grieving.
summary: Together in Grief is a club dedicated to creating an open dialogue about grief at UCR, providing a supportive space for grieving students to connect and support each other, and educating others on how to support students who are grieving.

name: Project 70mm
description:
Project 70mm is a Student Led Organization at the University of California, Riverside dedicated to inspiring interest and awareness for the beauty of South Asian films in all genres and languages. Our organization's goal is to create an inclusive space for exploring and celebrating cinema through regular screenings, discussions, and themed events.

We meet biweekly, and have plenty of events, snacks, and debates in store! Join our HighlanderLink to receive updates on our meeting times and check out our Instagram @Project70mm
summary: Showcasing films of all genres and languages to create interest and awareness for South Asian film culture

name: Aviation Club at UC Riverside
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to cultivate a passion for flying at UCR. Connect with other students who share a passion for aviation, find mentors who can provide guidance and support, and build lasting friendships with fellow aviators.

name: Pre-Student Osteopathic Medical Association
description:
Pre-SOMA is the undergraduate branch of the Student Osteopathic Medical Association (SOMA). It allows pre-medical students to explore osteopathic medicine, connect with DO students and physicians, and decide if the DO path is right for them. The Pre-SOMA Committee promotes osteopathic medicine, supports aspiring DOs, and provides networking and educational opportunities.
A DO (Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine) is a fully licensed physician who can practice all areas of medicine, including surgery. DOs receive additional training in osteopathic manipulative medicine and focus on whole-person, patient-centered care.
SOMA, founded in 1970, is the largest osteopathic medical student organization with over 15,000 members. It supports osteopathic medical students, represents their voices within the American Osteopathic Association, and helps shape the future of osteopathic medicine. SOMA is student-run at both local and national levels.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to increase the awareness about osteopathic medicine, provide interested students resources and information about osteopathic medicine, and help others in the community through service.

name: NeuroPsychology Club at UCR
description: Build bridges between the neuroscience and psychology field by bringing people interested in the brain’s structure, mental processes, and development, through fun activities and learning experiences.
summary: Build bridges between the neuroscience and psychology field.

name: United Health Pathways
description: United Health Pathways provides members with comprehensive guidance, resources, and opportunities to pursue medical licensing and healthcare certifications such as CPT, EMT, and CNA. Our organization connects students with local hospitals and healthcare institutions to help them secure externships, internships, shadowing experiences, clinical hours, and employment opportunities. Additionally, we are committed to inspiring the next generation of healthcare professionals by engaging in outreach programs with local K–12 students to spark their interest in the medical field. Through workshops, study groups, and networking events, UHP promotes both professional and personal growth while fostering collaboration among students who share a passion for healthcare careers.
summary: United Health Pathways guides students toward medical certifications like CPT, EMT, and CNA, connecting them with hospitals for hands-on experience. We inspire future healthcare professionals through outreach, workshops, and networking opportunities.

name: Special Olympics - UC Riverside Chapter
description:
• We are a campus-based club of the Special Olympics program here at UC Riverside.
  • Our goal is to connect college students with Special Olympics athletes to create an inclusive community. 
  • Club focus:
    • Inclusion: athletes are teammates, and we all work together
    • Continuity: consistent engagement with athletes and dedication to volunteering
    • Campus impact: Educate and involve the college community
summary: This is UCR's campus-based club of the Special Olympics program. Our goal is to connect college students and community members with Special Olympics athletes to create an inclusive community. Our focus is on increasing inclusion and campus impact!

name: Community, Outreach, Resources and Action
description: This mission is about helping underdeveloped communities by providing support, resources, and services through community outreach and volunteering. The organization runs different projects to meet the needs of various communities. It also focuses on empowering students by giving them opportunities to serve others, take initiative, develop leadership skills, and work toward important goals while making a positive impact.
summary: We are about outreach and community service. We focus on underfunded and underepresented communities.

name: Advancing Representation in Medicine & Science
description: The purpose of ARMS is to create a long-term educational pipeline that supports under-resourced and underrepresented students from elementary school through college in accessing pre-medical and health-related career pathways. ARMS aims to provide mentorship, academic guidance, exposure to medical professions, and leadership development through peer advising, community outreach, and professional engagement. The organization values equity, service, academic excellence, mentorship, and community empowerment.
summary: ARMS is a long-term educational pipeline that supports under-resourced and underrepresented students from elementary school through college in pursuing pre-medical and health-related careers by providing mentorship, academic guidance, professional exposu

name: Ace of Cultures
description:
The purpose of Ace of Cultures is to foster an inclusive and welcoming community at the University of California, Riverside by bringing students together through games from diverse cultures around the world. The organization aims to help students feel connected to their own cultural backgrounds while also learning about and appreciating the cultures of others through interactive, strategy-based, and social games.

Ace of Cultures hosts cultural game nights, tournaments, and social events featuring games specific to different regions and traditions, such as card games, board games, and team-based strategy games. Through shared experiences involving gameplay, food, and music, AOC seeks to promote cultural understanding, community-building, and meaningful social connections among students of all backgrounds.
summary: Ace of Cultures builds an inclusive UCR community by connecting students through games from cultures worldwide. Through cultural game nights, tournaments, food, and music, AOC promotes connection, learning, and cultural appreciation.

name: Undergraduate Chemistry Journal and Publication Club
description: The Undergraduate Chemistry Journal and Publication Club aims to prepare students for research through an emphasis on literature search and comprehension. Club meetings involve reading literature, presenting papers, and discussing intradepartmental publications. Although the club is targeted towards students interested in chemical research and graduate school, the club is open to all UCR students across all disciplines.
summary: The Undergraduate Chemistry Journal and Publication Club aims to hone skills in literature search, comprehension, and writing. The goal of the club is to prepare students for research at both the undergraduate and graduate level.

name: Future Founders
description: Future Founders is an organization dedicated to creating a supportive and collaborative environment that encourages entrepreneurial growth. The organization brings together individuals who are interested in business, innovation, and investing, providing a space to exchange ideas, learn from one another, and gain real-world insight. Through discussions, workshops, guest speakers, and hands-on opportunities, members are able to explore business concepts, develop practical and transferable skills, and better understand their personal interests and strengths. Future Founders aims to help members build confidence, expand their professional network, and prepare for future academic, career, and entrepreneurial endeavors.
summary: Future Founders is an organization dedicated to providing an environment that fosters entrepreneurial growth, allowing individuals to explore their business career interests and abilities.

name: Dale Play Club
description: Dale Play is a club that celebrates the rhythm, sound, and soul of Latin music. Our mission is to create a space where students can connect through reggaetón, salsa, bachata, cumbia, merengue, and other Latin genres, not just through dancing and listening, but by exploring their cultural roots, creativity, and community. Dale Play empowers women and allies to take the lead in shaping how Latin music is shared, experienced, and appreciated on campus.
summary: Here at Dale Play, we celebrate the rhythm and spirit of Latin music, creating a space where students connect through reggaetón, salsa, bachata, cumbia, and more. We encourage exploration of culture, creativity, and community.

name: Alternative Investments Group
summary: We educate students in asset management across public and private markets while developing skills in financial modeling, valuation, portfolio construction, and research. Members engage in real-world simulations and connect with industry professionals.

name: Jesus Nights
description: Jesus Nights are worship nights where we create a speace for God to move and encounter us. Whether you believe in God or you're still on the line, you are welcome to join us. There will be a community of believers and amazing worship with moments to share testimonies and verses in between songs. We are believing for a move of God as individuals and as a community and would love if these nights brought encouragement to you in you faith journy.
summary: Jesus Nights are worship nights where we create a space for God to move and encounter us. Whether you believe in God or still on the line, you are welcome! Come find community and enjoy the music, as we praise Jesus and ask him to move in our lives.

name: Sri Lankan Student Association
summary: The Sri Lankan Student Association will build a vibrant community about Sri Lanka. We'll host events and social gatherings to teach and engage students with the heritage. Our goal is to promote diversity and cultural awareness at UCR.

name: Pediatric Hope Project
description: The Pediatric Hope Project provides a free tutoring program connecting K-12th-grade pediatric patients with volunteer college students. The aim is to supply educational support during treatment.
summary: The Pediatric Hope Project provides a free tutoring program connecting K-12th-grade pediatric patients with volunteer college students. The aim is to supply educational support during treatment.

name: The Reach Initiative
description: The Reach Initiative is a student led organization committed to serving underserved communities in Riverside, with a special focus on elderly individuals and people with disabilities. Through meaningful service, advocacy, and strong community partnerships, we transform compassion into real impact. Our members don’t just volunteer, they build genuine connections, restore dignity, and create lasting change. If you’re looking for a club where your involvement truly matters and your service makes a difference, The Reach Initiative is where you belong.
summary: The Reach Initiative is a student led organization serving underserved communities in Riverside, with a focus on the elderly and individuals with disabilities. Through hands on service and advocacy, we build real connections and create lasting impact.

name: LEGO Club
summary: Get together to build Legos or just come to talk!

name: Tink'R Lab
description: The Tink'R Lab offers students opportunities to use maker space equipment to create inspiring projects through innovative workshops! From simple creative endeavors to detailed research, we are open to everyone being able to have a space to create or follow step-by-step workshops that help guide a path towards both creativity and innovative design.
summary: The Tink'R Lab offers students opportunities to use maker space equipment to create inspiring projects through innovative workshops! From simple creative endeavors to detailed research, we are open to everyone being able to have a space to create!

name: Ceramics Club
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to spread knowledge and skills relating to Ceramics and Pottery.
summary: A social club dedicated to spreading information and skills regarding ceramics.

name: Break and Build Engineering
description:
Furthermore, we are creative community of both engineering and non-engineering students that have an interest in how things are built but more importantly how we can break them. Taking things apart and putting them back together, understanding how things are made, restoring old or outdated technology are all things we will be doing along with the breaking and building. 

To learn more, follow us on Instagram @b2engineeringucr and join our discord at https://discord.gg/WvKPXwzDuF
summary: Get experience with fun building activities such as breaking down old technologies and rebuilding them, build a bridge activity using different materials and more!

name: Study Buddies
description: Welcome to Study Buddies! Study buddies is a club that offers students a place to study in a group setting. We aim to improve study habits, decrease burnout, and provide a community for individuals to learn. Join us for a time of weekly study sessions, fun stress relief activities, and build lifelong friendships. Follow us on Instagram @studybuddiesucr!
summary: Study buddies is a club that offers students a place to study in a group setting. We aim to improve study habits, decrease burnout, and provide a community for individuals to learn.

name: Latines in the Mathematical Sciences
description: The purpose of LatMath shall be to encourage students to view the Math Department and the math world as a whole as a space that is friendly, inclusive, and welcoming to all and that is made up of students just like them! Though the name is Latines in the Mathematical Sciences, anyone is more than welcome to join!
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to encourage students to view the Math Department and math world as a whole as a space that is friendly, inclusive, and welcoming to all.

name: Uncensored America at University of California Riverside
description: Uncensored America is a youth-driven free speech organization dedicated to hosting honest and fun conversations with controversial figures to fight censorship and cancel culture. Our goal is to create a free speech culture to make America free and fun again.
summary: Uncensored America is a youth-driven free speech organization dedicated to promoting open dialogues on college campuses.

name: Thrive
description: Our organization is a mental health awareness/community organization for students to connect with. We provide access to resources, host events to build community/support, have guest speakers, and facilitate discussions!
summary: Our mission is to promote a community for UCR School of Business students that raises conversations and builds support around mental health struggles.

name: Project Forget Me Knot at UCR
description:
Project Forget Me Knot is a non-profit student-led organization committed to providing high quality emotional support and care for senior residents of the Inland Empire and other underserved communities in California through volunteer-led recreational, musical, and social interaction-oriented service events. We aim to raise awareness and combat mistreatment while also bringing comfort to individuals residing in senior living and assisted care facilities.

Our volunteering opportunities are perfect for pre-health and pre-med students! If you are interested in learning more, check out our socials @fmkucr on Instagram for a sneak peek of what we're up to. Drop us a DM or email with any questions and we can't wait to see you at our events!
summary: To provide high quality emotional support and care for senior residents of the Inland Empire and other underserved communities in California through volunteer-led recreational, musical, and social interaction-oriented service events.

name: The Serve Collective
description: The purpose of this organization is to empower students to use business knowledge and professional skills to create meaningful impact within the community. We aim to connect students with nonprofit organizations, small businesses, and community initiatives to provide pro-bono support, volunteer service, and strategic solutions that drive positive social change.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower students to use business knowledge and professional skills to create meaningful impact within the community.

name: Women’s Water Polo
description: The Women’s Water Polo Club will be a collegiate club team that trains at UCR and competes in tournaments both within the state and out of state against other collegiate club programs.
summary: The women water polo club will be  collegiate club that trains at UCR and competes within the state and out of state.

name: Zephyra Yoga
description: Zephyra Yoga is an eclectic, somatic‑based movement club blending Yin, Restorative, Somatics, breathwork, gentle Pilates, and intuitive flow. We focus on embodiment, nervous‑system regulation, and intention‑based practices like crystal and emotion‑centered flows.
summary: Zephyra Yoga is an eclectic, somatic‑based movement club blending gentle yoga, breathwork, Pilates, and intuitive flow with intention‑based practices like crystal and emotion‑centered sequences.

name: Master of Public Health Scholars
description: Our mission is to serve and protect vulnerable populations with empathy, inclusiveness, and cultural sensitivity by being open to innovation, flexibility, and transparency. This includes focusing on systemic change through advocacy, infrastructure, and social justice in underserved communities. In doing so, this organization aims to provide resources, opportunities, and support to its students to achieve academic excellence and career development.
summary: This organization shall be to act as an entity that collectively represents the Master of Public Health cohorts.

name: Career Accelerator at UCR
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide students with a welcoming place on campus to explore careers together through peer support, mentorship, and career events. The goal is to make career preparation feel less overwhelming.

name: Project Pulmonary Riverside
description:
This organization is dedicated to advancing firefighter health and safety through awareness, advocacy, and direct service. Its mission includes, but is not limited to, collaborating with municipal and regional fire departments, providing financial and material assistance such as donations, organizing educational and community outreach programs, conducting public awareness campaigns, and advocating for improved protective policies, safety standards, and PPE equipment.

Project Pulmonary Riverside stands with the key values of honor, accountability, service, and collaboration. This organization is committed to maintaining transparency, integrity, and professionalism while serving its community. Through partnerships and community service, PPM Riverside seeks to strengthen the health and well-being of firefighters while promoting improvements in pulmonary healthcare and rehabilitation.
summary: PPM Riverside is a community-based organization established for the purpose of promoting public awareness regarding the harmful effects of carcinogen exposure and other wildfire-related hazards on firefighters and other first-responders.

name: The News at UCR
description: The club is to create a network of individuals in our organization to work together to create a magazine style Newpaper to give to the students of UC Riverside.
summary: The purpose of this organization would be to report different events in the area, to give perspectives from events from our team, and to inform the school of new activities on campus.

name: Global Opportunities Abroad Leaders Initiative
summary: The GOALies program is focused on organizing student volunteers who wish to give back to their community, fostering cultural connections formed through experiences around the world, and giving students a resource to learn more about studying abroad.
